description:
Lecrae - Reconstruction World Tour NZ | Wellington, Lecrae - Reconstruction World Tour NZ | Wellington
4x GRAMMY® Award-winning artist, author, actor and activist Lecrae has formally announced his headlining tour, ‘Reconstruction World Tour,’ on the heels of unveiling the release date for his tenth studio album, Reconstruction, due August 22nd.

Tickets will be available starting with an artist presale beginning Wed, July 30 at 10am local time. Additional presales will run throughout the week ahead of the general on sale beginning Fri, August 1 at 10am local at lecrae.com/tour.

Making his anticipated return to the stage, the tour will mark one-year since Lecrae’s last outing in 2024 for ‘The Final Church Clothes Tour!’ The fan favorite is slated to perform songs from his decorated catalog, as well as music from his upcoming album Reconstruction.

Beyond the music, Lecrae has fostered a direct relationship with his local community. Last Monday (July 21), the Grammy-winner held a back-to-school drive in the heart of Atlanta alongside nonprofit City Takers. With over 100+ people in attendance, both fans and residents alike joined Lecrae in assembling backpacks filled with essential school supplies for students across the city.

As a special treat to fans, Lecrae revealed the features on Reconstruction with a one-of-a-kind mailer that went out to 1,000 of his most active supporters. Each mailer included a handwritten note from the Reach Records founder, and a QR code linking to The Workshop – an online community where fans can access early music, content, and engage to earn points for incentives.

Continuing his mission of bringing Christ to the culture, he now gears up to make international headlines with his renewed stage presence, ultimately setting the tone for an unstoppable and intentional run. The announcement also heralds the arrival of Lecrae’s forthcoming album Reconstruction – his first solo project since 2022’s Church Clothes 4 LP.

description:
Wilson Blackley Plays The Shed (The Remain Tour with Oscar LaDell), Wilson Blackley The Remain Tour with Oscar LaDell.
Wilson Blackley is an Australian multi-instrumentalist and producer currently based in Melbourne.
Born and raised in Poowong (Victoria), Wilson was surrounded by music from an early age. At just 2 years old he started playing the drums along to his mother’s music collection consisting of old soul, funk and r&b from the 60’s and 70’s. He started learning the guitar as soon as he could, and had the habit of making anything he could get his hands on into an instrument. Growing up far from the city instilled a strong sense of curiosity and work ethic in him from a young age, leading him to produce his first batch of demos in his teenage years. A stand-out among these demos was the song ‘Home’
‘Home’ found its way to Sam Teskey (The Teskey Brothers) who would go on to co-produce the song alongside Wilson when he was just 18.
Following the release of ‘Home’, he went on to sell out The Gasometer and play a string of festival slots alongside a variety of artists including The Teskey Brothers and Boy and Bear.
At this time, Wilson moved to St Kilda and in the heart of Melbourne began collaborating with Sensible J (Sampa The Great, Remi, Immy Owusu) on his debut EP ‘Material Plane P1’ which was released to critical acclaim in August 2022. After that, he co-produced Oscar LaDell’s EP ‘No Blue’ and subsequently embarked on a co-headline tour of Aotearoa and Australia with LaDell. After a three year break from releasing music, Wilson Blackley will return with his new song ‘Wired’ that bridges the sonics from his first EP with what's to come.
Also appearing Oscar LaDell hot off a US tour.
Doors open 8pm for an 8.30pm start.
label: Music & Concerts

description:
Alexandr Misko - The International Guitar Sensation LIVE at Old St Paul’s | Wellington, The international guitar sensation brings his groundbreaking stage show to New Zealand shores for the very first time!
May 31 at Old St Paul's, Wellington
2:30pm doors / 3:00pm show
Seated Concert
FAMILY FRIENDLY with $20 tickets for under 18s
Hailed as “the future of guitar” (Total Guitar), Misko is a globally renowned virtuoso known for his jaw-dropping technique, emotional depth, and genre-defying style. Fusing classical precision with elements of jazz, rock, and modern fingerstyle, his performances are both technically astonishing and deeply moving.
Misko’s self-taught, boundary-pushing approach has captivated millions online, earning him a massive global following and praise from some of the world’s top music publications. With lightning-fast fingerpicking, percussive tapping, and masterful arrangements, he transforms a single acoustic guitar into an entire musical universe — live and without loops.
A must-see for fans of instrumental brilliance, next-level guitar playing, and world-class musical storytelling!
WHAT THE PRESS SAY
“Misko is the future of guitar.” — Total Guitar
“A one-of-a-kind performance that will leave you speechless.” — The Guardian
“Alexandr Misko redefines guitar playing with every note he plays.” — MusicRadar
“A mesmerizing display of technical brilliance and raw emotion.” — Rolling Stone “A true guitar prodigy.” — Guitar World
“An artist who combines virtuosic technique with deep musical expression.” — Guitar Player
Tickets are VERY limited for these intimate concerts — don’t miss out on seeing live music at its finest!
Old St Paul’s is a stunning example of Gothic Revival architecture, constructed entirely from native timbers and consecrated in 1866. Its soaring wooden arches, stained glass windows, and rich acoustics make it a sought-after venue for a variety of events. The church offers seating for up to 460 guests, with facilities including a grand piano, organ, and a bar licence. While on-site catering isn't available, nearby venues such as Loaves & Fishes Hall, the Royal Society of New Zealand, and the Thistle Inn provide excellent options for receptions and gatherings. Located at 34 Mulgrave Street in Thorndon, Old St Paul’s is easily accessible by public transport and offers a serene setting for weddings, concerts, memorials, and other significant events.

description:
Antü Mārama, The Undercurrent presents Antu Marama!
Wellington band Antü Mārama brings you the vibrant evocative folk music of Latin America, with traditional tunes and original compositions. From Chile, the UK, New Zealand and the Canary Islands the group's ethos is represented by the name - Antü is the Mapudungún word for sun, while Mārama is the Te reo Māori word for Moon.
The group combines both the life-giving energy and vitality of the sun with the deeper emotions and more reflective energy of the Moon. Antü Mārama is a synthesis of the group's diverse backgrounds and musical experiences, united by a love of playing music, and a desire to share the rich folk tradition of Latin America with a local audience.

description:
Violet Hirst Summer Tour: Hataitai Bowling Club Wellington, Contemporary singer-songwriter Violet Hirst tours small Aotearoa venues this summer. An evening of layered vocals, familiar melodies and unique songwriting supported by Madeleine Dove.
Born and raised in Tāhuna Queenstown, Violet's musical journey began the day she was born: opening night of her mother's musical Kitchens. Her songs and live performance embody the theatrics and ballads that surrounded her childhood. Gaining comparisons to Aldous Harding and Julia Jacklin, Hirst’s songs combine hypnotic vocal melodies with meditative rhythms and poignant lyrics from a young woman. After touring the USA as a support musician for Flying Nun rockers Office Dog, Hirst is back on the road to share her own new works.
Her debut album Donegal was recorded in Central Otago with De Stevens (Roundhead Studios, Marlin's Dreaming) and Reuben Scott (Saltwater Criminals, Molly Payton). The album received a Student Radio nomination for Best Album, charting on both SRN and Radioscope charts. Followed with a nationwide tour and sold-out shows, Hirst is excited to return to the road for the first time since then. Joined by Madeleine Dove (vocals, keys) the duo will perform new contemporary meditations and vocal experiments.
“(Donegal) is brimming with the confidence of an artist fully inhabiting their own sound; wistful and slightly heartbroken, but unencumbered by expectation." - RNZ
“Evident to everyone present was how special that rainy night was, how flawless, nuanced and emotive Hirst’s singing and songwriting. The real deal, with the crowd standing in the rain outside afterwards, not ready to leave, raving.” - NZ Musician
https://violethirst.bandcamp.com/
label: Music & Concerts

description:
Sir Dave Dobbyn: Selected Songs - Wellington, Due to huge demand across already announced tour dates for Aotearoa’s treasured musical voice, Sir Dave Dobbyn and his Selected Songs regional tour, the run will continue into 2026 with today’s newly announced show at Kirikiriroa, Hamilton’s Waikato Regional Theatre on Saturday, 24 January 2026.

Sir Dave Dobbyn will also be stopping at The Opera House in Pōneke, Wellington on Friday October 31st, before heading to Ahuriri, Napier’s Municipal Theatre on Tuesday November 4th. He’ll then head to Papaioea, Palmerston North at Regent on Broadway on Wednesday November 5th, Ōtepoti, Dunedin’s Town Hall on Friday November 7th, followed by Sunday November 9th’s show at Ōtautahi, Christchurch in the new venue at the Christchurch Town Hall.

With over 45 years as a recording artist, Sir Dave Dobbyn has shaped the sound of Kiwi music, penning the soundtrack to generations of New Zealand lives. From iconic anthems to an extraordinary catalogue of deeper cuts, Dobbyn’s set promises an unforgettable journey through one of the nation’s most storied songwriting legacies.

Diagnosed with Parkinson’s Disease in 2022, Dobbyn declared it “business as usual” – and continues to inspire with his unmistakable voice and enduring presence on stage. Recognised with a Knighthood in 2021 for his services to music, Dobbyn remains one of New Zealand’s most revered cultural figures.

If the popularity of Sir Dave Dobbyn’s Auckland Town Hall show in June is anything to go by, audiences will want to grab tickets as soon as possible before they sell out.
label: Music & Concerts

description:
Soundscape Series: Rameka Tamaki, People can expect a level of artistry and mastery over the guitar. Rameka will be playing a selection of Classical Guitar pieces which include places like Spain, Japan, South America, and also his own compositions. These will range from Baroque, to Romantic, and Modern, with elements of different genres that encompass different cultures and time periods from around the world.

Rameka Tamaki (Tūhoe, Maniapoto, Samoan, Tongan, Irish) is a Classical Guitarist from Aotearoa, New Zealand who has performed in festivals and shows around the world. With a passion for the guitar and Māori culture he completed his Bachelor of Music with First Class Honours at The New Zealand School of Music, Victoria University. His performances include ASIA Topa, The New Zealand Opera Company, Performance Space at Carriageworks Sydney, the Matariki Ahi Kā Festival, Gardens Magic, and Los Angeles for Kapa Haka.

About the Mostly Music Soundscape Series

A curated series of intimate live music experiences showcasing the depth, texture and emotion of sound. Each performance invites you to immerse yourself in a new soundscape, informed by genre, sonic craft, and music as an art form. Designed for music lovers who want more than a background soundtrack, this series celebrates live performance with a diverse range of acclaimed performers.

Saturday 21 June — Nick Granville Trio

description:
Davey Griffiths & Celine Filbee Live at the RSC, Davey Griffiths & Celine Filbee Live at the RSC
Koha entry call 04 299 1658 to book a table. Davey and Celine have crafted their diverse set around shared love of the blues, a funky groove and beautiful ballads, including originals and covers, also encompassing americana and roots music. You can expect to laugh at their humorous take on some songs, cry at the beautiful ballads they partner on and be toe tapping to their groove. Davey’s acute ear and sound experience allow him to distil the very essence of a song and craft arrangements showcasing the underlying groove inherent in his playing. His voice, reserved, yet soulful and resonant along with his sympathetic guitar chops, elevate Celine’s and other songs to new and sometimes surprising places. Celine provides rhythm guitar and a powerhouse vocal performance, she can own the biggest banger, feel the suffering of a blues standard or dial it right back to expose all the sentiment in a ballad.

description:
Pip Payne with Richard Klein and Al Norman, PIP PAYNE, a regular player and founder of Capital Blues is back with another musical variation. Pip has had many artistic guises over the years which shows not only his versatility but also his depth of the music. This time he is joined by two outstanding musicians bring you some Louisiana blues and cajun.
RICHARD KLEIN is a high energy singing fiddler born in Orange, New Jersey. In his early teens he discovered folk and roots music through listening to the likes of Woody Guthrie, Pete Seeger and The New Lost City Ramblers. Travelling to Cape Breton Island in mid 1970s sparked the interest for Celtic music that later brought Richard to travel, live, work and busk extensively through Ireland and the British Isles. A chance meeting with a gang of Swedish fiddlers in Shetland in 1978 introduced another important influence in his fiddle style. But it was being properly introduced to Cajun music later that same year in France that had the most profound impact on his music. Resident in Wellington since 1997, Richard is best known for his collaboration with the Le Blanc Brothers Cajun Band (Melbourne) and the geographically challenged Kiwi Americana band. T-Bone. In 2023 he relinquished his American citizenship. The accent, however, remains.
AL NORMAN has been on the Wellington scene for a while. Early blues club jams and bands, on squeeze box and keys. For this gig it’s the squeeze box or snare. A multi-instrumentalist with a great feel for the groove. He’s been part of a number of well-established groups including Smokeshop, Warratahs, Laura Collins Band and still plays with the Rag Poets and, less regularly, The Hardcore Troubadours. He has also played a number of gigs offshore and toured NZ extensively.
Come and join us for another fine session featuring some virtuoso playing with a twist on the blues.
Tickets are $20 or $10 for members, with bar discounts for paid-up club membership.
label: Music & Concerts

description:

description:
Cairo Knife Fight + Sheeps Live at Meow, After ten years, Cairo Knife Fight is finally returning to New Zealand for a limited series of intimate shows. Known for their powerful live performances and genre-bending sound, the duo
is set to bring their unmistakable energy back to local stages.
Since their last performances in New Zealand, CKF has continued to push musical boundaries, earning Billboard chart success with tracks like “Churn” and “Nothing at All.”
Their latest EP, Dream Season, showcases the band's ever-evolving sound—fusing intricate live looping,
intense rhythms, and ambient textures. This homecoming is more than a tour—it’s a full-circle moment for one of NZ’s most innovative exports.
Joining CKF at Meow will be Wellington based Sheeps. Described as Art Rock for the end times, Sheeps are definitely the right band at the right time to partner CKF in 2025.
Don’t miss the return of Cairo Knife Fight. It’s been a long time coming.

Give yourself the gift of stillness as you surrender to the healing vibrations of:

description:
Wesley Huff | Leaders Lunch | Wellington, Wesley Huff | Leaders Lunch | Wellington
This September Wesley Huff is joining Thinking Matters for the Truth Matters Tour in Dunedin, Christchurch, Wellington and full-day Conference in Auckland.

While in Wellington, he will be doing his only Christian Leaders Lunch.

Proceedings
Doors will open 11:15 am
11:30 am - the event will begin with a short powhiri/mihi whakatau
11:50 am - Kai
12:20 pm - Wes will present his talk and do q and a
1:30 pm - Conclusion of event
About Wesley Huff
Wesley Huff was born in Multan, Pakistan, and spent a portion of his childhood in the Middle East. After being diagnosed with a rare neurological condition at the age of eleven that left him paralyzed from the waist down, Wes experienced a miraculous recovery for which the doctors themselves had no medical explanation. This experience, along with a great deal of study and soul searching in his later teen years, solidified his interest in faith and in engaging the beliefs and worldview perspectives of others. He spent his high school years in Northern Ontario and currently lives in Toronto, Canada, with his wife and three children.

Wesley is currently the Central Canada Director for Apologetics Canada and has been a guest on a variety of public platforms, from The Joe Rogan Experience and Piers Morgan Uncensored, to Apologia Radio and Daily Dose of Wisdom. He has participated in numerous public dialogues, debates, and interfaith events on issues of belief and religion globally.

He holds a BA in sociology from York University, a Masters of Theological Studies from Tyndale University, and is currently doing a PhD in New Testament at the University of Toronto’s Wycliffe College.

Come and join with us on Sunday the 1st February for a group ceremonial gathering in Paraparaumu.

We will explore sacred conscious sounding practices to balance and align the energy system, sing healing chants and songs from different traditions, and explore guided vocal improvisation for freedom, upliftment and joy!

There will be guided journal prompts around the energy of the Solstice and the whole session will finish with a full sound healing.

Everyone is welcome and there is no need for any particular preparation or pre existing skill.

So looking forward to gathering for the Lammas Full Moon. Come and join with us to invoke and celebrate the energies of balance, creation, abundance and celebration at this profound time of year.

This session will explore singing, chanting, sounding and conscious voice empowerment practices. We will also explore energy practices like Qigong and meditation. All within a ceremonial framework.

Everyone is welcome. Limited spaces. Get in touch soon

You are warmly invited to book to join this powerful sound and voice ceremony which will work with Spirit, the forces of Nature and Bryony's skill and expertise in creating sound for growth and transformation.

This is a special opportunity to tune into the sacred energies and potentialities of this time: opening us up to balance, healing, nourishment, new growth and positive change.

Bryony will invoke and work with the healing power of the supportive energies of Spirit, ancestors and our highest Selves. Bryony's has an extensive background experience with Spirit, the Earth forces and sacred lands; these forces are central to her practice and Self.

She will lead you firstly in a process of meditation, conscious sounding, toning and singing healing chants.

This use of our voices to co-create a sacred group sound is uplifting and transformative and will support you in your process of healing, releasing and visioning.

There will be time for guided journaling and intention setting.

The second section of the workshop will be a focused and dedicated sound bath which will bring balance and deep relaxation, into a sense of stillness and peace. There can be a sense of gratitude and appreciation.

Both the conscious use of the voice and the sound healing have many benefits, which are verified by science and which can effect us positively on the physical, mental, emotional, energetic and spiritual levels. A document which has more information about these benefits can be provided on request.

description:
Astrology: More Than Just Sun Signs, Astrology: More Than Just Sun Signs
Astrology is a beautiful and powerful way to journey inward and discover more about yourself. It helps us find the light within and recognize that same light in others.
In this workshop, we will open the doorway into the world of astrology. We will explore what astrology truly is, and discover the differences between traditional and modern Western approaches. Together, we’ll enter the natal chart to understand its key elements and the wisdom they hold.
We will uncover how all twelve zodiac signs live within each of us, reminding us that no soul is ever defined by a single sign. Along the way, you will also gain insight into why two people with the same Sun sign can feel so different. By the end of our time together, you will understand the meaning of the seven traditional planets, the twelve zodiac signs, and the twelve astrological houses—and see how they interlace to reveal the hidden patterns and the unique story of your soul.
Whether you are just beginning your journey with astrology, or already walk with some knowledge, this workshop will offer you guidance, clarity, and a deeper sense of connection—to yourself, to others, and to the stars.
Plus - your ticket includes a plant-based dinner & dessert!
Whats on the menu?!
Jackfruit Rendang
Fragrant Rice
Satay Skewer
Gado Gado Salad w/ Peanut Sauce
Banana Cake w/ Caramel Sauce
Hot Carob Drink
Meet Jelena, your host
Jelena has been passionate about astrology and was fortunate to begin her studies under the guidance of one of New Zealand’s most respected astrologers, who helped her start on the right path. From the very beginning, she has pursued her learning independently, drawing extensively from recommended books. Last year, she completed a course in Horary Astrology through the School of Traditional Astrology (STA). She is currently continuing her studies through a one-on-one course with a traditional astrology teacher in the UK while also receiving mentorship from an astrologer based in Perth, Australia.

Breathwork results in certain specific physiological changes in the body and also can result in intense physical and emotional release.

As a precaution, the following conditions are contraindicated in certain breathwork practices.

It is crucial that you inform your breathwork practitioner if any of the following conditions are relevant to you:

• Pregnancy (at any stage)

• Severe PTSD or trauma

• Currently using recreational drugs

• Recent surgery

• Taking any medication that alters brain chemistry like anti-anxiety,anti-depressant, ADD, OCD medications, etc.

•Detached Retina

•Glaucoma

•Kidney disease

•High Blood Pressure

•Cardiovascular disease, including angina, previous heart attack or stroke
•Diagnosis of aneurysm in the brain or abdomen

•Uncontrolled thyroid conditions

•Diabetes

•Severe Asthma— for mild to moderate asthma, we can discuss but you must bring your inhaler to the session.

• Epilepsy

• History of Seizures

• Prior diagnosis of bipolar disorder, schizophrenia or previous psychiatric condition.

•Any other medical, psychiatric or physical conditions which would impair or affect the ability to engage in any activities that involve deep physical and/or emotional release.

Email or message Tara to let her know what these are and she’ll let you know if she can offer adjustments so you can attend.

description:
Gong Bath at Vogelmorn Hall, $20 -tickets via link: https://events.humanitix.com/gong-bath-at.../tickets
*Limited door sales for $25*
*Please bring a yoga mat, pillow, eye mask and blanket - or whatever it is you need to feel most warm and comfortable *
Discover the transformative effects of sound therapy with this 1-hour gong bath experience.

During this session, participants will be surrounded by the resonant vibrations of gongs, carefully designed to induce deep states of relaxation and calm. As you settle into the experience, allow the soothing tones to wash over you, releasing tension and promoting a sense of inner peace.

Scientific studies have highlighted the benefits of sound therapy, particularly gong baths, in reducing stress and promoting overall well-being. The rhythmic patterns and overtones of the gongs can help entrain brainwaves, leading to a meditative state and improved mental clarity.

Beyond the immediate effects, many participants report experiencing lasting benefits such as improved sleep, reduced anxiety, and enhanced mood following a gong bath session. By immersing yourself in the healing vibrations of the gongs, you can tap into a deeper sense of relaxation and rejuvenation.

Whether you're looking to unwind after a long day or explore new avenues of self-care, this 1-hour gong bath offers a unique opportunity to reconnect with yourself and find balance amidst the hustle and bustle of daily life. There will be cups of tea available afterwards to help us integrate and ground.

**THIS EVENT IS RESTRICTED TO 40 PEOPLE ONLY**

Disclaimer
Gong baths are safe and supportive for most people. However, please consult your doctor or mental health professional before attending if you:
-have a pacemaker or other implanted medical device
-live with epilepsy or a sound-sensitive condition
-are in the first trimester of pregnancy
-are experiencing severe or unmanaged mental health challenges (such as psychosis, schizophrenia, or PTSD)
If you have recent injuries, surgeries, or other health concerns, let me know so we can make sure you’re comfortable.
Everyone responds differently to sound. If at any point during the session you feel overwhelmed or uncomfortable, you are welcome to sit up, move gently, or step outside. Your wellbeing always comes first.

description:
Ecstatic Dance 🌷 Solstice Celebration, Ecstatic Dance 🌷 Solstice Celebration
💫 Move. Release. Connect. 💫

🌟✨ Ecstatic Dance Wellington – Summer Solstice Celebration ✨🌟

With DJ Codaraan & Kimberley Jade @ St Peters Village Hall, Paekākāriki.

Kimba Jade & Bernie Cruise are thrilled to be hosting a special Summer Solstice Ecstatic Dance — a night to move, release, and celebrate the turning of the seasons. 🎉

☀️ Summer Solstice Celebration
As we gather close to the longest day of the year, we honour the light, abundance, and life that the Sun brings. The Solstice marks a time of illumination, gratitude, and renewal — a powerful moment to celebrate all that has blossomed, and to release what no longer serves as we prepare to turn toward the next cycle.

Together, we’ll weave a simple solstice honouring and meditation before the dance to ground into our intentions and connection with the elements. We’ll close the evening with chai, chocolate, and community — a warm space to share reflections, smiles, and heart connection.

🎶 What is Ecstatic Dance?
A freeform, drug- and alcohol-free dance experience where you move however your body wants. The music flows like a wave — starting slow, rising to ecstatic peaks, then softening into stillness. It’s therapeutic, uplifting, and a powerful way to connect with yourself.

Please note, this Ecstatic Dance is not verbally guided — we let the music guide you 😊. Dance freely, how your body moves you.

🎶 The Dance Floor
Guided by DJ Codaraan (Bernie Cruise), expect an immersive journey through deep bass, tribal rhythms, and global soundscapes that awaken both body and spirit. His music will invite you to move and connect with your body, expect an enticing groove-focused journey that builds slowly, allowing you to find your flow at your pace.

🎶 The Sound System
Here at Ecstatic Dance Wellington, we believe in using a phat sound system with lots of bass for our dances. Expect an ultra-vibey, deeply immersive experience!

🗓 Event Details
📍 St Peters Village Hall, Paekākāriki
🕖 7:00pm – Opening Circle & Solstice Meditation
🕢 7:15pm – Ecstatic Dance
🕘 8:45pm – Closing Circle
🕘 9:00pm – Chai, Chocolate & Community

🎟 Tickets
$25 online – Humanitix link
https://events.humanitix.com/ecstatic-dance-solstice-celebration

$30 cash on the door

If you would like to come but are unwaged or struggling, please send us a message directly — we’d love to help.

☀️ Extra Info
Doors open at 6:45pm for you to settle in and connect before we begin. We’ll start promptly at 7pm.

❤️ What to Bring

Water bottle

Loose, comfy clothes

Warm layers for afterwards

Pillow or blanket if you wish

A friend!

🌟 Guidelines

Drug & alcohol free event

Silence on the dance floor

No phones/cameras on the dance floor

Please reframe from wearing strong scents

Respect the space & each other 😊

✨ Come celebrate the Summer Solstice through music, movement, and magic — a night to honour the light within and around us. ✨

we are here for you, for ourselves and for it all. Come all Jah one - individual in our togetherness, together in our uniqueness. Our personal messages and mauri and our collective vibrations and spontaneous moments of authentic expression. From vulnerability to mana, from stillness to ecstasy, the troughs and the crests, the eggs and the fluids. the dub and the deep, the groove pocket and the light tasty treats. well prepped to be enjoyed by all but also unprepped to be raw and pure. digested in reciprocity, fed by those sentients present souls, a gift. Its a Sunday funday, a gathering to reset the weekly cycle, go for a run, a swim, a sleep, take a breathe and connect with te taiao - do some mahi, something good.... for you or someone else and then you have the option of enriching this collective experience by gracing us with your essence, as we sink into the hum of the universe together within the stories of MOON 1. every day is but a little life. See you there, here and now. Hanno, Simon and Nikau x

description:
USUI REIKI LEVEL TWO IN KAPITI with Heartsong, USUI REIKI LEVEL TWO IN KAPITI with Heartsong
Level Two Usui Reiki is a significant development on from Level One. Get in touch to enquire. You can study Level Two with me if you had your Level One attunement elsewhere.

This TWO DAY course offers 10 hours of in person training, a full manual, certificate of completion, a follow up Zoom call and ongoing support. Maximum of 6 participants to enable close support and interaction.

Level 2 involves a new powerful attunement and learning new techniques such as Distance Reiki, sacred reiki symbols, and preparing for working on people you may not know. You can start charging for your Reiki services after completing level two.

We learn about three Reiki symbols and practice drawing them. By Day 2 it is expected that you have memorized how to draw Level 2 Symbols (I teach with a gap between day 1&2 so this is very achievable).

Becoming competent at distance healing is a significant priority of Level 2 training and we will have plenty of practice doing this. Distance healing is practiced on people and animals. Basic business set up is discussed.

Advanced Practitioner Level Reiki is a great tool if you want to feel centered in your life, it helps to balance and align our energy bodies, and can cause positive changes in many areas of our lives. Reiki can be used to help heal a wide number of conditions and to initiate and support positive life changes. Further details on enquiry. Reiki Level 2 opens you to a deeper channel of healing energy and enables you to offer healing professionally.

Dates are specially chosen to be auspicious in relation to the moon and cosmic energies

Saturday FEBRUARY 21ST, 10.am-4.30pm

Saturday MARCH 14TH, 10am-4.30pm

The gap in between the two training days allows you to receive support as you experience the 21 day 'cleanse' and integration period after the Reiki attunement - and also enable time to work on developing and practicing the material learnt in the first days training.

Total of 12 hours tuition time plus some at home practice/study required.

Light simple shared lunches are provided - you will need to bring a contribution.

Herbal teas and light snacks are also provided.

Location: Paraparaumu Memorial Hall, Corner of Aorangi and Tutanekai Streets, Paraparaumu

Pre-enrollment questionnaire -

Investment:

£555 for full TWO day course, training, all support materials including manual, certificate, light healthy lunch, refreshments and ongoing support. Plus extra hours online in follow up Zoom call on 21st day.

Pay in Full Here - https://buy.stripe.com/9B628q2W4ahTghqdcKew81V

A non-refundable $155 deposit secures your place - book here: https://buy.stripe.com/eVqcN4bsA89LaX6b4Cew81S

There is a payment plan available - contact me

Bryony is a Usui Reiki Master Teacher and she has 27 years healing experience. She loves teaching this precious, versatile healing skill and it gives her great pleasure to offer this training in Lancaster

Bryony says:
Reiki has had a powerful positive influence on my life, helping me to heal and change mindset, physical and emotional conditions, and to see life in a new light. Reiki helped with my recovery from Chronic Fatigue syndrome and Depression ❤ I will share this with you in this course and help you open your life to the positive transformation that Reiki can bring.

If you are interested in coming on this course, please contact me by email at to receive a questionnaire to answer which gives me a sense of your interest, previous experience of Reiki healing and conditions I should be aware of.

Cancellation Policy:

If Heartsong Healing needs to cancel the course all payments are refunded. If you withdraw from the course before 14 days before start date, your payment will be refunded minus the 55 deposit, kept as an admin fee. If you withdraw within 14 -7 days 50% of your payment will be refunded, 7-3 days 75% of your payment will be refunded, 3 days no refund. If you need to withdraw, you may be entitled to a discount on future bookings, at our discretion.

description:
A Journey Through the Chakras Workshop - Journey 1- the Root Chakra, A Kundalini Yoga and Gong Bath workshop to provide grounding, safety and stability

In 2026, I’ll be guiding a journey through the chakras — a series of workshops designed to support balance, awareness, and nervous system regulation throughout the year.

The Root Chakra Journey is the first step in this series and focuses on creating a strong, steady foundation from which everything else can grow.

The Root Chakra (Muladhara) is the base of our energy system. It governs our sense of safety, stability, belonging, and connection to the physical world — home, body, trust, finances, and basic needs. By strengthening this energetic centre, we cultivate resilience, stability, and a deeper sense of being held and supported in life. When it’s out of balance, we may experience anxiety, restlessness, fatigue, or a sense of disconnection.

This workshop is an invitation to slow down, reconnect with your body, and strengthen your inner foundation through movement, breath, sound, and rest.

- Kundalini Yoga - to awaken and stabilise the root chakra: 1.30pm – 3.00pm

- Break: Herbal tea, healthy snack & oracle cards

- Gong Bath - a restorative practice to settle the body and mind: 3.30pm – 4.30pm

** Come for one session or enjoy the full workshop experience.

** Open to all levels – no experience with yoga or sound healing needed.

** Bring an open heart, a yoga mat, blanket, water bottle and anything you need to feel cozy + supported.

Investment: $35 Yoga | $30 Gong Bath | $55 Both

*** 3 workshop special: $150 to attend both the Yoga and the Gong Bath sessions for Chakra Journeys 1 - 3 (being held on 21 Feb, 3 May and 31 May).

Lay down strong roots, regulate your nervous system, and begin a year-long journey of energy awareness and self-connection.

https://events.humanitix.com/yoga-sound-journey-with-shared-vegetarian-food

🌸 Yoga & Sound Bath with Shared Kai 🌸
✨ Reconnect, Restore, and Nourish ✨
🌸 Join us in the sanctuary of our beautiful home in Karori for a unique and profound healing journey. This immersion is designed to release stress, anxiety, and physical tension, guiding you back to a state of deep peace through breathwork, sound healing, meditation, and restorative yoga.
🌸 Experience a gentle yoga flow with Pam, specifically curated to assist in the release of stuck energy and emotions. Together, we work to heal core emotional wounds, trauma, and self-sabotaging patterns in a safe, held space.
🌸 Transition into an intuitive Sound Journey Integration. Using the resonant frequencies of crystal singing bowls, medicine drums, Koshi chimes, and more, we support the release of excess energy while opening you to powerful insights and profound relaxation.
🌸 We work with the sacred plant medicines of Ceremonial Cacao and Blue Lotus. These gentle allies support the reconnection to your sacred heart, relaxing the mind and allowing you to go deeper into your healing and inner guidance.
🌸 To ground our journey, we gather for shared vegetarian Kai and stories around the fire. It is a time for community, connection, and integration within the warmth of our home.
✨ The Benefits of Our Journey ✨
🌸 Release suppressed emotions and heal core emotional wounds
🌸 Reduce stress and anxiety while regulating your nervous system
🌸 Experience deep inner peace, balance, and mental clarity
🌸 Release limiting beliefs, habits, and unhealthy patterns
🌸 Increase your natural energy levels and motivation
🌸 Reconnect deeply with your authentic self
✨ Event Details ✨

🌸 Investment: $45 per person
🌸 Intimate Setting: Strictly limited to seven spaces

description:
Immersive Yoga: a Journey Through Chakra Energy, Immersive Yoga: a Journey Through Chakra Energy
It’s more than yoga — it’s an experience.
Immerse yourself in the radiant world of chakras with this transformative yoga event designed to awaken and balance your inner energy. Blending traditional yoga with modern immersive visuals and sound, this session takes you on a sensory journey from the root to the crown.
Aromatherapy and soundscapes will harmonize with each chakra as you move through your practice, supporting a deep sense of alignment and flow.
Throughout the session, the space will come alive with dynamic color projections — grounding reds, creative oranges, golden yellows, soft greens, tranquil blues, intuitive indigos, and luminous violets. Created in collaboration with a visual artist, these shifting hues mirror the energy of each chakra, deepening your awareness and connection to self.
Feel your energy awaken as movement, breath, and color merge into a meditative rhythm — a deeply relaxing and revitalizing experience for body and mind.
Plus, your evening includes a nourishing plant-based dinner and dessert, a delicious way to conclude your journey.
Meet Nicola, your teacher
The essence of Namaste Nicola’s teaching is soulful, gently guiding you through the yoga experience to a deeper connection with yourself.
She has over 20 years of teaching in Australia and New Zealand, and over 300 hours of training with International lead trainers, and forever expanding her knowledge. The yoga philosophy, the poses, the movement now interweaves through her entire being, thinking, relationships with others and herself. Yoga isn’t something she does, it is who she is, and it is a joy to guide others in their hatha or yin practice.

description:
Harmonic Sound Journey at Space with Claudio SOLD OUT!, We invite you to a special evening of Sacred Harmonic Sound with multi-instrumentalist Claudio Escutia. This immersive sound journey offers a deeply restorative experience that invites you to settle into stillness, reconnect with the heart, and explore a sense of inner spaciousness through vibration and tone.
Claudio skilfully layers the sounds of gongs, crystal and Tibetan singing bowls, Native American flutes, and other traditional instruments, enhanced through high-quality audio equipment so the sound can be both heard and felt throughout your body.
Harmonic sound and vibration offer the nervous system a chance to truly rest. In this state of relaxation, the thinking mind softens, and we find connection to citta — the heart-mind. From this place, silence arises naturally through the vehicle of sound.
This experience is open to everyone. No previous yoga or meditation practice is needed.
What to bring:
You are welcome to bring your own yoga mat, though studio mats are also available. We will provide bolsters, blankets, and eye pillows for your comfort.
Booking is essential — spaces are limited and this event often fills quickly.
About Claudio
Claudio is a multi-instrumentalist musician originally from Mexico City and now based in Wellington. Born into a family of musicians, he began playing music at a young age and later studied classical guitar. His journey led him to Montreal, where he explored jazz, world music, and spiritual sound traditions.
Extensive travels in Asia and Europe deepened his connection with Indian music and yogic philosophy. He studied Tibetan singing bowls in Kathmandu and Varanasi, and has since worked with a wide range of instruments including the sitar, esraj, bansuri, harmonium, flutes, gongs, and voice.
Claudio’s work blends technical skill, deep presence, and a profound respect for the healing power of sound.
Learn more or listen to Claudio’s music here:
SoundCloud https://soundcloud.com/claudioescutiasounds
Instagram https://www.instagram.com/claudio_yoga_sound_healing
Facebook
https://www.facebook.com/soundhealingyogamusicclaudio

description:
VOCAL REBIRTH RETREAT, VOCAL REBIRTH RETREAT
LIMITED PLACES REMAINING - BOOK NOW!!!

** Are you looking for a new, empowered sense of your voice? **

** Are you keen to dive deep into your energy and your connectio with yourself? **

You are warmly invited to join this unique FOUR DAY Heartsong Healing weekend HIGH-VIBTATIONAL retreat in a beautiful venue on the Kapiti Coast

Come, explore and celebrate the powerful energies of Vocal Rebirth together with other women, led by international Voice expert Bryony Rogers, originally from the UK now living in Kapiti.

This nurturing and deeply enjoyable retreat is a fabulous opportunity to dive deep into powerful Voice Empowerment, Singing, Conscious Sounding, Song work, Energy Healing and Visioning - all in peaceful, beautiful natural surroundings, and within a framework of mid-summer celebrations.

Also included are daily Yoga Sessions, Yin Yoga and Sound Healing, Qigong, Cacao Ceremony, Meditation, an Extra special Sauna experience and Nature Connection.

This retreat is open to any woman of any level of confidence and skill with her voice. The work will support those who are not confident with their voice and will also give new skills and tools to more confident singers and speakers.

Empower yourself through exploring deeply uplifting and energizing practices which enable you to connect with your authentic voice in an embodied way!

The powerful and transformative Voice practices will be held within the context of ritual, meditation and profound nature connection - including a healing cacao ceremony.

All these practices have a positive effect on the physical, emotional, mental and spiritual senses and are a great stress-relieving energizer!!

There will also be time and space to rest, journal, to be in nature in an unstructured way, to integrate the powerful energies of the Voice work, and to receive healing.

Look at the retreat website on https://www.songofawakening.com/vocal-rebirth-retreat

All of this wonder will be taught in a held safely facilitated space by Bryony from Heartsong Healing. Bryony has been working with the Voice and Energy in a dedicated conscious way for 27 years, after these practices helped her heal from chronic fatigue syndrome in the 1990s.

Bryony says:
** 'I have seen enormous positive change in the people I work with. My work has developed immensely over the past five years and I can truly say that it is transformational.' **

You can see many positive testimonials on her Heartsong Healing Facebook and Google reviews page and on her website https://www.heartsonghealing.life

** Thank you, I loved every minute of it! Your teachings have been life-changing. My voice is becoming more empowered and I'm excited about what my future will bring' ** Alexandra Xena

Bryony says - ** I can't wait to gather for a deep sacred time with you in this deeply beautiful location and to share powerful healing and uplifting energy work with you. **

This retreat will be held at the quality accommodation of Strathean Retreat Centre - including extremely comfortable hotel standard beds - and with exceptional, quality vegetarian food.

This Retreat is open to all women - please make contact to receive the participants questionnaire.

This retreat will include high quality vegetarian food, comfortable accommodation, expert training, nurturing support, great company and deep heartfelt connection and group healing, as well as a number of valuable resources.

Here is the website with all booking information and also with some wonderful video testimonials from past participants of Bryony's retreats - https://www.songofawakening.com/vocal-rebirth-retreat

The investment for the Retreat is all-inclusive of quality accommodation, high quality food, all facilitation, expert training, special facilities, resources, sauna, preparation group call and follow up Zoom call.

SECURE YOUR PLACE WITH ONLY A DEPOSIT PAYMENT on the website - https://www.songofawakening.com/vocal-rebirth-retreat/

It will be possible to book a 121 voice coaching (online) or an energy healing session (distance or in person) with Bryony to take place before or after the retreat

Be in touch by phone or on messenger or whatsapp or book in a video conversation here - https://calendly.com/bryonyheartsong/free-consult

To fill in the participants questionnaire - click here -

If Heartsong Healing needs to cancel the Retreat all payments are fully refunded. 100% deposit refund for cancellation 60+ days before retreat start date. 50% deposit refund for cancellation 30-59 days before retreat start date. 0% deposit refund for cancellation 0-29 days before retreat start date.

description:
EYA Presents: The Bible and the Sacraments Bible Study, What are the Sacraments? Where do they come from? What do they mean? And why are they so central to our Catholic Faith?
In The Bible and the Sacraments, you’ll discover the deeply biblical roots of the Seven Sacraments and how they are more than mere Church rituals, but streams of life flowing from the living Body of Christ.
Throughout this study, you’ll discover how God has always dealt with His people through “sacraments,” and how the sacraments of the Old Testament find their ultimate fulfillment in the Seven Sacraments instituted by Christ and celebrated in the Catholic Church to this day.
Eucharistia Young Adults (EYA) introduces our second Bible study for 2025! This eleven-lesson Bible study by St Paul Center will delve into the beauty of the sacraments of the Catholic Church.
Please bring your Bible, journal, pen - and of course, your lovely self! Bring your friends, co-workers, family members, the more the merrier!

description:
Qi Gong, Qi Gong
Qigong has both psychological and physical components and involves the regulation of the mind, breath, and body’s movement and posture.
In most forms of qigong:
Breath is slow, long, and deep. Breath patterns may switch from abdominal breathing to breathing combined with speech sounds.
Movements are typically gentle and smooth, aimed for relaxation.
Mind regulation includes focusing one’s attention and visualization.
Dynamic (active) qigong techniques primarily focus on body movements, especially movements of the whole body or arms and legs. Meditative (passive) qigong techniques can be practiced in any posture that can be maintained over time and involve breath and mind exercises, with almost no body movement.
Wear loose, comfortable clothing and bring a bottle of water.
[This is a physical activity that involves an element of risk.  While all possible measures are taken to prevent accidents or injury, please note that participation is at your own risk]. 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

description:
French Film Festival - Maigret and the Dead Lover, Following the break-out success of 2025 hit The Stolen Painting, acclaimed writer/director Pascal Bonitzer brings his unique perspective and panache to Simenon’s cherished Parisian detective - a French counterpart to Agatha Christie’s Poirot - as he attempts to solve the murder of a diplomat with a long-held secret.

Inspector Maigret (four-time César nominee Denis Podalydès, in a tremendous performance) is urgently summoned to the Quai d’Orsay: a former ambassador has been found shot dead by his long-serving housekeeper (Anne Alvaro, The Taste of Others). But rather than contact the police, she has followed diplomatic protocols by first informing the Ministry of Foreign Affairs. Forewarned that he will find a series of salacious letters - and cautioned to be discrete - Maigret heads to the victim’s opulent apartment. He finds no forensic evidence at the crime scene, but certainly, the letters prove startling - secret, decades-spanning correspondence between the ambassador and the Princess of Vuynes, whose own husband, by strange coincidence, has also just died…

Bonitzer, renowned for an intuitive and empathetic focus, finds a perfect match with a protagonist who relies on quiet observation. He delivers an absorbing, wholly compelling mystery that also serves as a thoughtful examination of modern life’s key concerns: hierarchy, aging, and the very different worlds that money creates. Best of all, the twist ending is… a killer.

description:
Toitu: Visual Sovereignty film screening, Toi Tū Toi Ora: Contemporary Māori Art was the largest Māori exhibition in the history of Auckland Art Gallery and attracted attendance levels not seen by the gallery since 1989. Chelsea Winstanley’s documentary follows the journey of curator Nigel Borell as he navigates the constraints between institutional authority and Māori self-determination.
Chelsea Winstanley’s documentary follows the journey of curator Nigel Borell as he navigates the constraints between institutional authority and Māori self-determination. Borell’s curatorial vision is clear – Māori art must be authored, not simply advocated for. For the artists, this represents a chance to be seen on their own terms and is an initiative that ultimately leads some to the global stage of the Venice Biennale. 
But as the scale of the exhibition grows, so does the institutional resistance. The result is a reckoning: whose story is being told, and who holds the pen? With many of the artworks exploring the legacy of colonialism, the making of the exhibition itself becomes a living reflection of that very struggle. Laying bare behind-the-scenes conversations rarely seen on screen, TOITŪ Visual Sovereignty lifts the curtain on the inner workings of an institution and examines the cost of visibility without control – and the quiet power of walking away. — Heperi Mita 
The screening will be followed by a Q and A with Director Chelsea Winstanley.

description:
British and Irish Film Festival - A Room with a View, A Room With a View captured the attention of the world upon its release, bringing the novel by E.M. Forster to dazzling life in the Florentine countryside and in the well-appointed homes of the English Edwardian upper classes. A comedy of manners with a quick wit and impeccable comic timing, A Room With A View is also a portrait of the quiet solitude that lies beneath Forster’s characters, and of the need for human connection in a world of rigid convention.

The young Englishwoman Lucy Honeychurch (played by Helena Bonham Carter), arrives in Florence on a Baedecker-style grand tour with her aunt Charlotte Bartlett (Maggie Smith). Through a series of events involving English expatriates Miss Eleanor Lavish, an unflappable novelist (Judi Dench), and the Emersons, a free-thinking father and son (played by Denholm Elliot and Julian Sands), Lucy’s life is changed forever under a loggia in Florence and in the Tuscan countryside.

Ivory’s delicate and playful direction spirits us from an adventure in the back alleys of Florence, lost with Dench and Smith, to the lace-parasolled rigidity of English lawn parties. Shot on location in and around Florence (including unforgettable scenes in the Piazza della Signoria and at Giotto’s frescoes in Santa Croce), A Room With A View made stars not only of Bonham Carter, Day Lewis and Sands, but of the Tuscan landscapes and Puccini arias (as sung by Kiri Te Kanawa) featured throughout.

Director
James Ivory

Producer
Ismail Merchant

Cast
Maggie Smith, Helena Bonham Carter, Denholm Elliot, Julian Sands
label: Film & Media

description:
British and Irish Film Festival - Gerry Adams: A Ballymurphy Man, - WINNER: Best International Documentary, Galway Film Fleadh -

Irish republican leader Gerry Adams is one of the most controversial political leaders and visionaries of our time, having led the people of the North of Ireland from conflict to peace.

Adams was a critical voice in the decision taken independently by the IRA to lay down their arms after their twenty-five-year war against the British. Imprisoned and shot, he was demonised and censored by dominant media as a subversive and a terrorist.

Ultimately, the British and their allies were forced to recognize his legitimacy and negotiate with him - and his party Sinn Féin - the Irish peace accord known as ‘The Good Friday Agreement’.

Today, Adams supports the next generations on their peaceful and inclusive path toward Irish unity.

An intensely private man, protecting his family from the public eye, Adams tell his story for the first time in this eye-opening documentary. Interwoven with his words, a wealth of imagery of what is one of the most documented conflicts of our time gives an insight into Adams’ world - relaxed, informal, and uncensored.
Director
Trisha Ziff

description:
Spin- A Multiformat Movie Marathon, What this is:
The event is a mystery movie marathon where each film plays on a different home media format over 12 hours from 10am to 10pm. There are three distinct sections, times are approximate. The event is R18, nothing is overly controversial but one film is a horror movie rated R18 for violence and nudity. The line up is subject to change but here is an indication:
Film:
Standard 8mm film - 10 minutes
Super 8mm film - 20 minutes
16mm film -90 minute urban adventure
Analog Video:
Betamax - 90 minute horror
VHS - 90 minute action
Laserdisc- 100 minute scifi action 

Digital Video:
VCD- 100 minutes comedy
DVD - 100 minutes ???
HD-DVD TBC
The film will be played on film projectors, the analog video on a CRT video projector (all going well) and the digital video on a digital video projector. All in either mono or glorious 2.0 stereo sound. Most projection is on a 2 meter wide screen.
There will be a half hour break between the analog and digital video sections. And smaller breaks during some format changeovers.
Seating provided will largely be camping chairs, if you want to bring your own chair you can- and there is a reduced ticket price option. This is intended for normal sized chairs, a camping chair, an office chair etc, not a huge armchair. There is limited space where you could bring your own beanbag up front, but you may be staring up at a sharp angle.

What this isn’t:
This is not polished professional event, it's a little bit improvised. We’re not screening in a regular cinema, we’re screening at Valhalla who are very kindly giving us a dark versatile space to work in. Technical issues may arise and we will keep on rolling, discarding formats and entire films in the line up if we can’t get them running. Some things have back ups, some don’t. No refunds, your ticket price is directly funding the event, and the event is an experiment. You can leave at any time, but please don’t yo-yo in and out every few minutes.
The presentation of the films is experimental, but the films for the most part are narrative drama films, not overly inaccessible.
Valhalla is a licensed venue so please do not bring in alcohol- If you do I will ask you to leave- with no refund! That would be awkward for both of us!

Dress up is encouraged and we'll have prizes for Best Dressed on the night!
“… brings the metal, drowns viewers in spectacularly sloppy violence, and does so with a mischievous smile.” – Matt Donato, Daily Dread
-- Brodie is washed-up, broke, and stuck in small-town purgatory. But when he learns that ‘NoizeQuest’, a battle-of-the-bands competition is coming to his town, he sees a shot at redemption – and maybe even a second chance with Medina. One problem: his drummer is missing an arm, and the rest of his band is, well… dead. Luckily, Brodie still has the pages of The Black Hymn, so raising his bandmates from the great beyond is just a riff away. But with every chord comes blood, guts, and the kind of apocalyptic chaos only DEATHGASM can unleash. --
Directed by Jason Lei Howden, DEATHGASM II: GOREMAGEDDON stars Milo Cawthorne, Kimberley Crossman, Joshua Blake.

description:
British and Irish Film Festival - Christy, Seventeen-year-old Christy is at a crossroads. He’s just been thrown out of his pleasant suburban foster home and moved in with his estranged older brother Shane. As far as Shane is concerned this is a temporary arrangement, but Christy begins to feel at home on Cork’s working class north side. As he makes friends and begins to let the community in, he also reconnects with his past through his seemingly more corrupting extended family, despite Shane’s efforts to steer him away from this crowd.

Shane wants something better for Christy at any cost – even if it means he has to push him away. After so many years apart, the brothers need to now reconcile their turbulent past whilst deciding what the future looks like.

Christy draws inspiration from the lived experiences of the young people involved, assembling much of its ensemble cast from the Kabin Studio in Cork, a non-profit community space that has grown out of a love for hip hop and art.

Winner of The Grand Prix at this year’s Berlin International Film Festival, as well as Best Irish Film at the Galway Film Fleadh, Christy is a punchy drama not to be missed.
Director
Brendan Canty
Cast
Danny Power, Diarmuid Noyes, Emma Willis, Cara Cullen

description:
NZIFF: Sentimental Value, This film may touch on the familiar theme of how artists draw from their own lives, but Renate Reinsve and Stellan Skarsgard bring incredible tenderness to a story that is ultimately about what children and parents never say to one another — and whether those lifelong silences can ever be broken.
Collaborating again with frequent co-writer Eskil Vogt (now also a successful director), Trier fashions a nuanced look at both a troubled family and a dysfunctional film industry. (Gustav’s comeback project will be distributed through Netflix, and the divisive streamer is the target of a couple of the screenplay’s better barbs.) Much of Sentimental Value takes place in the Borg family’s home, and an efficient flashback, framed as an essay Nora wrote as a girl, explains how they have lived in the house for generations… That slightly fading family home becomes an apt metaphor for the weathered bond Nora, Agnes and Gustav share, although Reinsve’s fragile performance articulates the festering resentment she feels toward her absent father.
Trier’s third picture to screen at Cannes, after 2021’s The Worst Person In The World’s breakout lead Reinsve won Best Actress, the reunion of director and star will entice arthouse crowds… and those seeking a smart, thoughtful tearjerker should be pleased with this sentimental but farfrom-mawkish film.” — Tom Grierson, Screen Daily

Join us for a rare and intimate evening with one of Aotearoa’s most celebrated performers. As Circa marks its 50th anniversary, we are honoured to welcome Sir Sam Neill back to the theatre where he first appeared in 1976 in Juno and the Paycock.

In conversation with broadcaster and writer Ian Fraser, Sir Sam will reflect on his extraordinary career spanning more than 150 film, television and documentary roles; his early life in Christchurch; his deep commitment to environmental advocacy; and his enduring connection to Aotearoa.

This special one-night-only event is a fundraiser for the Theatre Artists Charitable Trust (TACT), supporting the next generation of theatre-makers to build sustainable careers and continue creating world-class work in Wellington.

description:
Te Rā o Waitangi - Waitangi Day at the National Library, Join us this Te Rā o Waitangi and learn about the hītori (histories) of the 1840 Te Tiriti o Waitangi.

Can you tell which Te Tiriti sheets are made from animal skin? Can you find Hōne Heke’s signature? Did rangatira (chiefs) in Te-Whanganui-a-Tara sign Te Tiriti?

There will be fun activities for tamariki (children), rangatahi (youth), and pakeke (adults) throughout the day.

Join a reorua (bilingual) tour to learn more about Te Tiriti, brush up your whakahua te reo Māori (pronunciation), sing waiata, or add your own tohu (mark) to the collective tiriti canvas. Watch film footage compiled by Ngā Taonga Sound & Vision or test your Te Tiriti knowledge with a quiz.

From 9am to 4pm we will have taonga that relate to the signing of Te Tiriti o Waitangi on display in the Alexander Turnbull Library.

HOME Café will be open until 2pm, and City Gallery Wellington will be open until 4pm.

description:
Dear Gentlemen (Colombia), Dear Gentlemen (Colombia)
Director: Patricia Castañeda -

Genre: Drama

About this Event

Dear Gentlemen brings to light the little-known sufraggist movement in Colombia. Set in 1954, the film follows Esmeralda Arboleda, a fiercely intelligent leader who sparks a revolution in a society deeply rooted in patriarchal traditions - a revolution that culminates in a fierce and decisve debate. A women group embarks on a historic struggle to bring the issue of women's right to vote before the National Constituent Assembly. With bold marches, radio appearances, and a brilliant media strategy that puts them on the front page, they achieve their goal, but the real challenge begins during the debate, where they face fierce opposition. Esmeralda Arboleda must endure personal attacks from the assembly members, and just as she is about to speak, she receives a heartbreaking call: her son has suffered a serious accident. She is torn between her duty as a mother and her commitment to the cause, with an unfavorable outcome. Although all seems to be lost, her perseverance leads to a historic victory when women's suffrage is finally approved, marking a turning point in Colombia's history.

description:
Visual Takes: An evening with Getty Images (Wellington), Visual Takes: An evening with Getty Images
You know the story you want to tell. Getty Images will help you find the content to bring it to life.
After a successful event in Auckland, we are bringing this kaupapa down to Pōneke! Come along for an insider's look at Getty Images' comprehensive suite of services designed for directors, producers, art departments, researchers, and creative professionals across the industry. Whether it’s scripted or factual, Getty Images helps find the right content (fast), access archival gems or fresh contemporary visuals, navigate licensing and clearance, negotiating and pricing, and much more.
Our panelists are:
Arran Birchenough For over 25 years, Arran has been working with TV and Film producers in NZ, Australia, and APAC, and has been based in NZ for the last 20 years. Arran is responsible for the teams at Getty Images that support producers in NZ and across APAC with the range of Getty Images services and support, including free access to content to support getting funded, free research including from the TVNZ, BBC, NBC and ITN archives, advice on clearance, copyright, legal and licensing, and developing offerings to meet the needs of scripted and unscripted producers alike.
Sarah Bailey-Harper from Tenzing, The Luminaries and Sparticus joins to bring perspective from the art, set design, costume and textile departments of scripted productions, sharing her experience and knowledge when using creative content in pre-production and production.
Ruth Korver is an experienced producer who has worked across the full spectrum of content. Her production company Useful Media focuses mainly on factual content. Ruth has produced a number of TV series, documentaries and feature films for TV and cinema. Her most recent work includes feature documentary The Stolen Children of Aotearoa for Awa Films and premium factual series Hua Parakore - Fixing our broken food systems for Storybox. Ruth was a senior producer at creative agency Storybox for eight years and in that time worked on a number of commercial campaigns and visitor experiences.
Date: Wednesday 15th October
Location: Wētā Workshop Group Space, 129 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington 6022, New Zealand
Time: 6:15pm
We look forward to a relaxed, insightful evening and discussing how Getty Images can support WIFT members with their upcoming/current projects.
Snacks and drinks will be provided.
Free to WIFT members and $20 for non-members.

description:
NZIFF: Sex, Returning from last year’s Festival to screen alongside the rest of his Sex Dreams Love trilogy, Dag Johan Haugerud’s comic drama takes a candid and refreshing look at modern gender roles.

There has hardly been any investigation on contemporary male identity and sexuality as candid, insightful and hilarious as Norwegian auteur Dag Johan Haugerud’s Sex. After winning all major laurels in Nordic cinema with his impressive fresco Beware of Children (2019), the acclaimed director and author embarkson an Oslo-set trilogy simply called Sex Dreams Love.

The first instalment opens on one of the most tantalising and unexpected preludes in recent cinema. Two chimney sweeps frankly open up to each other on a coffee break in their office: the first reveals that he dreamt of being intensely looked at by none other than David Bowie, who was inspecting him as if he was a woman; the other confesses that he recently accepted the sexual advances of a male customer who asked him to have sex with him. But both men are “straight” and married to women. Here, the deft tone of the film and Haugerud’s rules of the game are immediately set.

A fervent admirer of Eric Rohmer’s thickly scripted skirmishes of love and ethics, the Norwegian writer-director infuses irony, depth and compassion in each of his elegantly composed tableaux depicting how the two men cope with the turmoil these experiences bring to their relationships with their partners and family, but most of all with themselves. Featuring some of the brightest and funniest dialogues of 2024, Sex is a real eye-opener. — Paolo Bertolin

description:
NZIFF: The Teacher Who Promised the Sea, A progressive teacher brings new methods to a village in Burgos on the eve of the Spanish Civil War, whilst in present day Catalonia a woman searches for answers as to the whereabouts of her great-grandfather’s remains.

Catalan filmmaker Patricia Font takes us to 1930s Spain by way of real-life educator Antonio Benaiges, assigned to teach in the small village of Bañuelos de Bureba. Intertwined with these flashback sequences are scenes from present day Catalonia and Burgos where Ariadna, the granddaughter of one of Antonio's pupils, seeks to locate ancestral remains at the mass graves of La Pedraja.

Antonio’s leftist leanings and critiques of the new Francoist government anger the more conservative townsfolk, particularly the town mayor. This is despite his daughter being one of the pupils benefiting most from the free expression encouraged by Antonio’s Freinet method teachings. Meanwhile, Ariadna teeters between hope and despair in her search for closure – a human vessel embodying the plight of a nation still wrestling with the consequences of a brutal conflict.

The closing credits remark that the remains of 12,000 people have been exhumed across the country. Like Ariadna, the film attempts to seek answers from the horrors of a war that took place almost a century ago – one driven by political sentiments that feel unsettlingly familiar as some of today’s most powerful nations shift towards fascism. — Matt Bloomfield

description:
British and Irish Film Festival - Beat the Lotto, - OFFICIAL SELECTION: Dublin International Film Festival 2025 -

Beat the Lotto is a rip-roaring tale about the mathematician who, along with a ragtag syndicate, plotted to fix Ireland’s National Lottery in 1992, captivating the nation and sparking rabid debate.

In Ireland, the public became obsessed when the National Lottery arrived, none more so than mathematician and avid stamp-collector Stefan Klincewicz. Using mathematical analysis, he reckoned the system could be beaten if he could just gather enough people to form a syndicate to buy every single ticket combination – and front up all the cash, of course.

Director Ross Whitaker fashions this unbelievable true story into a gripping thriller, mirroring the tension felt by the public as the lottery authorities caught wind of the plan.

With insightful interviews and fantastic archival footage, Beat the Lotto is a look back at Ireland in the 1990s and a compelling tale of one man’s attempt to outsmart the system.
Director
Ross Whitaker

description:
Grey Not Blue: Depression and suicide in older people - Te Whanganui-a-Tara - Wellington (GNB2026/07), Take the opportunity to learn more about suicide in older people, the forgotten population in mental wellbeing and suicide prevention
There are some suicide risk factors that are particular to older people yet rarely are included in suicide risk assessment tools nor covered in suicide prevention training.
Many general population suicide prevention and mental wellbeing campaigns do not or inadequately address the unique contributing factors to depression and suicide in older people.
Studies indicate that the aged care sector is underprepared, the workforce under skilled and there lacks leadership to champion effective prevention strategies and promoting wellbeing in older people.
It is critical that those working with older people are:
knowledgeable about the latest research and understanding of suicide in older people
confident and competent in responding to the depressed or suicidal older person
Suicide in the over-65 age group is more prevalent than commonly understood. Recent statistics indicate a rising trend in suicide among the elderly. Often under-reported, depression and suicide are significant mental health concerns for this demographic.
An emerging mental health concern for Retirement Villages and Aged Care Facilities
As the baby boomer generation ages, many will enjoy reasonable physical health. However mental health issues particularly depression will become more prevalent. This is not just a concern for primary care or aged care facilities but requires serious consideration by management and staff in retirement villages. A study of suicide locations in New Zealand found that aged care facilities and retirement villages were the most common places where older people killed themselves. This has implications for family, staff and other residents.
Older People Suicide vs Assisted Dying
An understanding of the distinction between assisted dying and suicidality in older people is crucial for those working with older people. The workshop will cover the complex interrelationship of factors the between suicidality and assisted dying.
TAYLORMADE TRAINING AND CONSULTING IS PROUD TO BE AN AGE CONCERN DIGNITY CHAMPION - TAKING A STAND AGAINST AGEISM & PROMOTING POSITIVE AGEING

description:
Men's Circle: Authentic Conversations, Men’s Circle: Authentic Conversations

In a world where men are often expected to have everything figured out, this gathering offers a space to be genuine, not perfect.

This is a free event, with an optional plant-based dinner available for $15. While there’s no obligation to stay for dinner, sharing a meal together offers a meaningful way to strengthen connections. Andre and Sri Jiva co-host this circle with a shared passion for creating authentic, supportive spaces where men can connect deeply—offering this gathering simply because they believe in its importance.

The Men’s Circle provides a safe and nurturing environment where men can share openly, express their feelings, and encourage personal growth—free from societal pressures to be stoic or self-reliant. Through honest sharing and collective support, participants build resilience, deepen self-awareness, and strengthen their sense of identity and purpose.

Structure of the Circle

Gathering in a Circle: Participants sit in a circle to symbolize equality and unity.

A short meditation or moment of silence may be included to help everyone arrive and connect.

Talking Object: A sharing stick or object is placed in the center. Only the person holding the object may speak, ensuring uninterrupted sharing.

Open Sharing: Anyone may choose to share while holding the object. Sharing is optional, and passing is always respected.

Active Listening: While one person shares, others listen attentively without speaking, interrupting, or offering advice. The focus is on witnessing and being present.

No Judgments or Advice: Participants refrain from judging or offering unsolicited advice, creating a safe space for authentic expression.

Closing: The circle may conclude with a group reflection, a moment of gratitude, or a simple acknowledgment of the shared experience.

Circle Agreements

Supportive Environment: We are here to uplift and support one another, fostering trust and brotherhood.

Focus on the Present: Share what's alive for you now, focusing on your emotions and experiences to deepen emotional awareness.

Use "I" Statements: Speak from your personal perspective using "I" statements to promote authenticity and responsibility. Avoid generalizations like "we" or "us."

No Judgment or fixing: Refrain from judging others sharing or offering unsolicited advice, ensuring a safe and non-critical space.

Confidentiality: What is shared in the circle stays in the circle. You may discuss your own experience outside the group, but keep others' details private.

Meet Andre and Sri Jiva, your hosts

Andre has been immersed in men’s work since the age of 13, when he first experienced a rite of passage in Golden Bay. That formative experience sparked a lifelong connection to circle work, which he later deepened by returning as a mentor. A multi-instrumental musician, poet, and pranayama (breathwork) facilitator, Andre also brings his passion for ecstatic dance, philosophy, and authentic connection into his offerings. He is deeply inspired by the question of what it means to be human—how we can more fully experience our aliveness, our depth, and reconnecting with the beauty of life together.

Since childhood, Sri Jiva has been fascinated by meaning, purpose, and authentic life fulfilment. This, among other things, led him to join a monastery at the age of 19. After years of monastic training, he chose to marry and pursue a career. He felt called to use his monastic experience to help others understand themselves, and to find wisdom, meaning, and purpose in their own lives. Inspired by this calling, he went to university to study counselling. Now, Sri Jiva and his wife live near Wellington, where he works with a small team of counsellors, supporting clients individually and in groups.

description:
Men's Health and Aging, Men's Health & Aging: Take Charge of Your Future

Join us for a no-nonsense, straight-talking session on men’s health and aging. Discover how to stay strong, sharp, and in control as the years go by. It's time to break the silence and tackle the issues that matter most—your energy, vitality, and longevity. Don’t let aging slow you down. Step up, connect with other men, and take charge of your health. (Free for NZ Citizens/Residents, others pay fee of $120)

Aging is inevitable, but how you age is up to you. This essential course offers men a rare opportunity to confront the physical, mental, and social changes that come with age, with a holistic approach to staying strong and engaged.

What We’ll Cover:

Physical Health:

Stay Ahead: Preventive care strategies to keep you at your best.
Fuel & Fitness: Nutrition and exercise tips tailored for men over 40.
Managing Chronic Conditions: Tackle issues like hypertension and diabetes head-on.
Mental Health:

Stay Sharp: Techniques to manage stress, anxiety, and maintain cognitive health.
Mind & Mood: Discover mindfulness and relaxation for a balanced mind.
Movement & Activity:

Keep Moving: Boost flexibility, balance, and strength to stay active.
Strength Training: Simple routines to maintain muscle and vitality.
Your Role in the World:

Purpose & Identity: Redefine your role and find fulfilment in later life.

Balance: Master the art of balancing work, life, and leisure.
Fact vs. Fiction:

Why Join?

description:
Men's Group Tuesday 4 Nov, To join this community, and book your Tuesdays - please sign up here first, and we will email you the event access code. (our venue address will be emailed with your ticket).

Join the event waitlist if meetings are full - a space often comes up!

This is a space for men to talk honestly, listen deeply, and figure some things out together. It’s not about having it all sorted. You don’t need to be a certain type of guy. Just show up as you are, with no pressure to speak or share.
Open group: new faces are welcome
Flexible: book to come when it suits you. Each Tuesday is a new event. Most will try for fortnightly.
Small groups: 8 men + 2 experienced hosts
Inclusive: all ages, backgrounds and identities (18+)
It's about connection and showing up, instead of going it alone. It makes time for meeting our various challenges, while learning and growing us into better friends, partners, dads, sons and humans. 
It isn’t a boys or bros club, therapy or a self-help seminar, religious or new-age. No fixing, preaching, or pressure. It’s not a crisis service or a substitute for professional care. The sign-up form explains this in more detail.
You can expect to:
Practice listening and learn communication skills for life
Discover greater integrity and self-accountability
