description:
From Sad Blokes to Well Men: Changing the focus in male suicide prevention - Wellington (FSB2025/01), From anxious boys through to lonely old men: Tracking male suicide across the lifespan.
Are traditional notions of masculinity meeting the needs of men in 2025? How the changes in men’s roles and identity contribute to suicide in men.
A vision for men’s wellbeing: An holistic approach to conceptualising men’s wellbeing and key mental wellbeing messages for men.

IT'S TIME FOR A SERIOUS RETHINK IN HOW WE PREVENT MALE SUICIDE AND TO TAKE SUICIDE IN MEN OUT OF THE TOO HARD BASKET
Hear a comprehensive critique of why the suicide rate in men continues to rise despite numerous prevention initiatives, the reasons why we need to focus more on men and what are proven effective strategies for preventing male suicide.
BE PART OF THE SOLUTION
DURING THE MONTH OF MOVEMBER TAKE THE OPPORTUNITY TO PARTICIPATE  IN A WORKSHOP WITH INTERNATIONALLY RESPECTED AND AWARD WINNING MALE SUICIDE PREVENTION SPECIALIST BARRY TAYLOR
It matters that men are able to optimise their wellbeing.  Well men contribute to well whānau, communities, workplaces, economy and society
Drawing on his lived experience and his extensive experience in engaging, assessing and supporting suicidal men of different cultures, age groups, genders and sexualities, he offers practical and time proven approaches to both preventing male suicide, engaging with a suicidal man and effective initiatives to optimise men's wellbeing.
"Helping men and boys to transition from sad blokes to well men must be our focus"
Topics covered:
Overview of suicide in men
The Suicidal Moment - Men's thoughts, emotions and behaviour in the "suicidal moment”
Masculinity for the 21st Century - Are traditional notions of masculinity meeting the needs of men in 2024?  How the changes in men’s roles and identity contribute to suicide in men.
Intergenerational suicide among men and its impact of whānau and hapū
From anxious boys through to lonely old men: Tracking male suicide across the lifespan
A vision for men’s wellbeing: An holistic approach to conceptualising men’s wellbeing and key mental wellbeing messages for men.
Tāne Ora: An Online Masterclass in creating a movement of wellbeing for men by men
Participants would benefit from also registering for this online workshop series which takes a deep dive into the determinants of wellbeing men and what is required to shift very ingrained understandings in men about being a man.  How do we move from a program approach to mens wellbeing to a movement of change that engages men in conversations of the heart and the walrua. Click here for more information and to register
Previous participants’ feedback
"The best and most informative workshop I have attended in 27 years of mental health nursing”  Community Mental Health Nurse
"This workshop should be compulsory for anyone working with men”  Male Family Violence Worker
"It was as if Barry was talking about every young man I see at school”  School Counsellor
"I have much better understanding about depressed and suicidal men. Going away with lots of ideas of how work with the men in my community”  Rural Community Support Worker
"Thanks for being so inclusive of older men. They are so often forgotten”. Aged Care Worker
"The incorporation of Te Ao Māori into the training was excellent and made it relevant to the men I work with” Kaiwhakatere
"Most helpful workshop I have been to in regards to suicide”  Mental Health Recovery Programme Facilitator
"Appreciated how inclusive the presenter was of different cultures and his analysis of how culture influences what it means to be a man”  Refugee Health Worker
An opportunity for a day of learning with award winning suicidologist, Barry Taylor

Barry has worked in mental wellbeing promotion and suicide prevention / postvention for 36 years at the local, national and international levels.
His extensive experience includes sitting on state and national government advisory committees; designing, implementing and evaluating suicide prevention programs; writing suicide prevention guidelines, training packages and community resources; providing technical advice to and evaluating  community based and national mental wellbeing and suicide prevention programmes.
He has led multiple ‘firsts’ in the context of suicide prevention and postvention both in Aotearoa New Zealand and overseas. In recognition of his outstanding contribution to mental wellbeing and suicide prevention, he was awarded in 2016 the NSW Mental Health Commissioner's Community Champion Award. He currently sits one of the Health and Disability Ethics Committees.
As a health sociologist, Barry offers an unique insight into the phenomenon of male suicide in the Aotearoa-New Zealand context, the role of gender construction and the critical social and cultural determinants that have influenced men's distress and impaired wellbeing. Insights that very few others trainers or training programs in this country provide. He has had an interest on the impact of social exclusion, alienation and discrimination on mens's wellbeing and advocates for a human rights based, systems change and person-centred approach to wellbeing.The workshop is enriched by the learnings from Barry's own lived experience of depression and suicidality and his pathway to recovery.
After a number of years overseas, Barry is living back in New Zealand and is passionate about building the knowledge base, competence and capability within our country to effectively respond to the unacceptably high rate of male suicide in this country.

WORKSHOP DETAILS
WORKSHOP PLACES ARE LIMITED. REGISTER EARLY TO AVOID DISAPPOINTMENT
Places in the workshop are limited. If the workshop is full please register your name on the waitlist

description:
Peace Over Panic: Rewiring the Anxious Mind, Peace Over Panic: Rewiring the Anxious Mind
Tuesday Wellbeing Seminars
How do we respond when the mind spirals, when uncertainty clouds our judgement, or when we find ourselves at a personal breaking point? This workshop draws from ancient and time-tested wisdom found in yoga philosophy — especially the Bhagavad Gita — to explore how anxiety and inner conflict can become turning points for insight and conscious action.
Rather than avoiding or suppressing discomfort, the Gita offers a way to meet it — with clarity, honesty, and meaningful direction. Through a blend of philosophical reflection and grounded tools, we’ll explore how to steady the mind and reconnect with deeper purpose in the face of pressure or doubt.


Meet your host, Sivananda
Sivananda’s background is in both science and music, holding a Master's Degree in Data Science, a Bachelor of Science in Physics and a long history of playing and performing on the double bass. His passion for philosophy, music, good food and community attracted him to the bhakti lifestyle. Having studied and practiced the science of the ‘self’ for six years now, he thrives on drawing insights from contemporary science and combining it with yoga wisdom to encourage others to explore new perspectives.
6.15pm Tuesday 19th August
End the session on a high note with a delicious plant-based dinner, dessert, and good conversation.

description:
JOURNEY TO SERENITY Yin Yoga and Sound Healing Special Series # 6, JOURNEY TO SERENITY Yin Yoga and Sound Healing Special Series # 6
Welcome to the Journey to Serenity Yin Yoga and Sound Healing twice monthly session!!!

These beautiful, nourishing Yin and Sound Sessions are on the second and Fourth Friday of each month (between November 28th and March 28th 2026) and are held in the Meeting / Supper Room of the Paraparaumu Memorial Hall on Tutanekai Street.

It would be lovely to share this healing, nourishing restorative practice in the special energy and support you to give your life a boost of wellbeing and good feeling!!

INTRODUCING - SPECIAL WELCOME OFFER - TWO PEOPLE FOR $90 (You don't have to be a couple) - Book through bank transfer or message me directly

Unique Yin Yoga, Sound Healing, breathwork and meditation come together to create this specially guided, deeply relaxing and healing experience, held within a unique holistic framework that encourages reflection and self-care.

Take the time and space that this session offers, for yourself, and give yourself a gift of true nourishment.

Bryony Shanti from Heartsong Healing will use a wide range of special healing sound instruments along with her voice to guide you into peace, relaxation and stillness. The healing vibration of the specially chosen sounds will wash over you bringing deep clearing, balance and alignment. There is a experience of gratitude and blessing.

Bryony's sound works to strengthen and deepen the powerful work of Yin yoga teacher Bianca who will guide you through 90-minutes of restorative and uplifting poses that are intended for you relax, heal and to open more to receive the healing vibration of sound.

Bianca's Yin Yoga focuses on easily accessible and effective poses
which work into the deeper connective tissues of the body. These poses are held for anywhere between 90 seconds to 5 minutes to allow for an opening in the body, the perfect way to receive the sound vibrations.

Yin yoga invites us into stillness and gives space for both the mind and body to relax and release tension and stress.

OPEN TO ALL: Whether you have experience in either Yin Yoga and Sound Healing or are looking to try something completely new, you are very welcome to join in the journey into a sense of serenity. Beginners and experienced practitioners alike will find something nourishing for themselves in this session.

Habit mastery series: Goal setting for the new year, The new year is fast approaching! Discover how to unlock your potential with this interactive goal-setting workshop so you can smash those new year’s resolutions. Learn how to clarify aspirations, set goals, and develop a success-driven mindset. Gain practical tools to turn dreams into actionable plans through hands-on exercises and expert insights.
This workshop is ideal for professionals, entrepreneurs, students, and anyone seeking clarity and direction in their personal or professional life.

The psychology of goal setting
Creating goals

Harnessing visualization and mindset for success


Join us for

Expert Insights: Best practices from experienced facilitators

Meet your host, Nava
Nava’s natural interest in studying physics blossomed into metaphysics when she arrived in the land of the long white cloud. Originally from Germany, Nava was travelling on a quest to find purpose and answers to the mysteries of life. Did she find anything on this quest? Yes! description:
Yoga for all at Kai Tahi, WHAT IS YOGA?

Yoga is breathing deeply, letting go of anxious thoughts, being kind, stretching the body to reduce tension, being more flexible, focusing and being mindful, and moving into different shapes.

WHAT ARE THE BENEFITS OF YOGA?

Get stronger, more flexible and re-learn to relax in these Sunday morning community yoga classes. Reduce your blood pressure, improve circulation, reduce anxiety – and find more ease in your body.

HOW WILL I FEEL AFTER MY SUNDAY YOGA FOR ALL AT KAITAHI CLASS?

Lighter, more relaxed, stronger, more confident, calmer.

WHAT KIND OF YOGA WILL I BE DOING?

A mix of vinyasa, hatha, restorative and mindfulness. Beginners welcome.

WHO IS THE YOGA TEACHER
Janie Walker is a trained and experienced vinyasa, restorative and yin yoga teacher. She weaves ancient yoga philosophy and breath work with easy poses. Her classes are for beginners and those who want to deepen their own practice. Janie focuses on relaxation, breath and relaxing the mind.

DO I NEED TO BOOK?
No bookings necessary. Just turn up. Yoga mats provided or bring your own.

CAN I DO YOGA IF I AM INJURED OR RECOVERING FROM ILLNESS OR DON'T FEEL GOOD?
If you have an injury or want to chat about how to look after yourself during your yoga class, please do contact Janie: becalmed@outlook.co.nz or phone 0274539099. If you have any other physical or mental conditions that you’d like Janie to be aware of so she can support you, please contact her.

Find out more about BeCalmed Studio and Janie Walker - www.becalmedstudio.com

description:
Sapphic Somatics: A Queer Embodiment Workshop, Somatic: Of or relating to the body, particularly the perception of physical sensations and internal experiences

What does it feel like to exist in a queer body? This workshop aims to help sapphic women and gender minorities find joy, freedom, and connection through somatic exercises that help you embody and embrace your queerness. You will leave feeling confident in your ability to take up space, tapped into your self-expression, and connected to the experiences of your sapphic community.

Here’s a rundown of what to expect:
- Opening Exercise: Guided somatic movements to loosen up your body
- Community Connection: Rotating conversations with other participants about queer embodiment
- Yoga Flow: Creative 30 minute class to explore presence and connect to your self-expression
- Guided Meditation: A relaxing combination of breathwork and visualisation to cultivate safety in your body
- Closing Connection: Gentle and grounding partner stretches (optional) and group reflections

No prior experience with yoga or somatic exercises necessary! All you need to attend is an open attitude, a desire to connect with your body, and a willingness to share your experience with other sapphics. Yoga mats and all other props will be provided. While this workshop dives into your embodied expression of sapphic identity/sexuality, none of the content is sexual in nature and the small amount of physical touch with other participants is completely optional.

The workshop is facilitated by Megan Edwards, creator of Golden Hour Gigs and a 500CYT trauma-informed yoga teacher. This event is intended specifically for queer women and gender minorities. No TERFS no SWERFS.

Content consideration: Because this workshop focuses on finding joy/pleasure/freedom in your body, it might not be suited for anyone experiencing intense dysphoria. With that said, you are welcome to explore discomfort and work on integrating challenging feelings. Megan is trained in movement techniques to lead you away from anything too activating and help you find safety in your body.

Accessibility: Awhi Yoga Studios has an elevator and is wheelchair accessible. There are plenty of gender neutral bathrooms and cosy spaces to rest if you need a break from the workshop at any time. If you want to attend but are concerned about the level of movement required, please email the organiser with any mobility questions at goldenhourgigz@gmail.com

Attuning to the wisdom of the menstrual cycle offers a meaningful way to honour your natural rhythms, nurturing ease, creativity, and deeper connection.

Amy Richards is a yoga teacher based in Pōneke, with a Certificate in MindBody Therapy. She is drawn to the ways intentional movement and embodied awareness can shape our inner experience and relationships. Grounded in a trauma-informed approach, her work invites exploration of connection with sensation and emotion through the body.

Her practice is influenced by Tantric Shaivism, contact improvisation, Open Floor dance, Hakomi somatic psychotherapy, Somatic Experiencing, Authentic Relating, and Nonviolent Communication.

description:
Women's Circle: To Be Heard, Women’s Circle: To Be Heard

This Women’s Circle is a calm and inclusive space designed to support rest, reflection, and gentle healing.

It’s a space where nothing is required of you. The circle is held in a way that allows women to settle into themselves, whether through quiet listening, reflection, or sharing if that feels right. Healing in this circle isn’t something that’s forced or directed - it’s something that naturally unfolds when there is safety, presence, and care.

The gathering begins with a simple guided experience to settle into the space. Followed by a prompt - everyone will a chance to share, within the circle, their mind and heart.

This is a space to be simply heard in a healing presence - one that supports the nervous system, release, and a sense of being genuinely heard.

There will be a nourishing plant-based dinner and dessert to close the night in a grounded and connected way.

Meet Rasesvari, your host

Rasesvari (pronounced Rah-say-shwari) has always remembered feeling connected to healing, the inner world, and what helps people feel well and in alignment. Growing up, she spent many hours tucked away in libraries, reading about philosophy, plants, and anything that felt meaningful or life-giving.

Her path took a few turns away from the usual route, leading her to spend six years in an ashram setting focused on self-development and spiritual life. During that time, she explored meditation deeply and had space to focus on her own healing. This naturally opened the door to learning about Traditional Chinese healing, Ayurveda, sound healing, bioresonance, and a lot of self-study along the way.

Her loving aim is simple: to create spaces where people can finally feel comfortable in themselves again and and have room for their own growth and healing.

description:
Held in Motion - Somatic Movement Series, Held in Motion
A 5-Week Evening Somatic Movement Series for Women
April 13 – May 11
(Monday evenings)
6pm - 7.15 pm

Held in Motion is a five-week somatic movement series for women who want to deepen their relationship with body, expression, and emotion …especially if you feel disconnected, overwhelm, or unsure how to stay in contact with what you’re feeling
At its core, somatic movement is movement guided by internal experience rather than external form.
It is movement led by sensation, impulse, breath, rhythm, and nervous system cues, rather than choreography, performance, or aesthetics.
This space is about listening.
~ Listening to impulse.
~ Listening to instinct.
~ Listening to sensation before it becomes story.
~ Listening to breath, to boundary, to rhythm.
~ Listening to the subtle yes.
~ Listening to the quiet no.
~ Listening to the places that want to soften.
~ Listening to the places that want to move.
Through free movement (without performance), somatic exploration, nervous system awareness, and guided reflection, we explore how to remain in relationship with ourselves while moving.
Each evening is intentionally paced and thoughtfully facilitated, weaving together:
~ Nervous system literacy, gently and accessibly held
~ Nervous system regulation practices integrated throughout
~ Guided somatic movement and free exploration
~ Music that fuels expression
~ Journaling and reflection
~ Moments of softness and moments of wildness
~ Joy. Depth. Integration.

Together we will explore how to:
~ Strengthen internal resourcing
~ Reconnect to sensation
~ Expand capacity for feeling without overwhelm
~ Access playful aliveness
~ Strengthen embodied choice and agency
~ Integrate emotional experience through the body
---
This is for you if…
~ You want to feel more at home in your body.
~ You sense there is more expression inside you than you currently allow.
~ You feel caught in thinking and long for deeper connection with your whole body.
~ You are curious about movement as a pathway to emotional integration.
~ You are ready to listen to your body in a new way.
~~~
REGISTER
Please fill out this form to register: https://forms.gle/vvGR4XzbynNBHEoa7
Spaces are limited to allow for a deeper, more intimate experience.
Cost: $30–$40 per session ($120–$160 for the 4-week series)
Choose the rate that feels right for you. Sliding scale pricing is to make this space more accessible while supporting the container we’re creating together.
***If finances are an issue for you to attend this space, please email me directly at freedomofsoma@gmail.com so we talk further

~~~

Four weeks.
Evenings devoted to the body.
If you are curious about using movement as a way back to yourself, without pressure, without needing to be a “dancer,” without needing to be anything other than human, this space may be for you.

See you soon!
Fiona x

~~
About me, your facilitator:
Hi, I am Fiona
I am an Integrative Somatic Facilitator™, Somatic Dance Facilitator, and Therapeutic Yoga Teacher devoted to helping people come home to their bodies. Drawing on somatics, nervous system literacy, and embodied movement, I hold spaces that support regulation, expression, and deeper self-connection.
Alongside my facilitation work, I collaborate with SomaPsych, supporting the expansion of trauma-informed and somatic education into community-centred trainings for vulnerable populations, minority communities, and professionals in demanding, client-facing roles. I am passionate and commitmed to collective care and to making embodied practice accessible beyond traditional wellness spaces.

description:
Gut Health - With Nutritionist Lucy Edwards, Gut Health - With Nutritionist Lucy Edwards
Do you suffer from bloating, constipation, stomach cramps, weight gain, stubborn fat, or a low mood? Or do you just want you and your family to be healthier?

Did you know your gut influences every aspect of you health and wellbeing?

In this fun, relaxing, educational and interactive workshop, you will learn from one of New Zealand's leading Nutritionists Lucy Edwards. Lucy will guide you through some easy and effective tips that you can use to improve your health today. You will also be provided organic herbal teas to sip on chosen especially for gut health.

More about your host Nutritionist Lucy Edwards
A qualified Nutritionist, Natural Therapist and health and wellness expert with more than 20 years experience. She is also an author of 'The Grumbles' a children's book about having a positive mindset.

Lucy is also available to provide workshops and wellness retreats both in person and online, on a range of health-related topics. She will tailor these to suit the participants and the topics they are most interested in or concerned about, such as popular diet and exercise trends and the research around these, or information regarding Health and Nutrition in general. Some of her high end clients include Shortland Street Actors, International Comedians, Olympic Athletes and NZ Army Fitness Instructors.

description:
The Check-in Men's Group Tuesday 9th Sept, To join this community, and book your Tuesdays - please sign up here first, and we will email you the event access code. (our venue address will emailed with your ticket).

This is a space for men to talk honestly, listen deeply, and figure some things out together. It’s not about having it all sorted. You don’t need to be a certain type of guy. Just show up as you are, with no pressure to speak or share.
Open group: new faces are welcome
Flexible: book to come when it suits you. Each Tuesday is a new event. Most will try for fortnightly.
Small groups: 8 men + 2 experienced hosts
Inclusive: all ages, backgrounds and identities (18+)
It's about connection and showing up, instead of going it alone. It makes time for meeting our various challenges, while learning and growing us into better friends, partners, dads, sons and humans. 
It isn’t a boys or bros club, therapy or a self-help seminar, religious or new-age. No fixing, preaching, or pressure. It’s not a crisis service or a substitute for professional care. The sign-up form explains this in more detail.
You can expect to:
Practice listening and learn communication skills for life
Discover greater integrity and self-accountability

description:
Emotional Mapping: Making Sense of Your Emotional Patterns, Emotional Mapping: Making Sense of Your Emotional Patterns
Have you ever noticed that when emotions arise, you tend to react in the same ways over and over again?
Some of us shut down, disconnect, or tell ourselves we’re “fine.”
Others overthink, become emotionally overwhelmed, or feel responsible for everyone else’s feelings.
These are emotional patterns, learned ways of relating to emotions that helped us cope in the past, but can leave us feeling anxious, reactive, disconnected, or exhausted.
Most of us were never taught how to understand emotions in a healthy way.
Instead, we learned to avoid feelings, get stuck in them, or stay hyper-aware of the emotional states of others.
Emotional literacy offers another path.
It helps us recognise what we’re feeling, understand the needs underneath those feelings, and respond with more awareness and self-compassion.
In this workshop, we’ll explore common emotional patterns, what they can look like in everyday life, and how we can begin shifting them over time.
You’ll leave with:
•⁠ ⁠a clearer understanding of your emotional patterns
•⁠ ⁠practical tools to help you respond differently to emotions
•⁠ ⁠greater awareness of feelings, needs, and nervous system responses
•⁠ ⁠simple ways to build more emotional steadiness and self-empathy
This is a warm, supportive space to better understand yourself, connect more deeply with your emotions, and leave with tools you can actually use.
Plus - your ticket includes a plant-based dinner & dessert! You’ll get to eat good food in good company and have a wholesome evening
Meet your host, Chanchala:
Chanchala combines her background in psychology, education, and coaching to help people make sense of their emotions. She loves sharing practical ways to meet feelings, build clarity, and navigate overwhelm with confidence and self-compassion.

description:
NMHNZ CONFERENCE '26, NMHNZ CONFERENCE '26
Catalysts for Change : Naturopathy as Primary Care
Transforming lives and Redefining community healthcare
NMHNZ is proud to be at the forefront of Naturopathy as the leading Professional Organisation for Naturopaths and Medical Herbalists in Aotearoa New Zealand. This year our focus is on HOW we can position Naturopaths and Medical Herbalists at the forefront of health care in Aotearoa New Zealand, and beyond.
Our "Catalysts for Change" themed conference is all about putting our profession front and centre.We delved into our rich international heritage and explored the connections between the two, in 2024. We discussed how at the heart of Naturopathic and Herbal Medicine lies a profound respect for the wisdom of the past with a commitment to researching the benefits for our lives today.
2026 sees us looking outside the box at HOW we can be more active and visible.Our next step is to learn about integrating this knowledge and connection into primary health care.
Preventative health is at the forefront of everything we do, along with lifestyle, nutrition, and science. We want to redefine Community Healthcare using Naturopathic principles, Science & Research, and of course Nature.
This event will inspire and motivate you to get out & be a Catalyst for Change in your community.Our International speakers are renowned for delivering relevant and useable content, enabling our delegates to make use of every piece of information gathered.
Over two very full days, we will welcome speakers from around the globe. Along with delegates from Naturopathy, Herbal Medicine, and other Allied Health communities.

description:
Master Your Mornings: a Routine That Works, Master Your Mornings: a Routine That Works
Take charge of your mornings and set yourself up for a productive, goal-driven day. A well-designed morning routine can give you focus, fuel your ambition, and create momentum that carries through everything you do. In this workshop, you’ll learn how to build a routine that fits your life and drives your success.
Dive into the science behind effective morning habits, pick up practical strategies for building routines that stick, and explore proven techniques to stay motivated and on track. Whether your goal is to enhance productivity, improve wellbeing, or cultivate mindfulness, this workshop gives you the tools to turn your mornings into a launchpad for long-term achievements.
What You’ll Gain:
Understand the psychology behind morning habits and why they matter
Discover how to design a morning routine tailored to your personal and professional aspirations
Learn time-tested strategies for consistency and maintaining motivation
Build a morning routine that sets you up for success, every single day
Leave this workshop with a clear, actionable morning routine that empowers you to take control of your day, and more importantly, your future.
Start each day with intention, clarity, and the momentum to reach your goals
Meet Nava, your host
Nava’s natural interest in studying physics blossomed into metaphysics when she arrived in the land of the long white cloud. Originally from Germany, Nava was travelling on a quest to find purpose and answers to the mysteries of life. Did she find anything on this quest? Yes! After eight exciting years of inquiry, practice, and being immersed in the wonders of bhakti yoga, she is still fully inspired by what she has discovered. Mesmerized by the depth and broad-mindedness that a yoga lifestyle offers, Nava is keen to share with you that wisdom and love, along with her experiences on the road of life. description:
Breathe Bro Workshop, Breathe Bro Workshop
𝗕𝗿𝗲𝗮𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗕𝗿𝗼❟ is a grounded no-bulls**t!

Breathwork Workshop/Journey created to support men with their mental heath, emotional resilience and overall wellbeing. This space is design for the brothers, father's, uncles, grandfather's who are ready to release stress, reconnect with their bodies and breathe through the pressure that life stacks on their shoulders.

Through a guided breathwork journey, somatic releases, and simple tools you can take home. Breathe Bro helps you reset your mind, regulate your nervous system and step back into your life with clarity, strength and presence.

A space for men to breathe, feel and heal together.

𝗕𝗥𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗛𝗘 𝗕𝗥𝗢 — 𝗧𝗛𝗘 𝗠𝗘𝗗𝗜𝗖𝗜𝗡𝗘 𝗢𝗙 𝗕𝗥𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗛

Breathwork is an ancient, evidence-informed practice used to support healing, regulation, and expansion by bridging the nervous system, body, and mind.

Through conscious, circular breathing, altered states of awareness naturally arise. These states allow stored stress, emotion, and somatic tension to move through and release — without force.
Breathe Bro works directly with:

• Nervous system regulation
• Somatic intelligence
• Emotional processing
• Mental clarity and presence

As the breath deepens, the body’s own chemistry shifts. Breathwork influences brainwaves, cerebrospinal fluid, and the body’s innate capacity to reset.

Gamma brainwaves — associated with deep meditation, heightened awareness, and peak cognitive function — may be activated.

Some research suggests correlations between breathwork, gamma states, and the body’s natural production of endogenous DMT (often referred to as the “spirit molecule”). While experiences vary, many men report effects similar to deep meditation, emotional release, insight, and renewed clarity — without the use of substances.

Each journey is unique and meets you exactly where you are.

𝗕𝗥𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗛𝗘 𝗕𝗥𝗢 — 𝗕𝗘𝗡𝗘𝗙𝗜𝗧𝗦

Men attend Breathe Bro to support:

• Stress, anxiety, and burnout
• Emotional regulation and resilience
• Improved sleep and recovery
• Increased energy and vitality
• Lower blood pressure and improved circulation
• Nervous system balance
• Releasing stored tension, stress, and emotional weight
• Trauma release and PTSD support (trauma-aware, non-forcing)
• Improved body awareness
• Greater emotional capacity and presence
• Mental clarity and focus
• Pain and restriction relief
• Increased confidence and self-trust
• Reconnection to intuition and inner guidance
• Purpose clarity and grounded direction
• Creativity and self-expression
This is not about “fixing” yourself — it’s about creating the space to reset, regulate, and come back into alignment.

𝗖𝗢𝗡𝗧𝗥𝗔𝗜𝗡𝗗𝗜𝗖𝗔𝗧𝗜𝗢𝗡𝗦 & 𝗦𝗔𝗙𝗘𝗧𝗬

Breathwork can create intense physical, emotional, and neurological responses.
For your safety, it is essential that you inform the facilitator prior to attending if any of the following apply:

• Severe PTSD or unresolved trauma
• Current use of recreational drugs
• Recent surgery
• Medications that alter brain chemistry (e.g. antidepressants, anti-anxiety, ADHD, OCD medications)
• Detached retina or glaucoma
• Kidney disease
• High blood pressure
• Cardiovascular disease (including previous heart attack or stroke)
• History of aneurysm
• Uncontrolled thyroid conditions
• Diabetes
• Severe asthma (mild/moderate may attend with discussion and inhaler)
• Epilepsy or history of seizures
• Bipolar disorder, schizophrenia, or previous psychiatric diagnosis
• Any condition that may impair participation in deep emotional or physical release

This space is trauma-aware and integrity-led. Adjustments may be possible just reach out.

❓ 𝗙𝗔𝗤’𝗦

Q. What actually happens in a Breathe Bro session?

A. You’ll be guided through breathwork after a clear explanation of the technique, what sensations may occur, and how to stay regulated. There’s space afterwards for reflection, questions, and grounding.

Q. I’m not very emotional — is this still for me?

A. Yes. Breathwork meets you exactly where you are. Some men experience emotional release, others experience calm, clarity, insight, or deep relaxation.

Q. What if I have one of the contraindications?

A. Contact Tara via email () beforehand. She’ll let you know if adjustments can be made or if it’s best to wait.

🎟️ 𝗧𝗜𝗖𝗞𝗘𝗧𝗦 & 𝗦𝗣𝗔𝗖𝗘𝗦

Limited to 15 men — to maintain safety, presence, and integrity of the space.

• Standard Ticket — $55 per person ( Limited to 10 spaces)

• Koha / Flexible Pricing — (Limited to 5 spaces)

• Pay-It-Forward / Donation — Supports another bro to attend (This is advertised on IG @breathe.bro.nz - tag a bro)

𝗔𝗯𝗼𝘂𝘁 𝗧𝗮𝗿𝗮 𝗚𝗼𝗱𝗱𝗮𝗿𝗱 — 𝗙𝗼𝘂𝗻𝗱𝗲𝗿 & 𝗙𝗮𝗰𝗶𝗹𝗶𝘁𝗮𝘁𝗼𝗿 𝗼𝗳 𝗕𝗿𝗲𝗮𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗕𝗿𝗼 🤙

Tara Goddard, from Wainuiomata, Wellington, New Zealand, she is a Sacred Ceremonialist, facilitator, and teacher, bridging esoterics and science together through spirit-led guidance, informed through lived, embodied experience.

She is the founder of Esoteric Medicine Pathways and Breathe Bro, creating grounded sacred spaces that support mental health, emotional resilience, and holistic wellbeing.

Tara’s breathwork lineage comes through HineHau BreathWav, an IPHM-accredited modality founded by Shannon Rose. BreathWav uses circular connected breath through an open mouth to activate the body’s natural, inbuilt healing intelligence.

This approach does not force cathartic release; instead, it honours the wisdom of the body by gently riding the waves of what naturally arises. Each session is intuitively crafted as Tara attunes to the group’s magnetic field, shaping the ceremony in response to the collective energy in the space.

With many years of experience across multiple modalities including Breathwork, Quantum Healing Hypnosis (QHHT Level 2), Sound Therapy, Energy Healing, Intuitive Readings, Plant Medicine, and Coaching & Mentoring Tara holds grounded yet multidimensional spaces that support nervous system regulation, emotional processing, and deep reconnection to self.

Tara’s work is trauma-aware and community-centred, supporting individuals navigating stress, burnout, grief, and mental health challenges. Through breath, presence, and embodied practice, she offers pathways that foster resilience, connection, and self-trust. Her facilitation gently reminds people that they are not broken they have always been the healer, and the medicine they seek already lives within them.

Tara is devoted to guiding people to bridge their inner knowing into embodied, everyday life, empowering them to reclaim their wellbeing, sovereignty, and sense of belonging to themselves, to each other, and to the world around them.

🫁 Breathe. Reset. Carry less ❤️

#BreathBro #MensMentalHealth #BreatheBrother #StrongMenBreathe

description:
Yoga & Cacao Twilight & Morning Edition - Pop-up Parlour 2025, Yoga & Cacao Twilight & Morning Edition - Pop-up Parlour 2025
Come and join Jenny Allan for a cup of ceremonial grade cacao and then enjoy a gentle and relaxing restorative yoga practice - twilight edition.

Event details

• What - 1.5 hr yoga session
• When -
TWILIGHT EDITION 7-8.30pm Tuesday 23rd September 2025
MORNING EDITION 10-11.30am Saturday 27th September 2025

• Where - St Marks, 98 Rāwhiti Road, Pukerua Bay
• Cost - $30 per person

• What to bring? Most props are provided but feel free to bring your own yoga mat for extra comfort as well as a cushion, blanket, socks and an eye pillow if you have one.

This class is suitable for all - no previous experience is required.

LIMITED SPACES AVAILABLE.
TWILIGHT SESSION - https://tinyurl.com/5a2pad44
MORNING SESSION - https://tinyurl.com/mw82z38u

Restorative yoga is a practice of deep relaxation in which yoga poses (asanas) are held for several minutes with the use of props such as blocks, bolster and blankets to support the body in gentle stretch.

When your body enters a state of relaxation, your mind will also relax. Once you have been guided to let go of any tension held in your body, all that is required is for you to pay attention to your breath, becoming aware of any thoughts that may arise, and then gently releasing them.

This restful practice calms both mind and body, allowing the nervous system to enter its parasympathetic state – the state of “rest and digest”, in which healing is enabled and enhanced.

About Cacao
Cacao is a superfood. It is plant medicine at its finest. Its scientific name, Theobroma cacao, translates to "food of the Gods." Containing many minerals, vitamins and phytonutrients such as flavonoids and antioxidants, it is also an extremely potent adaptogen that encourages the body to come into balance.

Cacao has a particularly strong influence on the heart – both physiologically and energetically. Cacao anecdotally is reported to enhance connection in community, hence its beauty in being shared in circle.

In Jenny's experience, cacao seems to enhance whatever it is that you are doing – be it running a marathon or meditating – cacao can make you go deeper, further, longer, stronger. However she does seem to have a mind of her own so be open to the experience she offers you – it may not always go quite how you planned!

Cacao is very safe and tolerated well by most people. However it is to be used with caution by people who are taking high doses of SSRIs. Cacao releases serotonin, so it works as a mild antidepressant. When taken with SSRIs that release serotonin chemically it can lead to serotonin syndrome. Individuals taking MAOIs (monoamine oxidase inhibitors) should avoid consuming ceremonial cacao due to potential interactions.

This workshop is part of Pukerua Bay Hub's 2025 Pop-up Parlour - thanks for your support.

____________________________________
WHAT IS THE POP-UP PARLOUR?
A chance to create and connect in our pop-up community space. From 1-30 September we've got a jam-packed schedule with 65+ events for kids, families, rangatahi and adults to learn, hang out and have fun.

Check out all the free/koha events too -
https://www.pkbhub.org.nz/whatson

Waiho i te toipoto, kaua i te toi roa –
Let us keep close together, not far apart.

description:
Set your focus for 2026 - 2 Events, Set your focus for 2026 - 2 Events
UPDATE: the first workshop is fully booked and I have added another for 9.00am-12.00pm on the same day - 31 January. This event has a few spaces available. DM if you would like to join us.

I am really excited to offer this workshop and in such an amazing space. Let's do it. Let's create a 2026 that is amazing.

Each new year we are encouraged to think about new year’s resolutions. As we know these often don’t play out, we are unable to follow through or we lose momentum or we just make them unachievable.

The purpose of this workshop is to explore what you might want to create, change, or stabilise during 2026 through the exploration and the identification of a word that brings the energy you want for the year.

I have been working with this concept of a word for a number of years now and always when I reflect at the closing of the year, I can identify how this has played out during the year. It works! Reset was my word for 2025!!!

On the day we will have a gentle yoga practice (all levels), meditation and journalling prompted by a framework (you’ll find out on the day) that encourages us to open up to the possibilities of 2026 and to set a clear intention of what we want from the year and how one word can energise that intention.

I will provide some materials for you to create a vision board if that is your intention to do so. I will also provide material (or a framework in corporate speak) to support you in your reflections on what it is that you want in 2026.

Investment is $60. If you want to explore Nga Manu as well, I would definitely encourage that, we can provide you with a discounted access ticket of $15.

Grab a free coffee and chat with other parents and carers who know what it’s like to care for a disabled child, a neurodivergent child, or a child with health impairments.


Parent to Parent hosts regular Parent Support Groups in Kāpiti Coast to support carers and parents just like you. These groups are a safe space for family and whānau to share their experiences, their challenges, their triumphs, and to learn from each other in an open and 100% judgement-free environment. description:
Workshop Series: Vinyasa – Mai i te pō ki te ao mārama: POSTPONED, Vinyasa – Mai i te pō ki te ao mārama is a four part workshop series themed on the phases of the Māori creation story (te kore, te pō, te whaiao, and te ao mārama) as a metaphor and framework for confronting the cyclical nature of life. Through vinyasa practice, we explore and embody the phases of the creation story, drawing parallels to yogic wisdom and setting a foundation and spark of curiosity for the wānanga that follows. After a short break, we'll reconvene in a wānanga space to further explore the deep wisdom of the creation story, learn more about the roles of the atua, and begin to unearth the relevance of it all to our own lives.

No prior experience of knowledge required. All are welcome - haere mai!

What to expect:
We will practice vinyasa flow together, sometimes incorporating meditation or breathwork. After a short break for showers/ lunch/coffee, we'll reconvene in a wānanga space to explore the relevant aspects of the Māori creation story for that workshop.

What will people take away from the experience?
A deeper understanding and appreciation of the Māori creation story and how its wisdom can help guide us through life, learned through wānanga and discussion but also through the embodied practice of vinyasa yoga and by drawing parallels with yogic wisdom.

Why Enrol?
To enjoy challenging and fun vinyasa flows themed around the phases of the Māori creation story. To engage in a safe space to ask questions and learn more about the Māori creation story, the phases of creation, the atua, and how the story offers us a framework for moving through the cycles of human life, from darkness to enlightenment.

What to bring:
Bring an open mind, perhaps a journal to gather thoughts down after the workshop. Light kai/food/snacks , as options for buying food are quite limited on the weekends. Mat hire and complementary herbal tea are included in your ticket. Towels can be hired for $3 if needed.

Facilitated by Ani Morris (Ngāti Kahungunu, Te Āti Awa, Rangitāne, Ngāti Mutunga, Rongowhakaata, Ngāti Tūkorehe, Tūhoe, Ngāti Pāhauwera) & Jenny Oliver (RYT 200).

description:
Cultivating Calm: Positive Psychology & Mindfulness, Cultivating Calm: Positive Psychology & Mindfulness
Cultivating Calm: Positive Psychology & Mindfulness

Develop the tools you need for navigating stress, anxiety, and overwhelm.

Mary Rose Dias - Positive Psychology Practitioner (MSc), Yoga Therapist, and Certified iRest®️ Meditation Teacher - will be leading a nourishing and practical evening exploring how mindfulness and positive psychology can help us cultivate greater calm, resilience, and inner steadiness.

Life can be demanding, and when stress builds in our system, it can become difficult to access clarity, ease, and perspective. While we can’t always remove life’s challenges, we can learn to shift our relationship with stress and develop tools that help us meet it with greater awareness and self-compassion.

Through guided meditation, reflection, and experiential practices, you’ll be introduced to accessible tools to help you:
•⁠ ⁠Regulate stress and settle anxiety
•⁠ ⁠Connect with a deeper sense of inner safety and steadiness
•⁠ ⁠Clarify intentions and reconnect with what matters most
•⁠ ⁠Access practical resources for greater calm and wellbeing in everyday life

Together, we’ll explore grounding practices drawn from mindfulness, meditation, and positive psychology, beginning and ending with guided meditation to support integration and deep rest.

This is more than just developing a mindset, you’ll be leaving with something practical to draw upon long after the evening ends.

Open to all - no prior meditation experience needed.

Plus - there is an amazing plant-based dinner & dessert included in your ticket! Stay back for the good food & great company.

Meet Mary Rose
Mary is a Wellington-based holistic therapist, meditation teacher and yoga practitioner whose work gently weaves together evidence-based psychology, ancient yogic wisdom, meditation practices and a whole lot of unconditional love and respect for each person’s unique journey. With over 15 years of experience in wellbeing, counselling, coaching, yoga and meditation, she is deeply passionate about creating nurturing spaces that support healing, self-discovery and inner calm.

Her heart-felt calling is to support others in accessing greater health, healing and happiness. While based in her birthplace of Aotearoa New Zealand, Mary’s dedication to wellness has taken her around the world, including Hawaii, Australia, Ireland, the Czech Republic and the UK, where she has trained with internationally recognised teachers in yoga, meditation and positive psychology. Through private sessions, workshops, courses and retreats, she offers a grounded, compassionate and holistic approach that honours both modern psychology and timeless wisdom traditions.

description:
LIFE DRAWING MEETS YOGA!, LIFE DRAWING MEETS YOGA!
Connect with your body and your creativity in this all-levels workshop that combines vinyasa yoga practice with a fun and interactive Life Drawing class!

The Life Drawing session is based on the teaching methods of Cecil Collins, a British artist whose non-academic approach to drawing challenges you to leave your inhibitions at the door and work freely from your creative centre...much like yoga!

“But I can’t draw!,” you say. Then even better! This approach is perfect for the artist and non-artist alike as it asks you to drop your expectations of what art should look like, inviting you to express the pose from within…just like we do in yoga.

Drinks & nibbles follow, along with a chance for kōrero and connection.

Guided by JoElle Gragilla from South Coast Studio in Island Bay, who studied Cecil’s methods in London and is an artist, art teacher and AWHI-trained yoga teacher. Co-facilitated by AWHI's Lachlan Broughton.

Investment: $75 (includes yoga class, mat & props, art materials, art lesson, herbal tea throughout and drinks & nibbles at the end!)

We've done this workshop before so can confidently say it's a REALLY FUN TIME! Definitely don't miss out!

description:
Mental Health First Aid Aotearoa: POSTPONED, About 1 in 5 New Zealand adults experience a common mental illness each year. Understanding how to talk about mental health and help someone in need are important skills. This certified Mental Health First Aid course, will teach you the skills and knowledge to recognise and respond to someone experiencing mental health challenges. Course fees: If you are a NZ citizen/Permanent Resident Visa the course fee is $199 (evidence of eligibility required). Others pay $499.

Description
Mental Health First Aid is an evidence-based workshop which gives people the skills and confidence to have supportive conversations with their whānau, co-workers and community and help guide them to professional help if needed.

You will learn to recognise common mental health challenges, how to provide initial help to someone experiencing a mental health challenge using a practical, evidence-based action plan, and how to respond in a crisis situation. During the workshop you will have the opportunity to practice new skills in a safe environment.

You must be a NZ citizen or resident to qualify for the reduced course fee. A condition of enrolment is that you must provide photo ID when you attend.

Mental health challenges covered in the course include:

Depression
Anxiety
Psychosis
Substance use problems
Mental Health Crisis Covered:

Suicidal thoughts and behaviours
Non- Suicidal self-injury
Panic attack
Traumatic events
Severe effects from Alcohol or other substance use
Aggressive behaviours
People who attend Mental Health First Aid training can complete a short online assessment to be accredited as a Mental Health First Aider. You must attend the full two days to be able to do the online assessment for accreditation. The accreditation is valid for three years.

If you choose not to be accredited, you will receive a certificate of participation if you attend the full course. You will also receive a workbook as part of your course fee.

A condition of enrolment is that you must provide photo ID when you attend.

Note: Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

Prerequisites
You must be a NZ citizen or resident to qualify for the reduced course fee.

A condition of enrolment is that you must provide photo ID when you attend.

description:
50/100 Hour Yin Yoga Teacher Training, ‘We do not use the body to get into a pose – we use the pose to get into our body.’ (Bernie Clark, author of Yin Yoga)
AWHI Yoga & Wellbeing’s Yoga-Alliance accredited 50/100 hour Yin Yoga Teacher Training (YTT) programme is a comprehensive and thorough journey to becoming a Yin Yoga Teacher. Whether you enrol with a view to teach or as a personal journey towards enhancing your own practice and knowledge, you’ll be guided towards a deeper inner-knowing and understanding of all things Yin. Our programme is suitable for anyone looking to learn more about the anatomy, theory and practice of Yin Yoga – haere mai!
Our 100-hour course is broken into two 50-hour modules - enrol in one or both, it's up to you!
DATES & LOCATIONS:
MODULE 1: FOUNDATIONS OF YIN (50 hours)
Friday-Sunday
20th Feb - 22nd Feb & 27th Feb - 2nd March, 2026
8am-6pm daily
MODULE 2: ADVANCED YIN - TRAUMA-INFORMED TRAINING (50 hours)
Friday-Sunday
13th - 15th March & 27th - 29th March, 2026
9am-6pm
All sessions held at AWHI Yoga & Wellbeing studio, Level 2/15 Johnston Street, Wellington Central.
FACILITATORS:
Co-facilitated by Tom Brotherstone (E-RYT 500) & Kathleen Kuehn (E-RYT 500) who have both trained in Yin Yoga, Meditation, Yoga Nidra and related modalities. Module 2 is assisted by Murray Hemi (CYT 200), a group facilitator with psychotherapy training from the Hakomi Institute and extensive expertise in trauma-sensitive and trauma-informed yoga.
* * * * * * * * * * * *
MORE DETAILS:
Our Yin YTT programme offers the theory, philosophy, anatomy, teaching and sequencing techniques that will enable you to experience and facilitate the profound physical, mental, emotional and energetic benefits of Yin Yoga. You will learn about the body’s subtle energy lines, key poses, sensitive cueing, modifications / intensifications, sequencing basics and how yoga philosophy applies to our practice both on and off the mat.
If teaching is your goal, our training programme also prepares you to join the well-rounded, world-class yoga teaching whānau for which AWHI is so widely renowned. Not interested in teaching? That’s OK, too! Our training is equally suitable for non-teachers who simply want to learn more about their body, being in their body, how to work with meridian lines and other energetic properties, how to apply yoga philosophy to everyday life… and so much more!
Our 50/100 hour Yin Yoga training programme is accredited by Yoga Alliance (YAECP) and can be completed for 50 or 100 Continuing Education credit hours with Yoga Alliance.
Investment Covers all tuition, training materials, unlimited AWHI yoga membership for duration of Modules, and certificate of completion.
*Payment plans available - please email kathleen@awhiyoga.co.nz to discuss!*
You can read more about each module, including registration, pricing & booking details by downloading our information packet through the website at:
https://www.awhiyoga.co.nz/yin-yoga-teacher-training

description:
Mental Wellbeing Impact Assessment Tool Training Programme - Wellington (MWI2026/01), “Facilitating healthy public policy through a systematic wellbeing assessment”
Become more familiar with wellbeing principles and how to undertake a systematic assessment of the impact of policy and programmes on mental wellbeing.
Public policy, the economy, community resilience and assets, the legal and justice systems, urban planning, the environment and human rights all impact on our wellbeing.
Self determination, social agency, mobilisation collective impact, participation and connection, safe and inclusive communities are key determinants for thriving people and economies.
Placing wellbeing at the heart of what we do contributes to a more thriving, inclusive and equitable society.
Register for the Mental Wellbeing Impact Assessment Tool six-day training program. In 2026, TaylorMade will be offering the training program in Wellington.
Mental Wellbeing Impact Assessment Overview
The Mental Wellbeing Impact Assessment (MWIA) provides a structured, evidence-based analysis of how policy development, planning, commissioning and programme delivery impacts on the mental wellbeing of individuals, whānau, hapū, iwi or communities.
With wellbeing and mental health being two areas of social concern in our society, the MWIA provides an ideal methodology for supporting system reform, transformational change and service reorientation using a mental wellbeing lens.
Learn how the MWIA tool can value-add to your work and analysis. The process identifies factors and social determinants that are having, or have the potential to have, a positive or negative impact on mental wellbeing. Once identified, actions and indicators are developed to maximise positive outcomes and provide ways to measure the impact going forward.
MWIA has a key role to play in:
Integrating the promotion of positive mental wellbeing in policy, services and programmes
Enabling a wide range of policy agencies and services to understand and improve their impact on mental wellbeing and preventing adverse impacts.
Developing measures of mental wellbeing to measure outcomes
Community engagement
Commissioning for well-being
An independent first level review of evidence to assess the effectiveness of the MWIA process concluded that there is strong qualitative evidence that the MWIA makes a difference to the way initiatives are implemented.
Utilising this evidence-based tool developed in the United Kingdom to holistically evaluate mental wellbeing in line with the Health Impact Assessment methodology.

description:
🧠 The Starving Brain | A Yogic Perspective on Nourishment, Energy & Focus, 🧠 The Starving Brain | A Yogic Perspective on Nourishment, Energy & Focus
We often talk about eating less — but not about what happens when we don’t eat enough.
When the body is under-fuelled, the brain takes priority — quietly redirecting energy away from digestion, hormones, and even mood regulation.

From a yogic lens, this is more than biology.
When energy stops flowing freely (prana), we lose clarity, intuition, and the natural rhythm that keeps body and mind in harmony.

In this 2-hour workshop, we’ll explore:
• Why the brain “steals” energy when under stress or depletion
• The emotional energy of food — and what your cravings might be teaching you
• The science of fasting — when it supports healing and when it harms
• The gut–brain connection and how awareness restores balance
• How nourishment, breath, and rhythm support neuroplasticity and vitality

We’ll finish with Q&A and discussion to help you integrate the insights into your own life.

💰 $40 per person
📍 Yoga Studio, Raumati South
🔗 Book here:

https://35daydetox.simplybook.net/v2/

“In yoga, nourishment isn’t indulgence — it’s how energy becomes consciousness.”

35 Day Detox | Reclaim your energy. Live with intention.

description:
Men's Group Tuesday 14 Oct, To join this community, and book your Tuesdays - please sign up here first, and we will email you the event access code. (our venue address will be emailed with your ticket).

This is a space for men to talk honestly, listen deeply, and figure some things out together. It’s not about having it all sorted. You don’t need to be a certain type of guy. Just show up as you are, with no pressure to speak or share.
Open group: new faces are welcome
Flexible: book to come when it suits you. Each Tuesday is a new event. Most will try for fortnightly.
Small groups: 8 men + 2 experienced hosts
Inclusive: all ages, backgrounds and identities (18+)
It's about connection and showing up, instead of going it alone. It makes time for meeting our various challenges, while learning and growing us into better friends, partners, dads, sons and humans. 
It isn’t a boys or bros club, therapy or a self-help seminar, religious or new-age. No fixing, preaching, or pressure. It’s not a crisis service or a substitute for professional care. The sign-up form explains this in more detail.
You can expect to:
Practice listening and learn communication skills for life
Discover greater integrity and self-accountability
Be supported in building your emotional intelligence
Become a part of a hearty and diverse community
Have a good time - it’s often uplifting and inspiring.
Joseph and your team of hosts have all been in regular men's groups for many years. The group sometimes offers simple and proven inquiries that draw on NVC, MBSR, active listening, gratitude and more.

description:
When Comfort Isn’t Enough: Finding True Satisfaction, Tuesday Wellbeing Seminars

When Comfort Isn’t Enough: Finding True Satisfaction

We often seek happiness by improving our surroundings, yet true satisfaction comes from within. But what does that really mean, and how can we tap into it? Drawing on the philosophy of the ancient wisdom text Bhagavad Gita, this session explores the key internal shifts that can bring lasting joy, even amidst life’s imperfections.

Meet your host Sivananda:

Sivananda’s background is in both science and music, holding a Master's Degree in Data Science, a Bachelor of Science in Physics and a long history of playing and performing on the double bass. His passion for philosophy, music, good food and community attracted him to the bhakti lifestyle. Having studied and practiced the science of the ‘self’ for six years now, he thrives on drawing insights from contemporary science and combining it with yoga wisdom to encourage others to explore new perspectives.

6.15pm Tuesday 17th of March

End the session on a high note with a delicious plant-based dinner, dessert, and good conversation.

description:
Kia Mau, Kia Mau is an arts festival that showcases innovative Indigenous performances. Held every two years, the festival lights up Wellington's winter. See contemporary performances by leading Tāngata Whenua , Tāngata Moana, and global Indigenous artists. The two-week festival includes dance, theatre, music, and visual art. Audiences can expect to be challenged and thrilled by new work from Aotearoa and beyond.
Kia Mau was first held in 2015, founded by two leading Aotearoa creatives. Hone Kouka (Ngāti Porou, Ngāti Kahungunu, Ngāti Raukawa, Kai Tahu) is the Artistic Director and Chief Executive. Mīria George (Ngāti Kearoa, Ngāti Tuara, Ngāti Awa, Ngāti Kūki ‘Āirani) is the Executive Director.
Hone and Mīria describe themselves as innovators. They push boundaries, redefine forms, and forge new paths. They say the festival is not just a platform for presentations, it’s a hub for kōrero and whanaungatanga . The festival has a strong influence on many artists and plays an important role in shaping New Zealand’s contemporary arts scene. “Kia Mau has continued to thrive because of the imagination and commitment of our artists. And of us to them,” says Mīria.

description:
Matariki Festival Paetūmōkai, Matariki Festival Paetūmōkai
Matariki herenga waka – for all of us.

Kia ora Paetūmōkai whānau, Wairarapa Moana communities. and manuhiri, it's time for us to start planning our Matariki celebrations again.

Soon we will put the call out; for those of you who are storytellers, pumpkin choppers, kawakawa gatherers, kaumatua and kuia chauffeurs, chair putter-outers, table decorators, steam pudding makers, sweepers, child wranglers, photographers, leftover hangi dispersers, fairy light arrangers; our small town makes wonderful things happen when we gather together. If you have helped before, thank you, and if you can help again- thank you- but if you need to rest, we understand. If you haven't helped before- nau mai, haere mai, welcome- and in advance, thank you!

This year’s theme of "Matariki herenga waka" is all about inclusion, and encouraging ALL people to celebrate Matariki together. The theme has strong connections to multi-culturalism and the sharing of cultural practices; we can ALL connect to the core values of Matariki and embrace the celebration of the holiday.

The Māori New Year is a time of reflection, celebration, and preparation. It is a time to remember loved ones, come together with whānau to give thanks to what we have and look forward to the promise of a new year.

In the meantime; here's a new Matariki waiata to get your heart singing. Don't worry, there'll still be the Matariki Macarena!

description:
The New Zealand Stationery Festival, Join us in beautiful Greytown for the first New Zealand Stationery Festival, a boutique celebration of the love of pen and paper!

Opening in our historic Town Hall, this is your chance to explore an inspiring collection of premium international stationery brands, along with handpicked local makers, all under one roof. Featured brands include ArtGecko, A. T. Cross, Esterbrook, Ferris Wheel Press, Lamy, Letts of London, MD Paper, Milan, Moleskine, The Superior Labor, Traveler’s Company, and many more.

From 10 am to 2 pm: Exclusive access for ticket holders to the show, with first-in-line chance to purchase products and take advantage of show special offers. Plus ticket holders also receive a collectable NZ Stationery Festival AS Colour tote bag; this one is going to be very sought-after!

From 11 am to 2 pm: Access for door sales, as capacity allows.

Between 2pm and 4pm: Festival opens for free public access (no entry fee, no totes).

Throughout the day, join creative workshops designed to spark ideas, improve your skills and deepen your love of stationery and creative journaling. See below for a summary of our creative workshops.

The New Zealand Stationery Festival will be intentionally intimate to fit within our wee town; a small but perfectly formed gathering focused on beautiful paper goods, pens, inks, journaling and creative tools, and the wonderful people who enjoy them.

Just an hour from Wellington, Greytown’s charm makes it the perfect setting for a day with your fellow ink lovers, journalers and stationery enthusiasts. Join us for New Zealand’s inaugural biggest little stationery festival!

The New Zealand Stationery Festival 2026
Saturday 10 January 2026, 10 am - 4 pm
Greytown Town Hall, 89 Main Street, Greytown
Creative Workshops:
11am - 11:30am: What the Washi? Introduction to the Creative Uses for Washi Tape
11:45am - 12:45pm The Write Way: A Smooth Introduction to Fountain Pens
1pm - 2pm How to Build a Daily Journaling Habit
2pm - 3pm Plan Your Page - Journaling Creatively
3pm - 4pm Writing to Reveal
Buy workshop tickets here: https://www.eventfinda.co.nz/tours-festivals/2025/new-zealand-stationery-festival
Please note: Event tickets do not give you access to Stationery Festival. Please buy a seperate Stationery Festival ticket, or you can enter for free between 2pm - 4pm (as long as capacity allows).

description:
Rocking Robins Birthday Hop - 6th june, Rocking Robins’ first hop of 2026 is coming up on 6 June and its to celebrate our Birthday!
We’re lucky enough to be hosting this at the Belmont Memorial Hall in Lower Hutt, which has a fabulous dance floor. To make sure there’s plenty of space for everyone to enjoy their dancing, ticket numbers will be limited. If you’d like to reserve a ticket or even a whole table, simply email rockingrobinsdance@gmail.com and we’ll send through payment details. Door sales may also be available, if not sold out prior.
There’ll be some food provided by the committee, and we’d absolutely love it if others could also bring a plate to share. Due to the February weather event, the hall kitchen is currently closed, so any shared kai will need to be ready to serve — unfortunately there are no heating facilities available.
In true Robins style, there’ll be amazing music and entertainment, raffles, and more… come along to find out! You’re also welcome to bring along some drinks if you’d like.
We really appreciate your support — it’s never taken for granted to see people turning up to these events. We try to keep prices as low as possible, with any profit made in June going directly back to supporting our nationals dancers and supporters. So grab some friends and come along for what’s sure to be a great night out.

description:
NZ Brewers Fest - Monster Mash, NZ Brewers Fest - Monster Mash
🦇A Day of Decadence, Darkness and Delights 🕷️

Join us from midday until the shadows stretch long, as Brewtown transforms into a haunted haven of unholy delights for NZ Brewers Fest 2025 – Monster Mash!

🍻Fiendishly good Beer!
It wouldn’t be NZBF without great beer, and this year 20 of Aotearoa’s best breweries will descend on Brewtown to pour every style you could possibly desire, from crisp Lagers, cold IPAs and hoppy Pale Ales, to funky Sours, smoky Porters, rich Pastry Stouts – and everything in between!

Visit Brewtown’s own custom Unimog Bar for 13 beers handpicked by our craft beer experts, but get in quick – we only have 1 keg of each, and they won’t last long!

🍸Haunting Spirits & Wine!
New for this year! Step into our haunted cocktail bar for some seriously spooky spirits! Our mixologists have been hard at work creating some devilishly delicious drops just for you.

We’re bringing in some of the best wines the Wairarapa has to offer, so whether you prefer a crisp Sauv, a fruity Pinot or a refreshing Frosé there will be something to enjoy.

Still not enough for you? Then head down to the Everything Else Bar, for NZ crafted ciders, as well as bourbon, rum, gin and vodka alongside a range of low and no alcohol beers, cocktails and soft drinks.

🌮Monstrously Good Kai!
The NZBF food court hosts the region’s top foodies, bringing you big, bold, flavour-packed festival eats that’ll satisfy both the living and the undead.
Watch this space as we announce our festive foodie lineup!

⚔️Enthralling Entertainment!
Lose yourself in a cabaret of the bizarre, where the performances are as dark as they are dazzling. Expect sultry burlesque, twisted characters and moments that will thrill, chill, and have you begging for more.

Witness the brutal thrill of buhurt armoured combat, as warriors face off in full medieval kit in a raw display of might and mayhem.

Dance the day away at the main stage, as Welly’s top talent smash out tune after tune, all building up to a headline show from Brewtown favourites, The Blueprint – as they bring a special set just for Monster Mash! Trust us, you haven’t seen them like this before…

Grab your mates and get stuck in at the Games Zone, where a bit of friendly rivalry meets festive fun, and the loser buys the next round.

Enjoy dressing up? Go all out in your most terrifying, theatrical or jaw-dropping costume — and you might just take out the crown in our epic Best Dressed Competition, with spooky-good prizes up for grabs.

With affordable return coaches from Welly and Petone and great discounts for group tickets it's scarily easy to get involved!
🎃 R18

description:
Summer Camp Closing Party! | Dillon Jo, Miles Calder & Keira Batten Coogan, San Fran Summer Camp is back for 2026!

Join us and some of Te Whanganui-a-Tara’s finest artists over five nights across January and February with tickets from $10 or a season pass for $40, featuring drink specials and cocktails thanks to our sponsor Jameson.

Here it is… our final night! Featuring not one, not two, but THREE of Pōneke’s premier acts to close our 2026 series out.

Dillon Jo is the genre defying project of Wellington Based artists Betty Smith and Georgie Levien. Dillon Jo built paths between the elements of dream pop, shoegaze, folk, alt rock, and indie pop to create their genre defying sound. Having recently recruited Kahu Sanson-Burnett and Ruadh Munro, Dillon Jo has transformed their live sets into an unbridled big-sound shoegaze experience.
Miles Calder released his solo debut album Autopilot Life in 2021, a self-reflective delve into waking up from an unexamined life, dressed in a psychedelic folk-rock palette that evoked late-era Beatles as much as contemporaries like Kevin Morby and Father John Misty. The album won over new fans and critics alike & led to a nomination for a prestigious Aotearoa Music Award (Te Kaipuoro Taketake Toa | Best Folk Artist 2022). 

Keira Batten Coogan writes raw and messy music for alien listeners. Joined onstage by some familiar Pōneke talent, his earnest earworms pack more punch than ever. Themes of queerness, growing pains, love, and nostalgia shine through his lyricism and captivates audiences. 


San Fran holds a Supervised License, meaning minors are welcome to attend events at San Fran so long as they are accompanied by a legal guardian at all times whilst at the venue. Minors are of course not allowed to purchase alcohol under any circumstance.

description:
Country Day Party: Wellington 🤠, Country Day Party: Wellington 🤠
Get your cowboys hats ready!
Join us for an unforgettable day filled with the chart-topping tracks of Morgan Wallen, Dolly Parton, and many more, combining the best of the old school and modern country hits. Whether you’re a die-hard country fan or just looking for good vibes, this is the ultimate party. 🤠🌵💃What to Expect:
🎶 Nonstop Modern Country Hits: Sing along to all your favorite songs by artists like Luke Combs, and Shania Twain.

📷 Instagram-Worthy Moments: Capture the day with scenic backdrops and photo ops.
🌟 Good Times and Great Tunes: Soak in the feel-good energy with a mix of country charm and party spirit.

Perfect for Stag/Hen/Birthday parties or if you just fancy a day out with all your mates! 🪩

Expect to hear music from:Johnny Cash ⭐️ Dolly Parton ⭐️ Luke Combs ⭐️ Morgan Wallen ⭐️ Garth Brooks ⭐️ Willie Nelson ⭐️ Chris Stapleton ⭐️ Hank Williams ⭐️ Patsy Cline ⭐️ John Denver ⭐️ Shania Twain ⭐️ Shaboozey ⭐️ Kenny Rogers ⭐️ Tim McGraw ⭐️ Faith Hill ⭐️ Keith Urban ⭐️ Carrie Underwood ⭐️ Blake Shelton ⭐️ Lainey Wilson ⭐️ Luke Bryan ⭐️ Billy Ray Cyrus⭐️ Megan Moroney ⭐️ Kacey Musgraves ⭐️ Eric Church ⭐️ Zach Bryan

FAQ’S
🗣️ Why do the tickets come in different price tiers?
Whilst there is no physical difference. There is only a limited amount of tickets we can sell. Therefore we operate a festival style ticketing model. The earlier you buy your ticket the cheaper it will be.
🗣️ Do you do group discount? Stag/Hen etc
Yes, we offer a discounted ticket price for groups of 10 or more. These tickets can be found listed as the “Big Group Package!” above, orders for these tickets are restricted to a minimum of 10.
🗣️ Will there be more tickets available if the event is sold out?
Once this event is sold out, there will not be any additional tickets released. In addiction to this we will be unable to sell them at the door due to maximum capacity and health and safety regulations.
🗣️ Is the venue accessible?
Our events take place at a variety of locations across the UK and Ireland. As each varies please ensure to check the specific venue's website for accessibility information.
🗣️ Can I resell my tickets?
Yes, if you are unable to attend an event you are welcome to sell your tickets on. We do not use names on tickets as a condition for entry.
🗣️ Are your events 18+?
All of our events are strictly 18+. A valid photographic ID may be requested on entry.
🗣️ I have more questions?
If you have additional questions and wish to contact us directly, don’t hesitate to drop us a DM on either Facebook (Daytime Disco) or Instagram (@daytimediscoevents)
Refund Policy:
In-line with normal consumer standards for ticket purchases, tickets for Daytime Disco events are non-refundable, on the condition that we not moved the event or canceled it. If you are no longer be able to attend the event, you are of course welcome to gift or sell your tickets onwards as we do not use names on the tickets as a condition of entry.

description:
Irish Set Dancing - day of dance 2026, Does Irish music get your feet tapping?  This is your opportunity to get up and dance. 
Set dances are the traditional social dances of Ireland, and are so much fun to dance.
 No experience necessary.  Suitable for beginners and experienced alike.   You just need a bit of coordination, a sense of fun, and some energy.  (This is not Irish step dancing, not Riverdance!)
You can come along without a partner, there will always be someone to dance with.
Age 10 upwards.
We recommend smooth soled shoes if you have them.
 Experienced teacher Helen Stonehouse will guide you through the moves and figures.  There are 2 workshops on Saturday and on Saturday evening we have a Ceili when we'll dance some of what you learnt in the workshops plus some more Irish ceili dances.
Anyone is welcome to the Ceili without having been to the workshops.
 Our amazing Round The House Ceili Band will be playing all the jigs and reels and polkas for us throughout the weekend.
 Good music, fun and friends.

description:
Brew Year's Eve, Wellingtons biggest New Years Eve party is back at Brewtown.
Celebrate Brew Years Eve with the ultimate party night of legendary Kiwi anthems, wall to wall bangers and classic 80s hits on a night where the dancefloor never stops.

A New Years Eve celebration like no other, back by popular demand Dragon and The Jordan Luck Band, joined by The Automatic 80s & The Blueprint smashing out all the big party tunes you know & love.

The Soundtrack to Your Night
Our epic lineup will take you back in time with all your classic favourites:
Dragon — get ready for timeless anthems like April Sun in Cuba and Rain.
The Jordan Luck Band — Kiwi sing-alongs like Why Does Love Do This to Me and Victoria.
The Automatic 80s — taking you back with the biggest 80s dancefloor hits.
The Blueprint — Wellington's best rock covers band taking you through to midnight.

Say goodbye to 2025 with a bang — party with us as we transform Brewtown's covered Greenspace into a full-blown New Year’s Eve Party, packed with live music, massive party vibes, and all the magic to make your countdown unforgettable.

Wednesday 31st December
Brewtown, Upper Hutt
R18

Tickets:
$65 Early Bird tickets on September 9th (one day only). - Register now for exclusive access!
General Admission on sale September 10th.

This isn’t just another night out — it’s Wellington’s biggest party of the year!

So round up your crew, grab your tickets, and get ready for confetti blasts, big sing-alongs, and festival-scale fun as we count down to midnight together.

description:
Open Air @ Carlucci Land, Open Air @ Carlucci Land
Open Air are excited to return to a day/night adventure at the weird and wonderful Carlucci Land in Happy Valley.

We are planning a fully immersive production again with an amazing range of artists.


Main stage:

3-430pm K-oTik
430-6pm NotMySister
6-730pm Everybody
730-9pm Donna-Marie
9-1030pm Greg Churchill
1030-12am Takas


2nd Stage to be announced soon!


Our friends from Cosa Nostra food truck will be back serving fresh pizzas and also have a range of soft drinks available.


Kids under 16yo are free as well as the mini golf for all attendees!


Please note no alcohol will be allowed, you are however welcome to bring your own food and beverage refreshments, NO glass is allowed please. There will also be multiple water stations free for use.


The #29 Metlink Bus route will be running on the hour with the last bus leaving Happy Valley around 10pm. We will also contact the taxi companies to get them to provision taxis for the event closure.


We will not be offering ticket refunds or swaps for this event, if you can no longer make the event, you can on-sell your own ticket - buyers please be sure about who you are buying from as there are lots of scammers around!


Consent is always required at any event to ensure that everyone is kept safe and boundaries are not crossed. Please ask before any hugging, touching of any sort, taking photos, etc. We operate a zero-tolerance policy to bad behaviour. If you see something that doesn’t seem right, you have a responsibility to do something. And remember, if it’s not an enthusiastic YES then it’s a NO.


Open Air reserve all rights and have a zero-tolerance approach.


Ngā mihi

Open Air

description:
Greytown Apple Harvest Festival 2026, Molewood Orchard is hosting the third annual Greytown Apple Harvest Festival on Easter Saturday, with the support of Greytown Country Village Heaven. Bring your family and come along for a day celebrating all things apple!

Entry to the orchard is free. The festival will be held in the orchard's idyllic picnic area, Molewood Meadows.

Pick your own apples (and possibly pears), carriage rides, face painting, food trucks, stalls, apple baking, cider and fruit wine samples, live music, games and activities for kids.

Please bring your own bags or buckets to pick into.

Access to the orchard is at 17 Mole St Greytown, or else from our “secret” door in the shelterbelt at the end of Farley Ave (this area has plenty of parking and is closest to the centre of Greytown).

Follow us on Facebook or Instagram for further details, or check out our website.

The rain day is Easter Monday, 6 April.
