description:
Rathkeale College Christmas Fête 2025, Rathkeale College Christmas Fête 2025
Welcome to the Rathkeale Christmas Fête 2025!
Now in its 22nd year, the Fête offers shoppers loads of inspiration. Stallholders will be selling festive decorations, clothing, arts and crafts, as well as flowers, toys and plants – there is something for everyone.
Gourmet food is also a highlight – with freshly made gourmet burgers, lamb & mint sausages, wraps, curry, salads, and the ever-popular berries and icecream available for purchase. Devonshire Tea served in the Rathkeale Homestead rose garden is a time-honoured tradition while refreshing cold drinks can be sipped on as visitors listen to live music performed by students at various times throughout the day. The popular home baking stall features again this year, and visitors are encouraged to get in early as the Christmas cakes, in particular, often sell out by midday.
The beauty of this Fête is it is a day out for the whole family. In addition to the 120 plus indoor and outdoor stalls, there is plenty to entertain the children with face painting and three magic shows during the day to round out the fun.
Visitors can meander around the beautiful campus and enjoy the unique surroundings including the school gardens designed by Alfred Buxton, the Greek Amphitheatre and the 3km eco-trail which weaves through large stands of native trees, including ancient kahikatea.
The Christmas Fête is the school’s biggest fundraiser of the year and comes about thanks to an amazing collaboration between the Friends of Rathkeale College, staff and students.
Koha/gold coin entry
Eftpos available but we strongly recommend bringing cash.
More information: https://rathkeale.school.nz/community/#friends-of-rathkeale
label: Festivals

description:
A Celtic Christmas by A Taste of Ireland, “Jingle Jigs, Reels a Treat.”
Ken Longworth – The Newcastle Herald

Direct from its Off-Broadway season, A Taste of Ireland - The Irish Music & Dance Sensation, presents its holiday spectacular, A Celtic Christmas. Prepare to be enchanted with some of the world’s finest Irish dancers and musicians bringing the spirit of Christmas to life, in a show that will make you laugh, cry, and sing into the night.

After captivating audiences across the USA and around the world, A Celtic Christmas by A Taste of Ireland returns to New Zealand this festive season following a highly successful 2022 tour. Experience the magic as world-class performers blend melodic folk mash-ups, acapella tap battles, and heartwarming storytelling in one truly unforgettable performance.

Featuring former World Irish dance champions and stars from Lord of the Dance and Riverdance, A Celtic Christmas delivers a holiday spectacular packed with Irish wit, traditional tunes and heartfelt ballads, alongside all your favorite carols. The show’s exciting mix of electrifying rhythms and extraordinary talent promises an evening brimming with Irish charm and enthralling entertainment, all in one powerhouse event.

Immerse yourself in a traditional Celtic landscape and let the magic of an Irish Christmas transport you to a world filled with excitement and wonder, where vibrant music and dance embody the heartbeat of the festive season.

With over one hundred and fifty 5-star Facebook reviews and over 150,000 followers, A Celtic Christmas by A Taste of Ireland promises 100% live singing and dancing as it bridges the gap between energetic folk music, Irish dance, and modern tap styles.

Don’t miss your chance to see this magical show as it tours New Zealand for a strictly limited season. Get your tickets now before they sell out.

**Musical instruments are subject to change from performance to performance

description:
80's Day Disco: Wellington 🪩, 80's Day Disco: Wellington 🪩
Tickets selling fast → https://www.fatsoma.com/e/vb6j5pqr/la/qwmu

Get Ready to Groove at the Ultimate 80s Day Disco! 🎉

Step into a time machine and relive the magic of the 80s with a day filled with the biggest disco anthems and synth-powered hits of the decade. Whether you’re a true 80s fanatic or just here for the nostalgia, this party is all about electric vibes, funky beats, and non-stop dancing! 🎧🕺✨

What to Expect:

🎶 Nonstop 80s Classics: Dance to the timeless hits that defined the decade—from disco grooves to rock anthems and synth-pop bangers.

🕺 Retro Dance Floor Energy: Show off your best moves under the dazzling glow of neon lights and shimmering disco balls.

📸 Totally Rad Photo Ops: Capture the moment with throwback props, vintage backdrops, and all the 80s glam.

🌟 Feel-Good Vibes All Day Long: Celebrate the music, the energy, and the nostalgia with great people and iconic tunes.

Let’s make it a day to remember—80s style! 🎶💿✨

Expect to hear music from:

Michael Jackson ⭐ Madonna ⭐ Prince ⭐ Whitney Houston ⭐ Queen ⭐ George Michael ⭐ David Bowie ⭐ Tina Turner ⭐ Elton John ⭐ Phil Collins ⭐ Cyndi Lauper ⭐ Bon Jovi ⭐ The Police ⭐ Bruce Springsteen ⭐ Duran Duran ⭐ Journey ⭐ Billy Joel ⭐ Lionel Richie ⭐ Eurythmics ⭐ Depeche Mode ⭐ Aerosmith ⭐ U2 ⭐ The Cure ⭐ Van Halen ⭐ Hall & Oates ⭐ AC/DC ⭐ The Smiths ⭐ New Order ⭐ Fleetwood Mac ⭐ The Bangles ⭐ Rick Astley ⭐ Spandau Ballet ⭐ Roxette ⭐ Simple Minds ⭐ Guns N’ Roses ⭐ Billy Idol ⭐ Tears for Fears ⭐ Bryan Adams ⭐ A-ha

FAQ’S

🗣️ Why do the tickets come in different price tiers?

Whilst there is no physical difference. There is only a limited amount of tickets we can sell. Therefore we operate a festival style ticketing model. The earlier you buy your ticket the cheaper it will be.

🗣️ Do you do group discount? Stag/Hen etc

Yes, we offer a discounted ticket price for groups of 10 or more. These tickets can be found listed as the “Big Group Package!” above, orders for these tickets are restricted to a minimum of 10.

🗣️ Will there be more tickets available if the event is sold out?

Once this event is sold out, there will not be any additional tickets released. In addiction to this we will be unable to sell them at the door due to maximum capacity and health and safety regulations.

🗣️ Is the venue accessible?

Our events take place at a variety of locations across the UK and Ireland. As each varies please ensure to check the specific venue's website for accessibility information.

🗣️ Can I resell my tickets?

Yes, if you are unable to attend an event you are welcome to sell your tickets on. We do not use names on tickets as a condition for entry.

🗣️ Are your events 18+?

All of our events are strictly 18+. A valid photographic ID may be requested on entry.

🗣️ I have more questions?

If you have additional questions and wish to contact us directly, don’t hesitate to drop us a DM on either Facebook (Daytime Disco) or Instagram (@daytimediscoevents)

Refund Policy:
In-line with normal consumer standards for ticket purchases, tickets for Daytime Disco events are non-refundable, on the condition that we not moved the event or canceled it. If you are no longer be able to attend the event, you are of course welcome to gift or sell your tickets onwards as we do not use names on the tickets as a condition of entry.

Lunar New Year: Tea Ceremony Demonstration With Wellington Xiamen Asso, Ring in the Lunar New Year with a joyful celebration! Members of the Wellington Xiamen Association will host a special tea ceremony demonstration, sharing the artistry, history, and cultural significance of tea from Xiamen, Wellington’s sister city, and home to some of China’s finest teas.

This is a chance to pause, connect, and experience the beauty of tea culture in a welcoming setting. Wander through, listen to stories, and soak up the atmosphere as we mark the beginning of the Year of the Horse.

The Wellington Xiamen Association works to strengthen ties between our two cities through cultural exchange, education, and collaboration.

Saturday 21 February | 1pm–2:30pm

Te Waka Huia Wellington Museum

description:
Céilí with Round the House Céilí Band, Céilí with Round the House Céilí Band
Does Irish music get your feet tapping? This is your opportunity to get up and dance to some cracking tunes at our evening Céilí on Saturday 18th of April.

Come to St Michael’s Hall in Kelburn to meet new people and enjoy a great time in a friendly and welcoming atmosphere. No dancing experience needed, just a bit of coordination, some energy, and a big sense of fun!

Experienced caller Helen Stonehouse will guide you through all the dances. Our amazing Round The House Céilí Band, featuring Roger Stonehouse, Em Griffiths, and Thomas Stonehouse, will be playing lively jigs and reels and polkas for us.

Handy information:
- Dancing starts at 7:30pm
- Age 10 upwards. Under 16s accompanied by an adult
- Bring a water bottle, hydration is key!
- Parking is limited, we recommend catching the bus. Routes 4, 18, and 21 all stop right next to the hall.
- If you’re unwell then please stay home this time, we’ll see you at the next one

Door sales may be available on the day (cash only): General Admission $30, Unwaged $20.
Pro tip - online tickets will be cheaper!

This event is part of our Set Dance day, and there will be workshops running during the day. Set dances are the traditional social dances of Ireland, and are so much fun to dance. Have a look here to see what it's all about: https://www.facebook.com/reel/1062173322541491.
If you’re interested, head over to Humanitix to get a full day package and fill your weekend with dancing!

description:
Wellington Folk Festival 2025 - our 60th!, Welcome to Wellyfest!
The Wellington Folk Festival is held every Labour Weekend, celebrated on the fourth weekend of October, at Brookfield Outdoor Education Centre – a beautiful site owned by the Scouts at 562 Moores Valley Road, Wainuiomata, Lower Hutt, New Zealand.
The Festival gate is open from 3pm on the Friday, with events running over Friday evening, all day Saturday through to Sunday evening. You can come for the weekend, for a day, half day or an evening. Children (0 - 12) can attend free.
Weekend passes entitle the holders to camp onsite and there are a limited number of bunks available for sale as well.
PLEASE NOTE - ALL EARLYBIRD PRICING CLOSES AT 11.55PM ON SUNDAY 28TH SEPTEMBER. Full-price tickets can be purchased on-line until Sunday 19th October.
After ticket sales close on Humanitix, you can purchase at the gate, from 3pm on Friday 25th of October.  Weekend tickets, day tickets and evening tickets are all available at the gate.  There may even be some bunks available still.  You can email registrations@wellingtonfolkfestival.org.nz to find out.
To read our latest news click here, click the Latest News tab in the menu above, or check out our facebook page here www.facebook.com/Wellyfest/.
If you would like to be added to our email list, sign up to our mailing list here.

description:
Discover Te Muna - Go Behind the Scenes, Join us for a full-day immersive experience in the viticulture, winemaking and wines of Te Muna Valley. Walk the vineyards, meet the producers, go behind the scenes and taste exceptional wines right where they are made.

Tickets are limited to twenty people to ensure that this is an intimate experience, taking you to places that are not normally open to the public.

Your tour begins with pickup from Martinborough Square, leading to a special start at Big Sky Wines. Kath and Jeremy will take you through a hands-on experience of their vineyard and winery. From there, visit the stunning Blue Earth Estate vineyard and olive grove. Margaret and Mike will entertain you with stories of their journey and explain how they grow their olives and grapes. You will then indulge in a long, relaxed lunch accompanied with a glass of wine. Some of the region's winemakers will join us to share their stories and answer your questions. The adventure continues with an insiders' look at the state of the art winery at Escarpment. Rowan will walk you through the process of how grapes are transformed into their wines. This will be followed by an exploration of the beautiful Craggy Range vineyard with Anton. The day will conclude with the opportunity to revisit your favourite wines of the day overlooking Te Muna from Craggy Range's gorgeous private venue.

Santa Cipolla, “Holy Onion”. For the entire month, Cicio Cacio Osteria is getting brave and bold, celebrating the magic of vegetables in all their glory, especially the santa cipolla (aka the holy onion). 
Vegetables bring colour, life and joy. In Italy, they mark the rhythm of the seasons, stir up cherished memories and signal the excitement of fresh beginnings. Come along and experience vibrant flavours with playful snacks, fun street food, freshly baked breads, handmade pasta and a brand new range of dishes that celebrate the beauty, bounty and simplicity of Italian cuisine.

description:
Zwanze Day, GP is thrilled to be the exclusive host of Zwanze Day for New Zealand in 2026!

We’ll be tapping Cantillon’s Zwanze beers at the Wild Workshop in Wellington on 25 April, alongside a primo selection of beers from the GP cellar - an event this prestigious calls for the best, and we’re pulling out all the stops.

There are two ways you can join Zwanze Day 2026 at the Wild Workshop:

Get tickets to a guided tasting, hosted by Pete Gillespie and Dave Bell (Wild Workshop Head Brewer).

This session will run for 90 minutes from 1pm. Included in the ticket is a guided tasting of 9 beers (the two 2026 Zwanze beers, a few other Cantillon treats and some of GP’s best from the cellar), food to match and a tour of the Wild Workshop brewery. There are limited spots - get in quick.

Come by the Wild Workshop from 2.30pm for freestyle tasting.

Free entry, open for spontaneous walk-ins (get it). Pay-as-you-go to work your way through the line-up, or get a Zwanze flight, with some delish food to boot.

Zwanze Day happens but once every two years. It’s a global beer event run by renowned Belgian brewery Cantillon; they make a unique, one-off beer especially for the event, and it is tapped simultaneously at select venues across almost two-dozen countries. The Wild Workshop is Cantillon's exclusive partner in New Zealand.

This is the event for lovers of craft beer at its finest.
label: Food & Drink

description:
Mystery Feast on Mischief Night! Halloween dinner with Chef Max Gordy 🎃 Supporting Kaibosh., Mystery Feast on Mischief Night! Halloween dinner with Chef Max Gordy 🎃 Supporting Kaibosh.
🎃 Step inside, if you dare... on the eve of Halloween. It's a Mischief Night!
👻A chilling murder mystery unfolds over a hauntingly good three-course Halloween dinner by Chef Max Gordy!

On 30 October, gather your cleverest friends and find your darkest ghoulish attire 🧛‍♀️ Who will crack the case first: the Detectives or the Villains? 🕵️‍♂️
Expect twists, laughter, and a touch of fright 💀 with spot prizes through the night — plus a reward for the best-dressed.

👩‍🍳 Award-winning chef Max Gordy ( @grazewinebar ) will conjure up a frightfully delicious Halloween-themed feast.
🥂 Your ticket includes one complimentary alcoholic beverage (with more available to purchase on the night).
🙌 Every ticket sold helps Kaibosh ensure that no good food is left to die.

TICKET TYPES
Bring a team of six to solve the mystery and devour a fiendishly good feast for $666.
Have a frightening four-some? Book a table for $444.
Spooky single seats available for $111... we will seat you at a mystery table...

VENUE
60 Dixon Street is up a flight of steep steps – with only one way in.

What is Mischief Night?
In parts of Pennsylvania and New Jersey in the eastern USA, 30 October is called “Mischief Night.” The tradition refers to the eve of Halloween in which children and teenagers celebrate the holiday one night early with pranks, tricks and more. While in New England, some call it Cabbage Night...
Join us to experience Chef Max Gordy's culinary take on Mischief Night in Pōneke!

#Halloween #HalloweenNZ #MischiefNight #MaxGordy #EveybodyEats #CharityDinner #FundraisingDinner #WellingtonDining #Pōneke #WellingtonNZ

description:
END of year PARTY, Our last night of the year is here, and we’re going out with a bang! Come for the food, stay for the vibes, and leave with your heart (and belly) full.

Good live kirtan & dance(mantra music), tasty plant-based eats, and all the energy. Come hungry, come curious, and let’s send 2025 off with big smiles :)

Special guest:
We’ve got Devamrita Swami in the house—travelling monk, author, and life coach—dropping some wisdom. He mixes practical wisdom with modern life hacks on happiness, sustainability, and how to actually make sense of life today. Expect insights, laughs, and ideas that stick—good stuff for the mind.


Plant-based Menu:
* Juicy BBQ Jackfruit Burgers

* KFC-style Cauliflower

* Paprika Hot Chips & Tartar Sauce

* Crisp Garden Salad with a Creamy Sunflower Dressing

* Ice Cream & Mini Doughnut Sundaes


Deets:
* Sunday, 14th Dec

* Kicks off 6:00 pm, doors open 5:45 pm

* Bhakti Lounge, 1/175 Vivian Street

description:
Love Language, Calling budding poets and hopeless romantics - this is your opportunity to share your love of language, with a food and poetry degustation. 
This four-course dinner is a physical manifestation of love and an ode to food. Join Simon Sweetman, Wellington writer, critic, musician and poet, as he reads some of his own poetry, and yours too if you're feeling game. Chefs Tim and Aaron Banks will lovingly create a four-course dinner with ingredients locally sourced and lovingly produced by small scale sustainable farmers... and perhaps they will read their own poems too. 
If you're feeling poetic bring along your own poem specially crafted around this year's festival theme 'Food is Love'. It can be as long or short as you like, as happy or sad, comical or tragic, Keats or Bukowski. Spot prizes for various categories including 'Most Related to the Theme' and 'Most Obviously Foodie' poems. Includes four courses, and a welcome glass of bubbles on arrival.

description:
Maille Sunday Kai, Escape the winter chill and head along to Cuba St Tavern for some Sunday Kai with Maille Mustard, running every Sunday of August. Each week features a different delectable roast, served with either a Maille Dijon Bloody Mary or warming mulled wine, and finished with a house-made dessert. Sunday has never tasted so good! Bookings available from 1pm.

$50 for 2 courses
$65 for 2 courses inc a Maille Bloody Mary or House made Mulled wine

Menu
Every Maille Sunday Kai event will offer a vegetarian option of eggplant parmigiana with buffalo mozzarella, Maille Dijon mustard, tomato ragù, garlic potato and broccolini.

3 August
Free-range roast porchetta served with garlic potato, creamed spinach, broccolini, Yorkshire pudding, Maille wholegrain mustard jus and apple sauce.
Dessert: Sticky toffee pudding with custard.

10 August
Roast duck breast served with duck fat roast potatoes, Chinese broccoli, mushrooms, Yorkshire pudding, and Maille Dijon mustard and onion cream.
Dessert: Dark chocolate torte with orange mascarpone.

17 August
Conscious Valley roast lamb shoulder with garlic roast potatoes, honey-glazed carrots, minted peas, Yorkshire pudding, Maille wholegrain mustard jus and mint sauce.
Dessert: Bread and butter pudding with ginger gelato and crème anglaise.

24 August
Half a roast chicken with duck fat roast potatoes, garlic green beans, cavolo nero, Yorkshire pudding, chicken schmaltz and Maille mustard gravy.
Dessert: Steamed honey pudding with custard and honey gelato.

31 August
First Light roast sirloin served with honey-glazed carrots, duck fat roast potatoes, cabbage and bacon, Yorkshire pudding, horseradish, Maille Dijon mustard and jus.
Dessert: Chocolate fondant with vanilla bean gelato.

description:
THE WAY OF TEA, The Way of Tea is a traditional Japanese art—a ritual that embodies the mindful practice of harmony and tranquility in everyday life. Our Herbal Tea Ritual is our interpretation of the Way of Tea, infused with the therapeutic benefits of herbal medicine, bringing traditional knowledge into the modern world. You will be expertly guided by our knowledgeable herbalists in the art of mindful herbal tea blending.
You will discover the therapeutic qualities and unique tastes of nine medicinal herbs, focusing on their calming, grounding, uplifting, healing, balancing, and energising properties. From these, you will select your favourites to blend into your own unique tisane—a medicinal infusion.
Throughout the workshop, you will take time to reflect on the tastes, sensations, and effects of each herb as you infuse and taste your new herbal blend. You will leave with a tranquil mind, 50g of your custom tisane, a stainless steel infuser, and the embodied experience of this mindful ritual to invoke each time you brew your blend.
To complement your tea tasting, enjoy a selection of handcrafted bites from the Oatery.
When 11am, Saturday 7th February, duration 1 Hours.

description:
Taste & Create - A Painting with Sugar Valentine Affair, Taste and Create a palette of passion at the historic Inverlochy house. Join us in indulging in hand-crafted pastries by the renowned Maxine Schecter of Sugar Flour while working with Inverlochy's skilled tutors to create a piece of art in your choice of medium (drawing, printmaking, pastel painting, ceramics). Pastries are paired with refined tea and non-alcoholic bubbles.

The exact pastries are going to be a surprise (we'll drop hints along the way in the lead up to the event)! So pastries pictured are not what you'll get. The pastries will be incredibly seasonal and fruit focused (if anyone has any fruit allergies they’re unsure of you can can contact Maxine directly before booking). Maxine can also offer gluten free and gelatine free options for the dietaries too.

About the team:

Maxine Schecter is a French trained pastry chef who has worked in restaurants and patisseries in Wellington (Logan Brown), Paris (Gâteaux Thoumieux), London (The Fat Duck and Sketch) and is the author of Patisserie Made Simple: The Art of Petits Gateau.

Inverlochy Art School offers a broad range of high quality art classes and workshops, taught by a community of enthusiastic and talented practicing artists all year round. We believe in art for art's sake and allow students to find freedom in creative exploration.

The Enchanted Ale Quarter, Enter the magical realm of The Enchanted Ale Quarter, a fairytale-inspired pop-up tavern where whimsy, wonder and craft beer collide.

Step into a storybook setting, with twinkling lights, woodland decor, and forest-fantasy touches around every corner. Feast on gnome and fairy-themed comfort food like the solstice salad, loaded forest fries, sirens & ships fish & chips, and a decadent dark forest brownie, all paired with enchanted brews and mythical tipples.

Cosy, curious, and otherworldly, The Enchanted Ale Quarter brings a little magic to Wellington’s winter nights. Dust off your wings and join us for a pint, some sustenance or a potion, no quest required.

description:
Hōkai Tahi Winter Woodlands Pizza Evening, We’re inviting you to a cozy night out with heart.
Our Winter Woodlands Pizza Evening is a special fundraiser supporting Hōkai Tahi.
Based in the Wairarapa, Hōkai Tahi walks alongside women and whānau through pregnancy, baby loss, and the early years of parenting - offering time, space, and resources when they are needed most.
Indulge in a delicious 3-course meal (wood-fired pizza included, of course!) with non-alcoholic drinks provided.
A limited bar menu will be available, or feel free to BYO.
Alongside your meal, enjoy live music, a live auction of locally donated treasures, and for those feeling adventurous, a short glow worm walk through the native bush (don’t forget your torch, sturdy shoes, and warm layers - hot water bottles welcome!).
Details:
Watermill Bakery, 33 Blakes Road, Mt Holdsworth
Saturday 1st November 2025
5:30–8:30pm
$75 per adult - $20 per child (under 14)
Look out for our friendly parking warden in hi-vis on arrival and simply give your name to be guided in.
Please note there’s no reception at the venue, so make any arrangements before you head our way.
Share an evening of great food, live entertainment, and woodland magic - all in support of a truly worthwhile cause.
Every ticket sold helps Hōkai Tahi continue supporting local whānau through life’s most tender moments.

description:
🇳🇿🇩🇪Kiwi Kölsch Service!🇩🇪🇳🇿, 🇳🇿🇩🇪Kiwi Kölsch Service!🇩🇪🇳🇿
Kölsch Service is BACK!! But this time with a kiwi twist to celebrate FRESH HOP SEASON!! Frischë Dösen Kölsch is made with fresh Wakatū hops, straight off the bine in Nelson, which we'll be serving the traditional way on Saturday 11th of April - just like in Köln.

What's Kölsch service?
It's kinda like beer yum cha but there's only one thing on the menu - 200ml glasses of Kölsch bier! 🍺
We keep track of your tab by marking the edge of your coaster and when your glass is empty - voilà, another glass appears! 🪄 If you're done, just pop your coaster on top of your glass to indicate no more.
You can order food and non-alcoholics the same way, just take a seat and everything will come to you. 🪑

🇳🇿Special KIWI Menu!🇳🇿
We'll be serving up distinctively kiwi snacks - like classic kiwi dip with Proper Crisps, Marmite & Cheese Garlic Flatbreads plus our usual offerings of Cheese Rolls and Cheerios and delicioius pizzas.

To make sure you get a table, book here: https://www.meandoses.co.nz/bookings


We'll be running Kölsch service from 12pm - 5pm on Saturday April 11th, come along and get the Köln experience! 🍻🥨🇩🇪

description:
Lunch Speaker Series, Noble Rot invites you to enjoy a relaxed lunch in style. While away your afternoon with a leisurely three-course menu as they keep the wine flowing amongst you and your friends. They've lined up two incredible guest speakers at the forefront of the wine industry to entertain you with their stories, knowledge and insights into current trends in a relaxed but intimate atmosphere. You'll wish all learning was done this way. It’s the perfect excuse to treat your friends, your mum or your date.
 
Guest Speakers & Dates:

Saturday 9th — ‘Small Region, Big Sky’
Katherine Jacobs and her husband Jeremy embarked on a dream to produce some of Martinborough’s finest wines from their special vineyard in Te Muna, and they’ve achieved great success! Katherine will speak about upcoming trends, big dreams and hidden secrets in our favourite region’s wine scene.
 
Saturday 23rd — ‘A Rosie Afternoon’ 
Enjoy the company of Rosie Finn of Neudorf Vineyards, second generation of one of New Zealand's most prestigious wineries in Nelson. You’ll have the opportunity to ask Rosie all about current trends as she combines the story of her family winery’s historic roots and its impact on the landscape of New Zealand fine wines.
label: Food & Drink

description:
I-Sarn Thai KanTok, Join Daeng from Tuk Tuk Thai for a deeply personal culinary journey to the heart of E-Sarn, the north-eastern region of Thailand she once called home. This one-night-only event celebrates the traditional Kan-Tok style of dining – a communal meal served on handwoven bamboo trays and shared amongst family and friends. This event is made to be shared, the ticket price is for a shared menu for two. 

Growing up in the small town of Tha Bo near the Mekong Delta, Daeng’s childhood was filled with stories of rice farming, village generosity, and dishes cooked from what the garden offered. In E-Sarn, meals were acts of love, shared daily around the Kan-Tok with laughter, music, and warmth.

Now, Daeng invites you to gather at her table and experience the flavours of her upbringing. You’ll be served nine traditional dishes on a beautifully crafted bamboo tray, designed for sharing, just as they are in her village.

On the Kan-Tok:
Hor Mok Salmon – Steamed salmon in banana leaves, marinated with herbs, spices, and coconut milk
Mok Nor Mai with Pork Belly – Pork belly steamed with bamboo shoots
Larb Gai – A celebratory chicken mince salad with fresh herbs and toasted rice
E-Sarn Sausages – House-made, spiced and fermented in true north-eastern style
Nam Prik Tomato – Roasted tomato dipping sauce with chillies, garlic, and shallots
Som Tum – Green papaya salad with a sweet, sour, spicy kick
Sticky Rice – Soaked overnight and steamed in traditional bamboo baskets
Fresh and Steamed Seasonal Vegetables – To enjoy with sauces and mains

Dessert:
Kanom Chan – Thai layered jelly cake
Kanom Mun – Tapioca and coconut pudding

Pull up a seat at Daeng’s table and discover the stories, flavours, and traditions that shaped her childhood.
Single tickets or odd numbers unable to be catered to.
label: Food & Drink

description:
Wine Zen - A symphony for the senses, Wine Zen - A symphony for the senses
Take a breath and let your senses lead you. Join us for Wine Zen at Allan Scott Bistro at Allan Scott Family Winemakers – a sensory journey into mindfulness, connection, and the quiet beauty of wine.

We begin with a grounding meditation and explore five mindful techniques that awaken each of our senses before heading behind the scenes on a private tour with owner and winemaker Josh Scott.

The morning concludes in the Bistro with a guided sensory wine and food pairing that deepens our appreciation for flavour, texture, and the stories within each glass.

Spaces are limited! Reserve your place for a morning of calm, curiosity, and connection among the vines.

description:
A hāngi to remember, A hāngi to remember
Enjoy a delicious hāngi by P&S Kai, relax to live music and learn about the 1975 Land March coming to Porirua.

𝗖𝗮𝗹𝗹 𝟬𝟰 𝟮𝟯𝟳 𝟭𝟱𝟭𝟭 𝘁𝗼 𝗽𝗮𝘆 𝘄𝗶𝘁𝗵 𝗮 𝗰𝗿𝗲𝗱𝗶𝘁 𝗰𝗮𝗿𝗱 𝗼𝗿 𝘃𝗶𝘀𝗶𝘁 𝗣𝗮̄𝘁𝗮𝗸𝗮 𝗿𝗲𝗰𝗲𝗽𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝘁𝗼 𝗽𝗿𝗲-𝗽𝘂𝗿𝗰𝗵𝗮𝘀𝗲 𝘆𝗼𝘂𝗿 𝗵𝗮̄𝗻𝗴𝗶 𝗯𝘆 𝟯 𝗢𝗰𝘁𝗼𝗯𝗲𝗿. Be in quick! (Hāngi will not be available to purchase on the day)

$16 for a hāngi. Two options available:
Original: Chicken drumstick and pork belly, heaps of vegetables and stuffing.

50 years ago, Porirua welcomed thousands of marchers from the 1975 Land March. The marchers along with Dame Whine Cooper were welcomed with steaming delicious hangi and the legendary manaakitanga of Ngāti Toa and the Porirua Community before they delivered their message to Parliament.
To honour this significant time of unity and manaakitanga join us for ‘A hāngi to remember’.
Pick up your hangi, enjoy live music and visit the exhibition Not One More Acre: Remembering the 1975 Land March

https://pataka.org.nz/whats-on/events/a-hangi-to-remember/

description:
High Tea at Le Cordon Bleu | 29 NOVEMBER 2025, High Tea at Le Cordon Bleu | 29 NOVEMBER 2025
Experience an exquisite selection of the finest French Pâtisserie prepared by our highly creative & talented Diplôme de Pâtisserie students.

About this Event

Experience an exquisite selection of the finest French Pâtisserie prepared by our highly creative and talented Le Cordon Bleu New Zealand Diplôme de Pâtisserie students. Tickets are allocated for specific days and seating times, please ensure you arrive promptly at the time-slot you have booked for.
Kiwiana Edition: This High Tea is a speciality menu inspired by classic kiwi treats, enjoy a collection of old favourites with a Le Cordon Bleu spin.

Ticket Information:
IMPORTANT NOTE: Tables are arranged per order. If you purchase tickets separately you need to e-mail to advise which other ticket holders you wish to be seated with.
Date: Friday 29th November. (other sessions are also available, to purchase tickets for these events please click here and select your chosen date. Tickets are non-transferable, please ensure you purchase tickets for the correct date.)
Price: $45 per person includes High Tea and complimentary Tea and Coffee.
Booking Times: 11.30am | 12:00pm | 2:30pm | 3:00pm
Tickets are allocated for specific days and seating times, please ensure you arrive promptly at the time you have booked for.
The estimated time to enjoy the full Le Cordon Bleu High Tea experience is one hour. We invite you to sit back, relax and enjoy our students' collection of French Pâtisserie.
Dietary Requirements: Our menu is set and we are unable to make any menu alterations, or cater to any specific dietary requirements (including religious diets) or food allergies. We apologise for any inconvenience this may cause.



What is Brasserie Le Cordon Bleu?
Brasserie Le Cordon Bleu is our training restaurant where our students get practical experience in a real restaurant environment - all under the expert guidance of their chef lecturers. In the kitchen, we have students from our Diplôme de Pâtisserie programme. In the dining room we have our Bachelor of Culinary Arts and Business students serving you and putting their theory skills into practice.
Brasserie & High Tea Refund Policy
• No refunds are provided unless the event is cancelled by Le Cordon Bleu NZ.
• Customers can arrange for someone else to take their place.
label: Food & Drink

description:
Paint & Sip with Mandy Emerson, Enjoy a fabulous night of creativity and great company paired with delicious food & refreshments!

Mandy Emerson is an established NZ artist from Riversdale Beach in the Wairarapa. She started her paint workshops over a year ago and they have been such a success she continues to be fully booked out.

It is The Offering's absolute pleasure to host her here in Greytown. Enjoy a glass of bubbles on arrival, select a delicious dish from the curated menu and settle in for some creativity. The workshop will then pause for dinner.

Mandy's work is mixed media, feminine with florals and she has won awards for her works and exhibits all over New Zealand.

Her biggest success came from exhibiting at the NZ ART SHOW in Wellington which she still continues to be a part of and also attending ARTMELBOURNE in Australia. Visit her website www.mandyemerson.com

description:
Mana Taiohi - in Wellington, Mana Taiohi Workshop
Mana Taiohi is a principle based framework that informs the way people who work with young people work in Aotearoa. Mana Taiohi has evolved from the principles of youth development previously expressed in the Youth Development Strategy of Aotearoa (2002). It is informed by the voice of young people and people who work with young people, Aotearoa based literature into positive youth development, and a Te Ao Māori worldview.
Matt was born and raised in Te Awakairangi, Wellington and his ancestors are Ngāti Porou - Tikapa, Ngāti Kahungunu - Wairoa, Ngāti Ruapani - Waikaremoana, Āti Haunui-a-Pāpārangi - Pungarehu and Gujurat. He has been married to his wife Rachel since 2016 - she’s Ngā Puhi, Japanese, and English and their son, Mana Tuku Iho, was born in April 2024 after a 7-year fertility journey.
Matt has been involved in youth and community work since 2005 and co-founded the 'Te Awakairangi Youth Development Network' with a team of superstars. Matt is currently the Pouwhakamana for Zeal Youth Aotearoa, Āpiha Kaiwhakahaere for Ngā Kaihoe, and the Kaiāwhina of World Vision New Zealand.
In 2019, the whānau moved to Tāmaki Makaurau to study a Bachelor's in Indigenous Theology (Māori/Pasifika), a Diploma in Māori Leadership and a Diploma in Te Reo Māori, completing these qualifications in 2022. These studies have been transformational for both their personal growth and their work in youth and community development, changing the way they engage with the world. Through storytelling, making connections, and wānanga, they share these insights with the people they meet.
Now, with the arrival of Mana, they are back in Wellington, closer to friends and whānau. They are excited to continue their mission to see Aotearoa lifted up and our people thriving to their full potential.

description:
Dungeons & Dragons: Curse of Strahd Campaign - 26th January 2026, Dungeons & Dragons: Curse of Strahd Campaign - 26th January 2026
Outside the tavern, a fog lies across the town. The damp, cobbled pavement glistens as the lights of street lanterns dance across the slick stones. The night chills the bones and shivers the soul of anyone outside.

Yet inside these tavern walls the food is hearty, and the ale is warm and frothy. A fire blazes in the hearth, and the tavern is alive with the voices and laughter.

Suddenly, the tavern door swings open, and you all notice it at the same time. Framed by the lamp-lit fog, a form strides through the doorway. His brightly coloured clothes are draped in loose folds about him, and his hat hangs askew, hiding his eyes in shadows. Without hesitation, he walks up to your table and pulls from his tunic a sealed letter, addressed to all of you in beautiful flowing script. He drops the letter on the table and turns and leaves the tavern, vanishing into the fog.

‘Curse of Strahd’ is a D&D5E gothic horror campaign. It has everything you and your party needs to have a grand experience. It will take you to the mysterious village of Barovia, a realm surrounded by deadly fog and ruled by a wizard, the vampire Count Strahd von Zarovich, the master of Castle Ravenloft. ‘Curse of Strahd’ is a rich narrative story you are part of as it’s unfolding. It’s not just about fighting monsters and hunting for treasure in dungeons; there is a compelling story arc and a richly realized antagonist.

description:
Chicken Porridge with Shredded Chicken Salad | Cháo Gà cùng Gỏi Gà, Cháo gỏi gà is a comforting Vietnamese classic that pairs two contrasting elements on one table:
• Cháo gà – a light, nourishing rice porridge slowly cooked with chicken, ginger, and aromatics
• Gỏi gà – a refreshing shredded chicken salad with herbs, onion, and tangy dressing
Together, they reflect a very Vietnamese way of eating: balance, simplicity, and sharing. This dish is often enjoyed when families gather, when someone needs comfort, or when the weather calls for something gentle yet satisfying.
What You’ll Experience
• Learn how to cook smooth, flavourful chicken porridge from scratch
• Prepare a fresh Vietnamese shredded chicken salad with herbs and dressing
• Understand the cultural meaning of serving cháo and gỏi together
• Share a warm meal and stories around food, just like at home
Who This Is For
• Anyone curious about Vietnamese home cooking
• Families and community members looking for a relaxed, hands-on experience
• People who enjoy healthy, seasonal, and comforting food
No prior cooking experience needed. This is a welcoming, community-led workshop.
Ticket Information
• $10 per person – contributes to ingredient costs and facilitator effort
• Optional koha/donation welcome to support VHNZ Cuisine community programmes
Acknowledgements
With thanks to our community partners, volunteers, and supporters who make it possible for VHNZ Cuisine to continue sharing Vietnamese food, stories, and connection.

description:
Biochar Big Day Out 2025, Char curious? Keen gardener? Orchardist? Lifestyler? Climate Champion? BBQ enthusiast? 
Come one, come all to this one-off opportunity to spend a day making biochar in a magical orchard, forest and garden. Thank you Kapiti Coast District Council (KCDC) and Paekākāriki Orchards and Gardens (POG) for sponsoring this event so we can offer subsidised tickets as part of Sustainable Spring!
You will:

Morning sessions (9.30-12)...
Sipping coffee and learning the basics of Biochar. 
Getting hands-on exploring the property and learning to source good burn material (stuff you can find in your own backyard or lifestyle block).
Building a char toilet - great skills for civil defence emergencies, or just camping!
Ignite the Char-B-Que and BIG 600l kiln, learn how to feed the flames and trap the carbon.
Cooking using char!
At lunchtime (12-1)...
Enjoy a BBQ lunch cooked on the Char-B-Que. 
Learn all about the magic of biochar and how to use it to fight climate change, feed your garden. 
After lunch sessions (1-3)...
Quenching (pour cold water on) a kiln and processing char by drying and crushing it.
Making a home fireplace mini kiln using a biscuit tin (please bring a tin if you have one or koha for some extra we will source and bring from op shops).
Making a char water filter.
At the end of the day (3 pm)
Grab some biochar to take home (BYO container e.g. ice-cream tub or a small bucket
The big kiln will be quenched later in the day (around 4pm). For those keen to level up their learning on the kind of kilns used for large lifestyle blocks and small farms you are welcome to stay on and participate in a small advanced group dicussion and actvities between 3 and 4.30pm. 
Important things to note: 
Sustainability: 
We encourage carpooling to the event. Please bring your own reusable cup for hot drinks
Health and Safety:
 Please note there will be tree nuts and hazards onsite such as hot kilns and flames. So please take the necessary precautions by wearing non-flammable (cotton, wool, natural) materials and bring any personal medication that you need. We will have a health and safety volunteer onsite.

Family Friendly:
 It is a family-friendly event (kids under 18 are free) but they must be supervised by their parent or guardian at all times due to the hazards on site. 
Workshop Leaders 
John Wraight - CharPae 
Our CharPae Operations manager and ‘inhouse’ Biochar subject matter expert John Wraight has been making and using Biochar for many years and has delivered a number of successful workshops using these kilns. He is a BioChar Network member. He leads local CharPae burns, Char-B-Ques and kiln hire.
Holly Norton - CharPae 
Our CharPae Project manager has been making and using Biochar for the past 2 years. She co-leads these workshops and has additionally been exploring char use in sustainable construction and civil defence. She is currently on a mission to use Biochar to transform a tennis court into a thriving kai production space. She is this event's coordinator should you have any questions.

description:
Hands-on Sourdough Workshop, Sourdough for Beginners - $120 (paid via internet banking)
Come join us for a hands-on, in-person sourdough workshop at Willow Corner Collective headquarters (aka my home)! This workshop is currently the only hands-on sourdough workshop being offered in the Nelson region - I teach and demonstrate to you and then you get to mix and practice on your own dough as we go along.
Whether you're a seasoned baker or a beginner, this event is perfect for anyone looking to master the art of making sourdough bread, learning from someone who has 10+ years of experience. Perhaps you've found all the Youtube tutorials and Instagram how-to's overwhelming and are keen to learn via an in-person class. Then this is for you! I've baked hundreds of loaves in the last year alone, sold at a market, and hosted a pop-up shop. However, my passion really lies with teaching others to bake their own sourdough in their own homes, for their own families (or small businesses - one day!).You will be taught, demonstrated to, and then you get to have a go yourself; whilst learning my tips and tricks along the way. Plus we finish by sampling some delicious, fresh artisan sourdough with some favourite toppings!
You'll go home with all you need to make a good loaf of sourdough bread:
Knowledge
Student Guide, including recipes and troubleshoot guide
Bowl, liner, bowl scraper, and razor blade... all the tools you'd need to make sourdough
Dough made in class to bake the next day at home, to wow your family/friends
Confidence to continue the process yourself
Healthy, bubbling starter to continue your journey at home
Continued support and encouragement via our own private Facebook community group, for class attendees only
Note: You will have your dough to take home after the workshop that will need shaping around 6-8pm, so please ensure that will work for you that day.Also, please double check the date when booking. If for some reason you are unable to attend on the day, due to time constraints, I am unable to refund your ticket. I am happy for you to send someone in your place, though. In extenuating circumstances I may be able to hold your ticket for a future workshop; this is decided on a case-by-case basis.
*Payment to clear in bank account two days after ticket bought, after which time your ticket will be released on to someone else. Please pay immediately upon purchasing ticket.
**All workshop info you need to know ahead of time is in the confirmation email. Please ensure you read this down to the bottom.
***I recommend you already have a pair of digital scales, and a cast iron dutch oven at home to bake your loaf with as this makes the best, artisan sourdough, with great rise. Alternatively, a large, lidded casserole dish or enamel roasting pan work also. (The lids are a necessity).

"Sarah’s sour dough class is for real life! Achieve beautiful delicious bread easily in your own kitchen. Sarah teaches you how to fit sourdough into your busy life. She clearly and passionately gives the essential information, tips and tricks to make your own loaf. I loved getting to have a go at each step in the process. I love Sarah’s catchy philosophy “The goal is good bread, not perfect bread.” This took all the pressure off, for me, and I left the class empowered with all the knowledge and skills needed to give it a go at home. I appreciated the thoughtful and well prepared resources Sarah gave us. Thank you!" - Megan

"Sourdough with Sarah was a delight! From feeling overwhelmed and giving up on the idea of ever making my own loaf of sourdough, to dancing with joy as i watch a beautiful perfect sourdough loaf get nice and golden in the homewood stove!The information was packaged in bite sizes with hands on practice in between, where we all got to experience each step of the whole process, which was cleverly condensed and prepared so that despite normally having waits in between, we were able to see it all play out in a short few hours!The process was beautifully organised with attention to detail that had us fawning, came away feeling so blessed and privileged!Sarah's glowing personality made all the information feel easy and palatable, it was so fun!Such a lovely time, great environment to learn with ease." -Melody

description:
Lost Wax in Three - Friday Nights, Over three sessions, create stunning jewellery using the age-old lost wax technique. This is an excellent opportunity to treat yourself to some creative fun on a Friday night!

In weeks one and two, you will learn how to work with jeweller's hard wax to create beautiful textures in metal that can be used to create amazing rings or objects. Your waxes will be sent away for casting in the metal of your choice.

Then there will be a week's break before the third session, to allow time for everyone's waxes to be cast. The third session covers how to trim, clean and finish your casts, turning them into original jewellery ready to wear!

More advanced students can use these sessions to complete projects in a class setting.

Included in the class fee are all your different types of waxes, sandpaper and courier costs.

Not included are the casting and metal costs which vary from student to student depending on what sized ring they want to make and the type of metal you choose. Small castings can cost around $6.65 - $12 and might cost between $7 to $30 in metals depending on if you use red bronze, white bronze or silver.

description:
Amitayus Retreat - free event, Amitayus Retreat - free event
Long life, merit and wisdom.
In this special retreat we have the opportunity to increase our lifespan, merit and wisdom through the special meditation practice of Buddha Amitayus.

Our human life is our most precious possession because it gives us the opportunity to improve ourselves and attain the supreme happiness of enlightenment.
We therefore need a long and healthy life. To engage successfully in spiritual practice we also need wisdom, as well as the merit or good fortune that gives our mind the strength to support the growth of inner realisations. Amitayus is the Buddha of long life, merit and wisdom.

By engaging in Amitayus practice, we can develop these qualities and eventually attain the deathless state of enlightenment.
We can also use this practice to help remove obstacles endangering the lives of others, especially our close friends and family.

SESSION TIMES
10 – 11.15am
12 – 1.15pm
3 – 4.15pm

DETAILS
There is no cost and you can attend as many sessions as you like.
Coffee, tea and snacks are provided. Please bring your own vegetarian lunch if you are staying for the whole day.

If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
Explore how to appropriately structure and polish your CV and cover letter in order to achieve optimum results. This course prioritises you. Identify your key skills and weave these into your CV and cover letter to land you your next job interview.
 Whatever job you are going for, there are key characteristics that should not be missed in your CV and cover letter. The focus of this course is to provide you with the same lens as a recruiter. By learning how your job application is reviewed by a recruiter, you will gain skills to streamline your CV, and improve your cover letter writing. This will increase your chances of being a successful candidate and sets you up to land an interview for your ideal job.
 It doesn’t matter what previous experience you have - everyone has a range of skills that they can offer to their future employer. Fortunately, a strong and realistic CV and cover letter will showcase these skills and increase your chances for job success.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
- Recognise the features of an effective, well-crafted CV and cover letter and apply these to your own job applications;
- Apply different approaches, styles and techniques while writing your CV and cover letter depending on the context and job;
- Help others with their CV and cover letter writing (friends and whānau).
NOTE: This course is subsidised with funding from the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC) and you must provide photo ID when you attend your first session, if applicable.
 Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

description:
Jewellery-making in 8 weeks: Monday nights, Level 2+, This course is for level 2+ and advanced students; it is not open to beginners. This more advanced class is ideal for those who are already competent at soldering simple joins, annealing, and basic tool use on their own.

Students must have completed an 8-week block with ring-making and bezel settings or equivalent. If you have not done one of our 8-week beginner’s classes or are unsure of your level because you may have done a few different shorter classes, please contact us and we can advise you.

A more advanced set of students means that your tutor can support you and the group to take on more exciting and complex challenges. They will work with the group to find appropriate projects for you and for the class as a whole. Each 8-week block may focus on a particular skill topic, rather than doing loads of different topics with each student over the block. This means the students will benefit from a more in-depth learning experience, supported practice, and peer support from fellow students.

Level 2 students learn to solder more competently on their own using chain-making as a way to develop their fine soldering skills. They may create a simple stone setting or ring alongside this challenge project so they have something familiar to hone their skills on, as well as take on a new skill. Their silver project could be a pendant on a handmade chain, a charm bracelet, or a more complex bezel setting.

Level 3 and above students are encouraged to work on their design skills and try new and challenging techniques. They should prepare to have one or two simple projects they can work on self-directed in class—more chains or simple bezel settings are ideal to help them strengthen their soldering skills—alongside a challenge project that might be a shelf setting (an adaptation of a bezel setting that can take faceted stones), a tube setting, or a larger bezel setting for a box or brooch.

This class fee does NOT include materials for any student. All metals and stones, as well as quality findings and chains, are available for purchase each week in the studio. Students are encouraged to keep their silver scrap and find ways to use it thriftily in future projects!

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DfhRHnEAbak
Please note that there will be no class on 18th November, but we may run a practica
..............
This is stunning to watch and even more fun to dance! It is based on the original Lambada dance but adapted to more modern music with a slow-quick-quick beat. It can be danced to a wide variety of music including R&B, Rock and Pop.
In this block of 5 classes you will discover the fundamental techniques, movements and sequences of Brazilian Zouk to get you up and running on the dance floor.

Week 4: Body Roll, and Snake

They are designed so you can start this block as a complete newbie, or you can continue on as an existing student from the last block.
There are 4 blocks of classes (A, B, C, D) in the complete Fundamentals course. Once these are done, you can progress to the Improvers classes.

This class is ideal for creating sun-catchers or small decorative projects for beginners, or working on slightly more complex pieces for those with some experience.

Beginners will start by planning their design, then learning to cut the glass, and prepare it before foiling and soldering each piece, creating a simple elegant clear glass starter project.

description:
Rewa Rewa Station Craft Day, Our on-farm Craft Open Days are aimed primarily at knitters, spinners, crocheters and other fibre crafters.

Nestled in the small rural community of Tinui is Rewa Rewa Station. Operating as a hill country Sheep & Beef breeding farm with a strong focus on NZ Wool & Fibre. Romney, Corriedale cross Romney, Polwarth & Gotland sheep provide the fleeces to create fibre products for crafters, which are sold via the Rewa Rewa Station website, and at Fibre Markets. Our Studio Shop is also open during Craft Days or by appointment outside of these days.

Our Craft Days allow you to work on your own project, seek advice, learn a new fibre skill or maybe just spend time with fellow fibre crafters while potentially ticking off a project or two in the process.

Please bring your knitting, crocheting, spinning, hand sewing, or any project you are working on and enjoy a drive in the country!

Coffee, tea and snacks provided, but bring your lunch and stay awhile.

Everyone is welcome, the more the merrier!

Note: Craft Days are primarily held on the 3rd Saturday of every mont,h but occasionally we do have to juggle the dates.

description:
Genealogy - Basics of Ancestry.com DNA Tests, Have you done a DNA test with Ancestry.com? Don't know what to do next? This course will cover the basics of what your DNA results mean. We'll look at the tools available on Ancestry.com and other DNA websites to help you use DNA for your family's history.

Got your test results back and still wondering what a centiMorgan or a shared match is? Find out what the size of your match means and the tools available to help you work out possible family relationships.

We'll start by looking at Clustering and how the Grouping tool on Ancestry.com can help with this. We'll also look at the ThruLines and Common Ancestors tools to see how they can provide suggestions for knocking down brick walls in your research.

Also covered during this course are other websites you can upload your results to, how to manage other family member's DNA test results and other tools available when you get a bit more advanced - and just in case you need help in the future, we'll look at some of the places you could get this from.

Please note: This course will only cover DNA tests taken through Ancestry.com

Bring a USB stick and your Ancestry.com login details.

By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
- Understand the information provided by Ancestry.com about your DNA matches
- Use the Grouping, ThruLines and Common Ancestors tools on Ancestry.com
- Use the Shared CentiMorgan tool on DNAPainter.com
- Understand where to find other tools
- Understand the options for using other DNA websites
- Understand where to get further help

Prerequisites
For those who have already done a DNA test through Ancestry.com
You will need good computing and internet skills.

Note: Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

description:
Mana Up with Pio Terei in Wellington, Actor, singer, comedian, and storyteller Pio Terei joins The Village NZ for a transformative community kōrero: Mana Up - a deep dive into the real-life challenges that shape us and the real tools that can help us rise.
With his trademark warmth, humour, and humility, Pio guides us through the kaupapa that impact so many of our whānau:
Trauma
Relationships
Whānau dynamics
Grief
Anger
Alcohol & addiction
This is not a lecture. This is lived experience, shared with honesty and heart. Pio has a unique ability to speak with people, not at them. Creating a space where laughter and vulnerability sit side by side. His kōrero is grounded in te ao Māori, backed by simple, evidence-based tools, and designed for communities who are often the hardest to reach.
You’ll hear about concepts like Red Brain vs Green Brain, how trauma shapes behaviour, how to pause before reacting, and how to reconnect with the things that give us strength. These are tools people remember, take away, and use the very next day.
Why This Matters
Mana Up has been described as “an aha moment” for many who attend. One organiser reflected:
“An amazing workshop that was real, relevant, and so appropriate. Realising the whakapapa of trauma and being able to recognise behaviours as the result of such trauma really was an aha moment for a good percentage of the room.”
Another shared:
“The room spoke at length after you left, wanting more and asking for strategies to work on the trauma rather than just the behaviour. The feedback was exceptional -probably the best in my three years on the Executive Team.”
And perhaps the most powerful reflection of all:
“To have this delivered with such passion… I feel like there is hope for our ākonga, whānau, hapū, and iwi.”
These talks have sparked healing, shifted perspectives, and helped whānau feel seen, valued, and hopeful again. They are for everyone - parents, educators, community workers, youth, those impacted by trauma, those seeking reconnection, and anyone wanting to understand themselves and others better.

description:
Wholesome Introduction to Foraging + Healthy potluck Wellington, Wholesome Introduction to Foraging + Healthy potluck Wellington
Foraging and Growing Workshops coming to Wellington all the way from Chch. No need for prior knowledge we will learn everything from scratch.
I'm hitchhiking all the way from Chch to Whangarei and back to Chch with a purpose to pass on some knowledge while visiting my dear friends.
Workshop description:

During this workshop we will learn to substitute our salads, teas, seeds for the rest of our lives, save a lot of money while contributing to nature thriving again, with incredible insights all through life while reconnecting with nature everytime a bit more. A contribution to mental and physical wellbeing while having an excuse to go outside, treasure hunting with your family, neighbours, friends or solo💚
No prior knowledge is necessary. Just your enthusiasm to learn, ready ears and ready to try new things and sense of humour😊
We will have a very necessary 30-40min introduction talk with some unique understandings of what plants are, how we can help them and how they can help us. All with good scientific attitude. We will also learn some tools/attitudes to foraging and then walk around and learn about 10-12 plants that can be found nearly in every backyard during this workshop, which out of my experience have proven to be most practical and easiest to remember.
3 hours is a must to do all the above.
_______
Come with your family,friends, neighbours and furry companions, please share this event.
_________
I encourage everyone to walk, cycle or bus to the event as part of the experience and help not to impact the environment. I will have some special gifts for the ones who do 🌻💚

Please allow enough time to immerse into the workshop with no rush"

description:
Wairarapa Early Childhood Education Emergency Planning Workshop, Would you and your staff be confident in an emergency? Do you have a plan, and more importantly have you practiced it recently?

We are holding a free workshop for Early Childhood Education Centres in Carterton to come together to discuss your emergency plans.

This workshop is an opportunity for ECEs to share challenges and solutions and get advice from our Emergency Management Advisors.

It aims to help ECEs plan for significant events such as earthquake, tsunami, armed intruders and to share ideas and network around emergency planning.

This is an opportunity for Centre Managers, Directors, Health & Safety Representatives or Leads to:

- Share solutions, practical advice and learnings from recent events.
- Discuss your current plans with emergency management professionals.
- Learn more about our local hazards and important planning information including lessons learnt from the Christchurch Earthquake, Christchurch Mosque Attack and Cyclone Gabrielle.

Hope to see you there as we build safer and more resilient ECEs and communities together.

Places are limited to a maximum of 15 people attending each session. This workshop is endorsed by the Ministry of Education.

For more information, please email melanie.arcus@wremo.nz

We aim to make our community events inclusive to all people. If you are attending the event and have specific accessibility requirements, you can send us an email at melanie.arcus@wremo.nz with the event name and we will get in touch to see how we can meet your needs as best we can.

Step into the flavours of Vietnamese Lunar New Year with a special hands-on workshop that brings tradition, community, and storytelling together.Join us as part of the Lunar New Year Festival 2026 for a delicious cultural experience!� What We’ll Make Together:� Bánh Tét / Bánh Chưng – iconic sticky rice cakes wrapped in banana leaves, symbolising family reunion and prosperity� Dưa Món (Pickled Vegetables) – crunchy, colourful pickles that balance the richness of New Year dishes� Thịt Kho (Caramelized Braised Pork) – tender pork simmered in coconut water, a dish that brings comfort, warmth and the taste of TếtThis workshop is designed for all ages — beginners, families, food lovers, and anyone curious about Vietnamese culture. Learn the meaning behind each dish and how they come together on every Vietnamese New Year table.� Why Join?• Experience authentic Vietnamese Tết traditions• Learn recipes passed down through generations• Connect with community through shared stories and food• Take home what you cook• Celebrate culture in a warm, hands-on environmentSponsored & partnered with Pomare Taita Community Trust, E Tū Whānau & Hutt City Council.

Wednesday 24 September 2025, 10am – 2pm
Discovery Garden, Wellington Botanic Garden
Free
These September school holidays, welcome the season of new life and bright colours at our family-friendly Pavilion Open Day. Tamariki can explore the garden’s spring magic through self-guided, hands-on activities designed to spark curiosity and connection.
Activities include:
• Nature’s Colours Art Table – Create your own “spring colour” bookmark using petals, leaves, and other natural treasures.
• Spring Scavenger Hunt – Follow clues to spot signs of the season: sweet-scented flowers, fresh buds, busy birds and more.
• Spring Reading Nook – Relax on the grass mat with tree-stump cushions and enjoy a basket of spring-themed books.
• Bird’s Nest Discovery Table – Get up close to real nests (ethically sourced), soft bird puppets, and natural playdough to shape “eggs” and match them to their native bird owners.
Perfect for children aged 4–12 and their whānau. Dress for a day outdoors and celebrate te kōanga—the vibrant season of growth!
We’ll see you soon in the Discovery Garden, Wellington Botanic Garden!

description:

Purple Poppy Day honours the animals who served alongside people in times of war, companions whose loyalty and courage are woven into New Zealand’s military history.

At this special ceremony, hear stories remembering courageous service animals and meet Paradox the horse and Kingi the dog, who will join the event to represent the heroes in these stories. Guests are invited to lay a purple poppy at the Man and the Donkey Memorial or chalk paint a purple poppy and a message of acknowledgement into the park.

Come along to a summer's evening at Pukeahu to acknowledge our service animals.

description:
Wairarapa Walking Festival 2025: StoryWalk®, Turn reading into an adventure with your tamariki at this special StoryWalk® in Carrington Park, Carterton. A StoryWalk® transforms a children’s book into an outdoor journey—pages are displayed on stands along the path, and children can walk, run, hop, skip, jump, or be wheeled to each one as you read together. The StoryWalk® Wairarapa project was instigated by and is managed by Wairarapa Library Service.

Guiding this experience is local Speech Language Therapist Polly Newton, who will share simple, practical tips to help strengthen children’s speech, language, and literacy skills through the way you read with them.

The StoryWalk® is located on a flat, sealed path around the park, making it ideal for prams, wheelchairs, and scooters. Meet by the playground and enjoy a relaxed, family-friendly activity designed for children and their caregivers.

This event is part of the Wairarapa Walking Festival and is proudly supported by REAP Wairarapa, Chatterbox Wairarapa, and Speech Teacher. The StoryWalk® Project was created by Anne Ferguson in collaboration with the Kellogg-Hubbard Library, Montpelier, Vermont, and is a registered service mark owned by the Kellogg-Hubbard Library.

Bookings are now open at our Google Form (Link in comments) – we need bookings and payment by 30 November.

The main course will be largely catered, but we have volunteers making ćevapi, octopus salad and punjene paprike/stuffed peppers and also invite members to bring a dessert plate to share, whether it’s from Croatia, NZ or elsewhere.

We are planning to cater for different dietary requirements, so please put down any needs on the booking form.

Timing
11am - Croatian Mass
Fr Bozjak is coming down from Auckland to give a Mass in Croatian at St Anne’s Church (22 Emmett St, Newtown). He will join us for lunch afterwards.

12pm - Club doors open
The club doors open at 12pm at 32 Hania St, Mt Victoria. Father Christmas - Djed Božićnjak 🧑‍🎄visits before lunch is served (parents, don’t forget to bring a named and wrapped gift for your child to pop under the tree for them).

About 12.30pm Lunch starts
A buffet main course will be served. Dessert will be served after the main and members are welcome to stay and enjoy the day.

Payment:
The Executive keeps the costs as low as possible to make sure we get a good cross-section of members, including young families.

- $30 per person for Financial and Life Members of the Club

description:
Porirua Montessori Primary + Intermediate School Open Day. Kids and Children Porirua., Porirua Montessori Primary + Intermediate School Open Day. Kids and Children Porirua.
Come and visit us at our open day Saturday Jan 24th. We are a small school with a true family and community ethos. We believe that children deserve an individual education that prepares them for life. We value life skills, emotional intelligence and academic skills.
====

At Porirua Montessori Primary School we believe in a different kind of learning – one where every child is truly seen, valued and empowered.
👣 Instead of embedding children into age-only class groups, we use mixed‐age classrooms so younger tamariki learn from older peers and older children grow in leadership and empathy.


🎯 We focus on independence and choice. Our students plan parts of their day, select work that meets their interests and pace, and deepen concentration in long, uninterrupted work sessions.


🌍 Our curriculum is grounded in “Education for Life” – we integrate academic learning with care for each child, community, culture and the environment. Respect, responsibility and compassion are woven through everything.


📚 We go beyond the expected. With small class sizes and a learning environment designed especially for students aged 5-13, we tailor programmes to each child’s strengths and needs rather than expecting every child to move at the same pace.


If you’re looking for something beyond the usual primary school experience – a place where your child can grow not just academically but as a confident, capable, connected human – get in touch with us today to learn more about 2026

RSVP -
enrolments. https://poriruamontessori.squarespace.com/

description:
Commemorating Purple Poppy Day at Pukeahu, On Wednesday 24 February, tamariki and whānau are invited to come together for a Purple Poppy Day commemoration at Pukeahu.
Purple Poppy Day honours the animals who served alongside people in times of war, companions whose loyalty and courage are woven into New Zealand’s military history.
At this special ceremony, hear stories remembering courageous service animals and meet Paradox the horse and Kingi the dog, who will join the event to represent the heroes in these stories. Guests are invited to lay a purple poppy at the Man and the Donkey Memorial or chalk paint a purple poppy and message of acknowledgement into the park.
Come along to a summers evening at Pukeahu to acknowledge our service animals.
Wednesday 24 February
5pm – 6pm
Pukeahu National War Memorial Park

description:
Awa After Dark: Spotlighting in Central Park, Join experts from Mountain's to Sea Wellington's Wai Connection team, and the Te Papa Natural History team to explore your local park by night, at this free City Nature Challenge spotlighting event at Central Park, Brooklyn.
With your gumboots and a torch, head off on an evening hīkoi in your local park meet glowworm and ‘spotlight’ and observe freshwater species in their natural habitat. You'll also learn about the health of our waterways, freshwater conservation efforts in your neighbourhood, and some of the ways that you could become a kaitiaki of your local awa.
Awa After Dark events take place in the early evening, they’re perfect for the whole whānau.
This event is free, but bookings are essential. These events are very popular, please let us know if you have booked and can no longer make it so we can reallocate your spot.
Outdoor event: This event is outdoors, please dress appropriately (gumboots and warm clothing recommended). If the weather is really bad, we'll let you know we’re cancelling via the email you've provided us.
What is spotlighting? Ever played spotlight with a fish? Heaps of our native freshwater critters are nocturnal. Scientists often visit freshwater areas at nighttime with a torch, it’s the best time to see exactly who’s living in our awa!
What is Wai Connection? Wai Connection is a national programme delivered by Mountains to Sea Wellington aimed at connecting local communities to their waterways to improve them. We support communities and mana whenua with the knowledge, tools and expertise to monitor and care for their local freshwater places.
label: Conservation & Environment

description:
Climate Action Week Marlborough 2026, Climate Action Week 2026 - Agenda
Dates: February 2026 - 23rd - 27th February
Theme: Leading by Doing: Local Businesses, Local Solutions, Collective Action
Each day features:
Morning: Local Business Story Sessions (Curated stories for our business community to get inspired with raw, honest and authentic stories)
Midday: Action Design Workshops (Crafting Plans & Solutions)
Afternoon: Immersion Experiences and Hands-On Field Visits (Applied Learning & Practical Support)
Day 1 - Water, Risk and Resilience 
Morning: Marlborough businesses facing water risks (horticulture, viticulture, manufacturing, nature, tourism) share adaptation stories: irrigation shifts, stormwater projects, water reuse innovations.
Midday: Business-specific water risks and trials of resilience-building solutions.
Afternoon: Immersion experiences to diverse water initiatives across the region
Day 2 - Circular economy & waste
Morning: Circular leaders (including wine, food and beverages, building, construction, fisheries, nature) show how they have reduced waste, repurposed byproducts, or created new revenue streams from "waste."
Midday: Waste Challenge Mapping: Identify business-specific waste streams + select 1 trial action for next 6 months.
Afternoon: Immersion experiences to waste initiatives and innovative approaches across the region
Day 3 - Nature and Biodiversity 
Morning: Farmers, viticulturists & landscapers share regenerative planting, biodiversity corridors, and ecosystem services initiatives.
Midday: Biodiversity on Your Site Toolkit Workshop — Co-design biodiversity plans for business properties (guided by local ecologists & farming leaders)
Afternoon: Immersion experiences to nature, land and biodiversity initiatives across the region.
Day 4 - Energy & Carbon
Morning: Businesses share their real decarbonisation journeys: EV transitions, renewable installations, process efficiencies — including the unexpected savings, costs and impacts.
Midday: Carbon Reduction Sprints — Design quick-win carbon actions + long-term projects with support from energy consultants and local peers.
Afternoon: Site visits to Carbon and Energy initiatives across the region
Day 5 - Taking the courage to act
Morning: Businesses share how they initiated climate actions, including failures, lessons, and unexpected wins.
Midday: Climate Action Map Workshop — Business leaders build their tailored action roadmaps (guided by peers + facilitators).
Afternoon: Immersion to unique businesses focussed on climate resilience
Immersion Experience Hosts Confirmed
Yealands Estate Winery
Carbonscape
Wineworks
Climate Action Marlborough has had the support of Yealands Estate Wines from its inception and we are super grateful to have their support for Climate Action Week Marlborough 2026. They are committed to influencing the Marlborough business community, driving collaboration that impacts climate resilience, education, health and wellbeing of a thriving region and growing our regions expertise.

description:
Dragonflies are Amazing and Important - Why? Forest & Bird Talk, Ruary Mackenzie Dodds and his wife Kari de Koenigswarter are rated as Dragonfly Ambassadors by the British Dragonfly Society. Although they have recently been involved in the setting up of a Dragonfly Hotspot at Exbury Gardens in Hampshire, England, their work currently centres on New Zealand where awareness of the significance of dragonflies is possibly behind that of the U.K, which has a growing understanding that dragonflies are a key indicator of the health of waterspaces. In this talk, Ruary takes his listeners back to basics on the amazing life-cycle of these insects. He outlines typical preferred dragonfly habitats, and touches on identification of New Zealand species. In addition to Ruary’s talk, Kari’s larva demonstration table shows actual examples of the larval stages on NZ species.

In their New Zealand work, Ruary and Kari are carrying out survey work on behalf of the Greater Wellington Regional Council, and are preparing a dragonfly education pack for schools. They have also carried out five summer seasons of night-filming an endemic dragonfly species – the Bush Giant – that lives in a burrow! A Radio New Zealand Our Changing World programme has been devoted to their work. (https://www.rnz.co.nz/news/ourchangingworld/546925/dragonflies-masters-of-flight).

Ruary and Kari have been working with dragonflies since 1985. They opened Europe’s first Dragonfly Sanctuary in 1991, and founded the National Dragonfly Biomuseum in Northamptonshire in 1995. They and their team of volunteers continued work with the National Trust at Wicken Fen in Cambridgeshire, and were instrumental in the setting up of the Dragonfly Centre there in 2009. Their adventures were published in Ruary’s memoir, The Dragonfly Diaries (Saraband, 2014). Kari and Ruary have also published a how-to book, Dragonfly-Friendly Gardening (Saraband, 2024). Ruary is a Fellow of the Linnean Society. He has given talks in eight countries. He has appeared on both television and radio, and was awarded BBC Springwatch’s ‘Dragonfly Geek’ award. He has been featured in BBC Wildlife Magazine, The Scottish Field, and in Wildlife Trust and National Trust publications. Kari specializes in the larval stages of odonate life, while – even after over forty years – Ruary’s heart lifts at the sight of a dragonfly in flight, and he loves sharing this enthusiasm with his listeners!

Join artist Katy Cottrell as she chats with academic researcher Matteo Collina and director of the Victoria University Coastal Ecology Lab – Alice Rogers. description:
WECG ANNUAL GENERAL MEETING 2025, ANNUAL GENERAL MEETING
Tena Tatou Katoa, Greetings Everyone
A WARM INVITATION TO ALL TO ATTEND THE 2025 AGM OF THE WAIKANAE ESTUARY CARE GROUP INC
Venue: The Otaihanga Boating Club, Makora Rd,
Otaihanga
Date & time: Wednesday 3rd September at 7pm
Speaker Mike Joy - see Bio below

Mike is a Senior Research Fellow and Lecturer in Environmental Science at the School of Geography, Environment and Earth Sciences, Mike’s research includes ecological modelling, freshwater bioassessment, environmental policy, biophysical limits to growth and the role of energy in society. He is publicly outspoken about the decline in freshwater quality and ecosystems, especially the impact of nutrient pollution from intensive dairying on New Zealand's "100% Pure", clean, green image.
Mike is an outspoken advocate for environmental protection in New Zealand and for this has received several awards including an Ecology in Action award from the NZ Ecological Society, the Charles Fleming Award for environmental achievement in 2013 and in 2023 the Callaghan Medal for science communication from the Royal Society of New Zealand. In 2015 he was awarded the Morgan Foundation inaugural River Voice Award and in 2017 the inaugural NZ Universities Critic and Conscience award. His book 'The Fight for Freshwater' is a memoir that provides a rare first hand look at those whose voices question the health and future of our waterways.
The Meeting will conclude with supper and korero
