description:
Wairarapa Walking Festival 2025: Holdsworth Restoration Trust Walk, Led by volunteers from the Holdsworth Restoration Trust this walk highlights community-led restoration in Tararua Forest Park. Walk leaders will guide participants along trap lines and share their knowledge of the pest control project — including how often traps are checked, which pests are targeted, and how bird and animal populations are monitored. Keep your eyes and ears open for native birds such as tomtits, early evening ruru, grey warblers, North Island riflemen, and the ever-present pīwakawaka, tūī, and kererū.

Volunteers will also share their knowledge of native flora, including towering rimu, northern rātā, and miro, which rise above a canopy of kāmahi and beech. The forest is one of the finest examples of mixed podocarp–broadleaf forest in the Tararua Ranges and supports a wide range of indigenous biodiversity. Walkers will also learn how to identify native fungi, understand five-minute bird count monitoring, and discover how to get involved in pest trapping.

Walk format:
This slow-paced walk is suitable for most fitness levels and ages 5 +. The path is mainly on a flat track, however the ground can be uneven with tree roots - not suitable wheelchairs. Note, there is no cellphone reception on this walk. No dogs allowed.

Depart the carpark at 6pm, cross the bridge and along the track, breaking into 3 groups at the Gentle Annie junction. The 3 groups all take a different route along a trap line track, meeting at Donnelly Flat for a hot chocolate and snack before continuing the walk around the loop track and back to the carpark at 8pm time.

Image credit: Jet Productions
label: Conservation & Environment

description:
Community Agency - a conversation with Markus Luczak-Roesch and Gerhard Reese, Two scholars share their perspectives on Community Agency and discuss the links between the Global environmental and global mental health crises.
  Gerhard Reese
Gerhard Reese works as Associate Professor for Climate Change at Te Herenga Waka - Victoria University Wellington in Aotearoa / New Zealand. Before, he was Professor and head of the Environmental Psychology Research Unit at the University Kaiserslautern-Landau in Germany since 2016. He got his PhD in Psychology from Friedrich Schiller University Jena, Germany, in February 2010. His research focuses on individual, social and systemic catalysts and barriers of pro-environmental behaviour and the nature-health nexus. He attempts and sometimes succeeds to spend more time outdoors than indoors and he still is a serious competitor in Super Mario Kart.
Markus Luczak-Roesch
Markus Luczak-Roesch is Professor of Informatics and the inaugural Chair in Complexity Science at Victoria University of Wellington’s School of Information Management. He is also one of two Co-Directors of Te Pūnaha Matatini, Aotearoa New  Zealand’s Centre of Research Excellence in Complex Systems. He founded and leads the Complexity & Connection Science Lab, an interdisciplinary group developing theories and computational tools to understand how complex systems unfold over time. His research investigates how structure and meaning emerge in systems - from language and brain activity to online communities and the environment - using models that reveal meaningful coincidences and unexpected system-level behaviors.

description:
Dark Sky Trek Wainuiomata, Dark Sky Trek Wainuiomata
We’ve teamed up with the Wellington Astronomical Society to bring you this exclusive FREE behind-the-scenes event.

Walk deep into untouched native forest, through some of the oldest trees in the Wellington region as darkness falls. You may even hear the calls of kiwi and rūrū (morepork). Explore an area not normally open to the public, including a glowworm-filled tunnel that links to the Orongorongo Valley.

The Wellington Astronomical Society will share insights on Dark Sky awareness and preservation and provide a telescope for viewing the Moon and the Southern Cross. Talk to Remutaka Conservation Trust volunteers about their Kiwi release programme and nest monitoring efforts.

Wainuiomata Regional Park is working towards dark sky reserve accreditation, one of only three in the Southern Hemisphere. Light pollution from the city washes out most stars, but the park offers one of the best stargazing experiences in the region.

What to bring:
• Torches/headlamps (red lenses preferred)
• Sturdy waterproof footwear
• Warm and wet weather clothing

Note: This event includes a steep hill walk in the dark. A moderate level of fitness is required, and it is not suitable for children under 15.

This event is strictly limited to 30 people. Registrations for this event will be on a first in first served basis and opens at 10am on Tuesday 14 July via this link:
label: Conservation & Environment

description:
Botanical Insights talk - Electrical secrets of plants and fungi, This one-hour talk will reveal how plants’ electrical signalling system enables them to sense and respond rapidly, within seconds, to their environment in order to coordinate growth, defence, photosynthesis and whole of-plant acclimatisation. It will outline the practical application of research findings. The talk will also examine electrical signalling in fungi and the actions of their mycelial networks in a range of biological and environmental settings. It will touch on the emergent field of fungal computers and probable applications

Located in the Treehouse Seminar Room, Wellington Botanic Garden ki Paekākā.
Free of charge. You’re welcome to bring along your own ‘brown bag’ lunch to enjoy during the talk.

The speaker is Reg Harris, a volunteer guide [botany] at Wellington Botanic Garden.
Reg delivers botany lectures for diverse audiences, delving into plant interactions, adaptive radiation, tropisms, and more. As a dedicated volunteer guide at Wellington Botanic Garden (WBG), he pioneered collaborations between WBG and Victoria University, organising annual field labs for biology students.

We will meet outside Todd Energy, one of the oil and gas companies who have had outrageous influence in shaping this government's repeal of the oil and gas exploration ban AND helped weaken decommisoning rules around oil and gas installations. description:
Manawa Karioi Working Bees, Our track maintenance and planting working bees are focussing on plant species diversity rather than large planting quantities. We have ordered 500 special trees from the Berhampore Nursery. We are in this for the long haul and a forest that contains a wide range of plant species will, in turn, attract a wider range of birds, lizards, and invertebrates. A wider range of plant species also allows for the forest to naturally regenerate and helps prevent weed species from establishing.
Tools are provided, bring sturdy shoes and a water bottle, Working bees are held every Sunday in June (except June 1st - Kings Birthday Weekend) and August 2025.
Meeting at the Information Shelter in the Tapu te Ranga Marae car park at the end of Danube Street in Island Bay. Children are welcome with parents or guardians.
label: Conservation & Environment

description:
Emma La Rocca - Zero waste swimwear, Emma La Rocca is the founder of Emroce swimwear, the world’s first zero-waste swimwear brand.
Her cutting patterns are designed to fit together perfectly- like a puzzle. This innovative technique fuels creativity, reduces costs, and saves up to a quarter of fabric from ending up in landfill.
Now, Emma is on a mission to help other fashion brands embrace Zero Waste Fashion Design (ZWFD). Through fully funded consulting, she’s breaking down misconceptions and showing how sustainability and style can go hand in hand.
Join Emma as she introduces the world of ZWFD, shares her results, and invites you to try out some simple techniques yourself.
Register for this free event, enjoy a complimentary breakfast from our sponsors, and take part in this inspiring conversation about sustainable fashion.
label: Conservation & Environment

description:
The Great Southern BioBlitz 2025, A bioblitz is all about searching for and recording all the species you can over a set time period.
Zealandia is one of the most biodiversity rich places in the motu(show English translation) and the perfect place to come searching for species over the long weekend. You may well find species that we have seldom seen, so this is fun for you and potentially invaluable information for our conservation team. It’s a great reason to go exploring some of the tracks you might not know well, or at all!
All you have to do is upload your observations of species to iNaturalist. It doesn’t have to be done during those four days. In fact, you’ll have a further 14 days to upload and identify all your observations made during the official period.
The Great Southern BioBlitz 2025 is run by a network of keen citizen scientists from across the globe.
label: Conservation & Environment

description:
Climate Fresk Workshop in Wellington, Do you want to be part of the solution to tackle climate change but don’t have the time to become a climate scientist? Join us for a 3-hour interactive workshop to explore the causes and consequences of climate disturbances.
Based on the IPCC report and played by over 2 million people worldwide, this collaborative workshop allows you to understand the key issues of climate change to empower you to take action.
How it works:
The workshop is based on a 42-card game. Each card represents an element, a cause or a consequence of climate change.
In teams, guided by your facilitator, you are encouraged to find the cause-effect relationship between the different components of climate change. Collective intelligence will get you from one deck of card to the next!
This step-by-step reconstruction provides keys to understand the complexity and develop an overview of climate change.
Agenda:
Introduction, 15 mins
Fresk Card Game, 1 1/2 hours
Debrief and Actions, 1 hour 15 mins
Climate Fresk is an excellent opportunity to understand the key elements of climate change and what you can do to be part of the solution. No previous climate knowledge is needed.

Tea, coffee and snacks will be served during the short break. Workshop run by Climate Training Co. Ltd. Donations welcome!
label: Conservation & Environment

description:
Wainuiomata Old Forest Walk, Explore ancient forests on a guided walk in Wainuiomata Regional Park
Step into a world few get to see. This exclusive guided walk takes you deep into a 1,000-year-old forest in Wainuiomata Regional Park—an area normally closed to the public. Wander beneath towering native rātā and rimu trees and discover a sanctuary for North Island brown kiwi and other native birds.
Led by one of our experienced park rangers, you’ll hear stories about the park’s natural heritage, conservation efforts, and the history that makes this place so special.
Registrations are extremely limited. Each walk is capped at just 20 participants, with a maximum of two registrations per person. If you’ve joined this event before, please consider letting someone else experience this unique opportunity.
Event Details:
Distance: Approx. 10 km, including river crossings
Fitness level: Moderate
Age: Not suitable for children under 14
What to bring: Lunch, snacks, water bottle, and appropriate clothing
To book: Due to the overwhelming popularity of these limited capacity events, we have now decided that the fairest way to allocate bookings is via a ballot system. A ballot will open for registrations via the link below at 10am on Tuesday 24 February and close at 10am on Thursday 26 February. Names will then be drawn at random all applicants notified of the outcome by 4pm on Friday 27 February.

Enter the ballot here: https://forms.office.com/r/8JwALbS0Pb

Your adventure starts in regional parks.
Greater Wellington has been hosting recreational events like this for 25 years, helping people connect with nature, get active outdoors, and enjoy peaceful escapes close to home.
Wainuiomata Regional Park is not only a place of beauty—it’s a hub for conservation. Greater Wellington works here to protect freshwater, restore native habitats, preserve historic sites, and build resilience to climate change. Our partners and volunteers play a vital role in making this park a special place for everyone.
label: Conservation & Environment

SEED AND SEEDLING SWAP: Join us for the launch of Seeds to Feeds Island Bay!, We're starting a new group dedicated to building a kai secure Island Bay, and we'd love for you to be a part of it!

Where: Seed to Sea, 38 The Parade, Island Bay
What to expect:
Seed and Seedling Share: Bring your extra seeds and seedlings to swap, or just come and pick up some new ones - we have plenty!
Learn & Create: Discover how to create new plants from your seeds and make your own seed packets.
Delicious Treats: Enjoy coffee, fresh herbal tea, and tasty treats.
Garden Open Day: Explore the beautiful garden at Seed to Sea and meet the chooks!
Share Your Ideas: We want to hear your thoughts on how we can create a kai secure Island Bay.
Seed and seedling swaps are an awesome opportunity for gardeners of all skill levels to collaborate and share what they might have an abundance of.
It's a great way to save money on buying new seedlings, an opportunity to learn from others, and can lead to new varietals in your māra.
Bring something to share if you can, but if not, come as you are – we'll have plenty for everyone!

description:
Wairarapa Walking Festival 2025: Millennium Native Forest Reserve, Led by volunteers from the Friends of Millennium Native Forest Reserve, this walk offers a fascinating insight into how a once-degraded farmland has been transformed into a thriving native bush and wetland. The Millennium Native Forest Reserve is a community asset established by Masterton South Rotary on land leased from Masterton Trust Lands Trust. It is maintained by a committee called Friends of Millennium Native Forest Reserve. Walk leaders will share the Reserve’s inspiring history — from its beginnings in 1993 as a community-led project to restore local biodiversity, to its official opening in 2000 as part of a nationwide millennium celebration. QR codes placed at key spots offer short films presented by local college students, bringing the Reserve’s stories, conservation work, and unique ecology to life. Along the way, participants will hear how volunteers have reintroduced native plants and nurtured a habitat for native birds, insects, and aquatic life.

As you explore the urban Reserve’s meandering tracks, keep an eye out for tūī and kererū - with a bit of luck, you might also spot a gecko basking in the sun, a darting skink, long and short finned eels in the waterways, koura beneath the shallows, dragonflies, and even wētā tucked away in the undergrowth. You’ll gain an appreciation for the dedication it takes to maintain the Reserve and discover ways to get involved in its ongoing care.

Walk format:
This easy-paced walk is suitable for all fitness levels and ages. The tracks are 2.6km of largely limed winding tracks, with sections of boardwalks. Note: there is no toilet available at this reserve.

We’ll depart from the Hillcrest St Reserve entrance at 2pm. The group will pause at key points to hear stories, scan QR codes, and observe wildlife. Expect 1 - 1.5hr walk.

Photo credits: Friends of Millennium Native Forest Reserve.
This walk is part of the Wairarapa Walking Festival 2025.

description:
Climate Kōrero Pōneke - Storytellers Night # 4, Join us for Climate Kōrero #4!

After three sold-out events, we are thrilled to invite you to the fourth installment of Climate Kōrero - an evening dedicated to celebrating the inspiring mahi of individuals and organisations driving climate action in our communities.

We'll hear from four passionate speakers who will share their journeys, insights, and practical ways you can get involved. The event follows the Pecha Kucha format - each speaker presents 20 slides, with each slide shown for 20 seconds. This fast-paced visual format is a great way to share stories and keeps everyone on their toes! After each presentation, you’ll have the chance to kōrero directly with the speakers, so bring your pātai and curiosity.

Why should you come along?
* Get inspired by changemakers in the climate action space
* Free kai to enjoy while you listen and connect
* Meet like-minded people and grow your climate action network
* Discover opportunities to get involved, including funding through the Climate and Sustainability Fund

Our speakers:
* Ben Gleisner- Cogo
* Ash Holwell - Pōneke Climate Hub at two/fiftyseven
* Mary Moeono-Kolio - Pacific Climate Warriors
* Mike Anthony - Race to 2030 and GoZero
Our MC for the evening will be Lucy Shand

Event details:
* 5:00–5:30pm – Arrive, grab some kai, and explore info on climate action in Pōneke
* 5:30pm – Welcome and first two speakers
* Intermission – More kai and kōrero
* Final two speakers – Then wrap-up and mingling

Whether you're new to climate action or already involved, this is a great chance to connect, learn, and be inspired.

Come and kōrero with us!

description:
Wainuiomata Old Forest Walk - January, Wainuiomata Old Forest Walk - January
Explore ancient forests on a guided walk in Wainuiomata Regional Park

Step into a world few get to see. This exclusive guided walk takes you deep into a 1,000-year-old forest in Wainuiomata Regional Park—an area normally closed to the public. Wander beneath towering native rātā and rimu trees and discover a sanctuary for North Island brown kiwi and other native birds.

Led by one of our experienced park rangers, you’ll hear stories about the park’s natural heritage, conservation efforts, and the history that makes this place so special.

Registrations are extremely limited. Each walk is capped at just 20 participants, with a maximum of two registrations per person. If you’ve joined this event before, please consider letting someone else experience this unique opportunity.

Event Details:

Distance: Approx. 10 km, including river crossings

Fitness level: Moderate

Age: Not suitable for children under 14

What to bring: Lunch, snacks, water bottle, and appropriate clothing

To book: Due to the overwhelming popularity of these limited capacity events, we have now decided that the fairest way to allocate bookings is via a ballot system. A ballot will open for registrations via the link below at 10am on Monday 15 December and close at 10am on Wednesday 17 December. Names will then be drawn at random all applicants notified of the outcome by 4pm on Thursday 18 December.

Ballot opens 10am on 15 December:


Your adventure starts in regional parks.

Greater Wellington has been hosting recreational events like this for 25 years, helping people connect with nature, get active outdoors, and enjoy peaceful escapes close to home.

Wainuiomata Regional Park is not only a place of beauty—it’s a hub for conservation. Greater Wellington works here to protect freshwater, restore native habitats, preserve historic sites, and build resilience to climate change. Our partners and volunteers play a vital role in making this park a special place for everyone.

Species radiation in the Southern Hemisphere with New Zealand examples - Kōwhai, Hebe (Veronica) and Kauri.

Examples of rare and endangered species owing their situations directly to human activities. These plants include:

- Shrubby toroaro/mingimingi (Muehlenbeckia astonii),
- Three Kings kaikomako (Pennantia baylisiana),
- Bartlett's rātā (Metrosideros bartlettii)
- Three Kings vine/akapukaea (Tecomanthe speciosa).

Meet at the Cable Car Entrance Lookout, Upland Road, Kelburn

description:
Reimagining Construction: Participatory, Circular, and Open by Design, This presentation examines how the principles of participatory, circular, and open design can transform construction by bringing human and ecological values into the heart of how we build.
Drawing from both ancient timber framing traditions and modern innovations, it demonstrates how modularity and standardisation unlock flexibility, efficiency, and customisation while supporting sustainable, locally-rooted solutions.
A key focus is placed on design for disassembly and reassembly, enabling buildings and their components to be reused and adapted over time—reducing waste, lowering environmental impact, and fostering resilience for current and future communities.

description:

Discover the incredible potential of generating your own local power through solar panels, micro wind turbines, and more. With dynamic demos, interactive exhibits, and expert talks, you'll learn how easy and empowering it is to electrify your home, reduce your reliance on fossil fuels, and enjoy the long-term benefits of clean energy.
But that’s just the beginning. The expo goes beyond saving money and helping the planet—it’s about building stronger, more resilient communities. By embracing local energy generation, we can protect ourselves from rising energy costs, power outages, and climate extremes. It’s about creating homes and neighbourhoods that are not just efficient, but future-proof.
So whether you're a curious homeowner, a savvy business owner, or just someone who wants to be part of something positive, Electrify the Hutt is your place to plug in. Come connect with experts, explore cutting-edge tech, and join a growing movement to electrify our lives and build a cleaner, brighter Hutt Valley for everyone.
Add the date to your calendar now!

Friends of Taupo Swamp & Catchment Inc was formed in 2018 by a group of Porirua residents. Restoration activities have been focused on the part of the wetlands that is owned by Porirua City Council.

Back in 2018 there was a lot of blackberry on the site. Now, some of the 2019-20 locally sourced plantings by volunteers are 5 metres high. By the end of the current planting season FOTSC will have put in 18K plants. An adjacent planting programme around the Plimmerton Domain coordinated by the local Rotary Club and the Porirua City Council has resulted in 22k trees and plants.

Please register your interest/intention to attend by flicking a short email to . In the event of bad weather we will advise cancellation by 8am and rescheduling for a week later.

description:
W**d Releasing Bee, W**d Releasing Bee
Kia Ora Koutou

Now we have finished planting for the year we are turning our attention to the all-important W**d releasing. This is essential to keeping the young plants that we have planted this winter from being overwhelmed by grass and weeds. Also mulching to help with moisture retention over those hot summer days. Our next weeding is on Saturday 29th November, meeting at the Nursery on Hana Udy Place at 9.00am. Gloves, tools and sunscreen provided along with a morning tea and an update on the Reserve afterward. Hope to see you there! Feel free to bring a friend and encourage others to join us. This will be our last volunteer activity for the year.

Also over summer we may need some help in watering the plants we put in this year, I will do a post if we need extra help.


Health & Safety Notice: When working in the Reserve, please ensure your clothing and footwear are appropriate for expected weather conditions (cold, wet or hot) and the terrain (such as holes, hidden objects, blackberry, overhead branches). Trip hazards and slippery tracks are a possibility. Stay clear of waterways. Be cautious if you have allergies and carry the necessary medication. Enjoy your time in the Reserve and please avoid blocking pathways used by other users. Report any accidents, injuries or possible hazards to the H&S Coordinator (021 071 5364).

description:
Botanical Insights talk - Ancient plants, This talk, which partners a major pictorial display at the Tree House, starts with a look at landscapes preceding the rise of trees. It then focuses on the Silurian, Devonian and Carboniferous geological periods, characterised by the appearance of the likes of scale trees, seed ferns and calamites.….and the swamp forests that gave rise to the production of coal. Then it advances to the Permian period, including Gondwana prior to its eventual breakup, and the beautiful, strange, gymnosperm Glossopteris that was widespread in the ancient southern continents, embracing what is now New Zealand/Aotearoa. Finally, it progresses to the world of ‘living fossils’ such as ginkgo and the even more primitive horsetails. Attention will be drawn to some of the animals that populated this ancient world.

Located in the Treehouse Seminar Room, Wellington Botanic Garden ki Paekākā.
Free of charge. You’re welcome to bring along your own ‘brown bag’ lunch to enjoy during the talk.

The speaker is Reg Harris, a volunteer guide [botany] at Wellington Botanic Garden.
Reg delivers botany lectures for diverse audiences, delving into plant interactions, adaptive radiation, tropisms, and more. As a dedicated volunteer guide at Wellington Botanic Garden (WBG), he pioneered collaborations between WBG and Victoria University, organising annual field labs for biology students.

description:
Tip Track Marathon: Cloud 9, Tip Track Marathon: Cloud 9
START TIMES:
7am - Marathon
9am - Half Marathon

Website: tiptrackmarathon.co.nz
Rego: https://tip-track-marathon.lilregie.com/booking/attendees/new

COURSE DETAILS:
Tip Track Marathon (TTM) –
Tip Track Marathon is an out-and-back, out-and-back, out-and-back, out-and-back, out-and-back, out-and-back type course that has proven popular with participants. Each of the six laps is slightly over 7km, with around 400m in elevation (2500m in total). Try not to trip on a loose rock while taking in the aromatic smells emanating from the tip. Ignore the gale-force northerly. It makes for a challenging marathon but you can find comfort in the other runners suffering too.
Recommended donation: $40

Half Marathon -
For those that hate themselves but not as much as the marathoners. Same course as above but three laps instead of six,
Recommended donation: $25

Tip Track Marathon (TTM) is a low-key, no frills trail running race that takes place at the Tip Track in Wellington each year - all in the name of charity. TTM is all about hard hills, high-fives, and doing something stupid for the sake of it. There are no prizes. Your workmates will not understand why you did this. It’s not even that fun.

This is a race for those that want a little more from life than the ordinary. Those that question the monotony of their daytime jobs and wonder, "isn't there meant to be more to life than this?" Enter TTM... I mean, you won't find any answers here but those are the people that tend to be attracted to it.

DONATIONS:
All entry fees charged go to our charities for 2025:
Youthline Wellington
Social Supermarket

There is a recommended donation amount for each race of $40 (TTM) or $25 (Half). These are recommended only. If you are not in a position to donate, then give whatever you can, if you earn a bit more then feel free to pop in a bit more.
Previous years we have raised:

2024 - $5,500 for the Social Supermarket and OMF
2023 - $5,125 for the Social Supermarket
2022 - $3,600 split between Trailblazers and Wellington Women's Refuge
2021 - $3,770 split between Trailblazers and Wellington Women's Refuge
2020 - $3,376 split between Challenge 2000 and Wellington Women's Refuge


The day is a celebration of the Wellington running community. It is a social, unofficial event. Come along for a run, to support, and meet some new friends along the way.

description:
Inter-Club Football Match: 30th Anniversary of the Aldo Cuccurullo Memorial Trophy, This year marks the 30th anniversary of the annual football game between Club Garibaldi (Wellington) and Club Italia (Nelson).
We would like to invite past and present players to be a part of this event.
Please save the date. Details to follow in due course, including the time and location of the game and the after match function.
***
Aldo Cuccurullo Memorial Trophy
Each year Club Garibaldi of Wellington and Club Italia of Nelson interact through a soccer game between representatives of the two clubs.
The game is in memory of Aldo Cuccurullo, a young member of Club Italia who tragically died at a very young age.
Aldo was known as a gentle giant who gave of his time not only to play rugby league himself, but also to coach children.
The game, as you would expect of Italian football, is a tense one. Member supporters of each club travel to be at the game but the weekend is as much an opportunity to socialise and build friendships as it is to play football.

description:
February Shuffleboard League Night, Come along to our monthly shuffleboard league!
Register here: https://www.meandoses.co.nz/shuffleboard
Shuffleboard pairs tournaments start at 6pm on the last Wednesday of each month.
PRIZES
1st place: $60 of Taproom Vouchers + 1st place ribbon + Your team name immortalised on the honours board
2nd place: $40 of Taproom Vouchers + 2nd place ribbon
3rd place: $20 of Taproom Vouchers + 3rd place ribbon
Spin the wheel of prizes if you get eliminated!
Check out the league over at Challonge.com
https://challonge.com/communities/meanshuffle
Tournament Rules:
This is a double elimination format tournament. Teams must lose twice to be eliminated. Matches are played according to standard shuffleboard rules as can be read here:
https://www.theshufflehouse.com/.../how-to-play...
EXCEPT:
- There is a 10 MINUTE time limit each match.
- Matches are played FIRST TO TEN.
- In the event of a draw after the 10 minute time limit, a "Golden End" will be played. The winner of the Golden End wins the match.
- Pucks that do not pass the foul line are removed from play.
- The shuffleboard will be reset and resanded after each match.
- No sand will be added during a match.

description:
Wellington Rowing Club AGM, Lotto 8’s & Season Opening, The Wellington Rowing Club Annual General Meeting (AGM) and opening of the season will be held on Sunday 24 August 2024, 12 noon, at the WRC Function Centre.
We look forward to seeing you all at the Club at 9:30 for Lotto 8’s (weather permitting).
Following the meetings, a light lunch will be provided. This is also WRC’s opportunity to unveil the Function Space renovations to the membership.

Information about the AGM
The WRC AGM will commence at 12noon, in the WRC Function Centre. There will be provisions for online attendance.
At this year’s AGM, we have several board and committee positions that we will be seeking to fill. Many hands make light work, and we all depend on volunteers to keep the club growing and thriving. A variety of experience levels, skill sets, and perspectives are needed for a well-functioning club.
The positions to be filled this year are:
-Club Captain (2-year term, Officer role)
-Vice President (2-year term, Officer role)
-Club Treasurer (2-year term, Officer role)
-Club Secretary (2-year term, Officer role)
-3 Committee roles (1-year term, non-Officer role)
-3 Board members (2-year term, Officer role)
Officers of the must consent in writing to being an officer and certify that they are eligible and not disqualified from holding office as described under section 36B of the Charities Act 2005.
The Club by-laws outline the current role descriptions, can be found here, note that the roles can be tailored to suit individuals’ skill sets and experience. Mentoring and support can be provided to develop individuals in these positions.
If you are interested, but have further questions- please reach out to the people holding the current role or any of the board or committee members to discuss.

description:
2025 Corporate Challenge Wellington, Team work makes the dream work at Corporate Challenge Wellington! join us on Wednesday, 12th November for a fun-filled night of team building, fitness and friendly competition.
Nestled between Te Papa and Chaffers Dock, right in the centre of the city, our event HQ at Waitangi Park unlocks the gorgeous Wellington waterfront. The course takes in all the sights - Te Papa, Whairepo Lagoon and the Well_ngton sign - as it follows the wide public esplanade around the harbour. Views forever and usually (always) a decent wind to chase you to the finish line.
After you finish your 5k - running or walking a flat, two-lap course around the waterfront - grab a banana and water or sport drink before heading back to your tent to see how your mates did. The best performances of the day - plus our amazing team managers - are acknowledged at the Awards Cermony at 7:30pm. Spot prizes wrap up around 8pm and there's time to exchange war stories as you pack down.

description:
New Zealand Pinball Championships 2025, Welcome to the New Zealand Pinball Championships 2025!
This is part of a 4 day event - Fri/Sat/Sun/Mon - 16/17/18/19 Jan 2026.
The top 48 performers over 2025 qualify to play in the NZ Champs at Ye Olde Pinball Shoppe in Wellington.
Upstairs from Ye Olde Pinball Shoppe, our good mates at Fork & Brewer have opened up their bar to us for the event. To spread out the large number of players, we will have banks of games located upstairs, this will be a great spot to wind down and enjoy some food and drink.
Saturday 17th:
New Zealand Champs Qualifying - 9am-9:30pm (estimate)
This event is for the top 48 performers over 2025 and has a 150% points boost.
- Doors open 9am, tournament starts at 9:30am
- 7 rounds of 4 player match play
- 3 games per round
- Software will choose a bank of three games from the banks available.
- Player order will automatically rotate each game.
- Top 24 make finals
- Top 8 receive 1 bye
- One hour lunch break
Sunday 18th:
New Zealand Champs Finals - 9am-2pm (estimate)
Top 24 play off, best of 5 matches head to head.
- Top 8 receive one bye.
- Highest seed picks a bank of games and has games 1,3,5.
- Lower seed picks a bank of games, and has games 2 and 3.
- Games in each bank can be played in any order, but not more than once in a 5 game match.
The winner of the NZ National Championship receives qualification to the IFPA World Championship (along with the #1 ranked NZ player as of December 31st). Top 16 place getters will receive prize money, gathered from the $1 IFPA fee you have been paying to play in tournaments over the 2025 year. These amounts will be announced prior to the event.

description:
Corporate Trades Laykold Cup, Wellington's biggest one-day track racing carnival with racing for all ages and abilities.
Sponsored by Corporate Trades
The one they all want to win! The Laykold Cup was first presented in 1928 and in recent years a Stayers Cup for masters, and a Poneke Plate for women have been added.
We'll run a graded programme of supporting races with A, B, C and D grades except for the trophy races which riders can pick one to take part in. There'll also be a junior wheelrace handicapped race with its own prize.

Junior: 3 lap Handicap Wheelrace (under 11, 13, 15 and 17)

This event is also the second round of the prestigious Wellington Cup track cycling series, with great racing for all ages and abilities!

description:
Harcourt Park spring league (Santioned), Spring League at Harcourt park is returning
Mark Wednesday the 29th October - 17 December 2025 on the calender, as this is the start of the next PDGA sanctioned league at Harcourt park, Upper Hutt brought to you by the Greater Wellington Disc Golf Club.
The league will be for 8 weeks til 5th and offer divisions by PDGA rating. If you don’t have a rating TD will assign you to a ratings group based on level of skill and experience.
Costs for the league is preferred to pay in advance or at first round and priorty will go to club members if the league reaches greater than 45 participants.
League Entry Fee:
$10 for Greater Wellington disc golf club members
$20 for Non club member (required to pay in advance of league or on first day of play).
$5 for casual play (required to pay on day of round).
League fees can be paid in cash on the night or direct into the club league account (preferred). Please use Spring25 & your last name as reference.
Kiwi Bank: 38-9016-0650973-03
Prizes - Divisional Winners will receive braging rights, two prizes will be provided to the 2 participants that shoot the most points above their rating in a single round (every one has a change at taking this one out)
Also there will be an optional extra jackpotting ace kitty. For the league. How to enter - gold coin entry each week. To be in the running for the week need to have paid in. Eg so week one pay $1 and in if hit an ace, week 2 not pay in therefore no entry into ace kitty, week 3 pay into ace kitty. Hit an ace week 2 can’t claim the kitty as did not pay in, hit an ace week 3 collect the ace kitty (shared if others also hit an ace that week).
You say what happens if no one hits an ace - keeps jackpotting throughout the clubs PDGA sanctioned leagues until an ACE is hit.
Current ACE Kitty sitting at $xxx

description:
XTrail Spring - Rangituhi Rumble, XTrail Spring - Rangituhi Rumble
Rangituhi serves up a lush and leg-burning journey through one of Porirua’s most scenic spots. Starting from Spicers Botanical Park, all three courses wind their way up through the peaceful, green trails of Spicers Forest before breaking into the rugged beauty of the Colonial Knob Reserve. The short course offers a more forgiving climb with playful zig zags and a fun descent, while the long course takes you all the way to the summit of Rangituhi, a proper challenge rewarded with stunning views over Porirua Harbour and beyond. With a mix of forest trails, farm tracks, and fast-flowing descents, this one’s a true highlight of the region.

https://barefootxtrail.co.nz/

description:
24-hour Squash Marathon for Wayne, 24 Hours on Court for Wayne.
This time I’m picking up my squash racket for a challenge like no other: 24 hours of nonstop squash. Why? Because someone I care for has been facing a far tougher battle.
My father-in-law was recently diagnosed with Chronic Lymphocytic Leukemia (CLL). For years he lived undiagnosed, pushing through symptoms without answers. Now that we finally know what he’s fighting, I want to turn my energy into something that can help him—and others—on their journeys.
According to CLL Advocates NZ, only 10-15 people out of 250 on average per year benefit from the budget allocation. And many are not even able to receive the same treatment options here in NZ.
Squash has always been a sport of endurance, resilience, and determination. Playing for 24 hours is my way of honoring his fight, raising awareness, and bringing people together to support the cause.
You can help by:
Donating: Any amount you would like to.
Predicting: Predicting how many points and games will be played in 24 hours.
Joining me on court: Book a slot to play a game, cheer from the sidelines, or come be part of the atmosphere.
Buy raffle tickets, or sponsor a prize towards the raffle.
We would like to give a shout out to our current sponsors;
Hair Phoria,
Aimee Photography,
X-Potential,
Mitchells,
Meaters,
CBD Eatery,
Forrest Wines and
Fat Tony's
No matter how small, your contribution will make a difference. Together we can transform his story of late diagnosis into one of community, awareness, and action.
I’ll be pushing myself through the night, but I won’t be alone—your support will keep me going with every serve, rally, and game. Let’s make this 24 hours count.

Even more special, Wellington will be hosting Pistang Pilipino for the first time in 10 years through Streams of Life Charitable Trust, bringing together Filipino families, players and communities from all over New Zealand.

And this year, we’re adding something NEW and HISTORIC…
The FIRST‑EVER Filipino Pickleball Tournament in Pistang Pilipino history! A fun, competitive, and community‑driven event.

Calling all Filipino Pickleball Players!

This 11th April of 2026, we’re starting things off with the First Filipino Pickleball Fundraising Tournament! All proceeds will go towards supporting Pistang Pilipino 2026 through Streams of Life Charitable Trust, helping us make this celebration bigger and better for everyone.

description:
December Shuffleboard Pairs Comp, Come along to our monthly shuffleboard league!
Register here: https://www.meandoses.co.nz/shuffleboard
Shuffleboard pairs tournaments are 6pm to 9.30pm at the latest (usually earlier), the last Wednesday of each month.
Check out the league over at Challonge.com
https://challonge.com/communities/meanshuffle
Tournament Rules:
This is a double elimination format tournament. Teams must lose twice to be eliminated. Matches are played according to standard shuffleboard rules as can be read here:
https://www.theshufflehouse.com/.../how-to-play...
EXCEPT:
- There is a 10 MINUTE time limit each match.
- Matches are played FIRST TO TEN.
- In the event of a draw after the 10 minute time limit, a "Golden End" will be played. The winner of the Golden End wins the match.
- Pucks that do not pass the foul line are removed from play.
- The shuffleboard will be reset and resanded after each match.
- No sand will be added during a match.
- The final match is played first to 15 with no time limit - if time allows.
- Every "Hanger" is rewarded with a sweet treat!
PRIZES
1st place: $60 of Taproom Vouchers + 1st place ribbon + Your team name immortalised on the honours board
2nd place: $40 of Taproom Vouchers + 2nd place ribbon

description:
Blue Dragon Book Fair 2026, The Blue Dragon Book Fair 2026 will be held in the Ngaio Town Hall, as usual, on the weekend of 18 and 19 April. We are accepting donations of good-quality books, puzzles and board games from Saturday 21 March to 5pm Tuesday 14 April.
In recent years, we have been receiving huge numbers of donations for which we are very grateful. However, the space in the hall is tight, and our capacity to sort through so many donations needs to be managed. For those reasons, the 2026 book fair will NOT accept donations of:
• CDs, DVDs and records
• Travel guides, self-help books, business books and textbooks
• Food books and gardening books before 2020.
This decision is based on the fact that these books and CDs etc don’t sell very well but take up a lot of space.
We have a much-loved section of quality children's books for only $1 each. That's because the trustees all value reading and know how important it is for children to read.
Drop your donations from Saturday 21 March at Kennards Storage, 19 Hutt Road, in the downstairs area on the right-hand side, labelled 'Blue Dragon'. Please, no books after Tuesday 14 April. Kennards Storage is open M-F 8.30am-6pm; Sat 9am-5pm; Sunday 10am-4pm. It is closed on Easter Friday and open on Easter Monday from 10am to 4pm. Check Kennards’ website for opening hours
If you have any questions or want to help, please email us at bluedragonnz@gmail.com

Join us for a community market where every purchase makes a difference. All stallholders will be donating a minimum of 10% of their profits to our cause, with some generously contributing 100% of their profits.
There will be coffee, food, crafts, clothing, cat furniture, and much more. If you attend just one of our events this year, make it this one. Your support will help us open our doors and provide a safe, dedicated space for the cats in our care.

description:
Ceramicus 2025, Now in its 67th year, Ceramicus returns to the Wellington Botanic Garden’s Begonia House from 5–15 November!
Presented by the Wellington Potters’ Association in partnership with Wellington City Council, this much-loved annual exhibition celebrates the creativity and diversity of contemporary ceramics in the Wellington region.
This year we’re thrilled to welcome Aaron Scythe as our Guest Selector and Featured Artist. Known for blending Japanese and New Zealand ceramic traditions, Aaron brings both mastery of technique and a spirit of experimentation to his work. His curatorial eye promises an exhibition that’s thoughtful, dynamic, and full of surprises.
Don’t miss our Pop-Up Pottery Market!
Alongside the exhibition, join us on 8 & 9 November and 15 & 16 November for the Ceramicus Pop-Up Pottery Market, held just outside the Begonia House. Browse and buy directly from local potters, explore a range of handcrafted ceramics—from elegant tableware to unique statement pieces—and take home something special. The market is free and open to everyone, a perfect way to meet the makers and support Wellington’s vibrant ceramics community.
Exhibition: 5–15 November 2025, 9am - 4pm daily
Begonia House, Wellington Botanic Garden
Pop-Up Market: 8 & 9 and 15 & 16 November, 10am - 3pm (weather dependent)
Free entry

Whether you are looking for one-of-a-kind gifts, home décor, fashion or art, there is something for everyone at the Martinborough Fair.

Martinborough Fair highlights include:

- A large number of stalls offering jewellery and clothing, pottery, woodwork, metalwork, candles, plants, dried flowers, pet accessories and so much more.

- Fresh produce including fruits, vegetables, herbs, honey, and other foodie delights.
- Food stalls and local vendors offering delicious food, snacks, and beverages to satisfy every craving.

- Live entertainment from local musicians.

- Family-friendly fun activities including big bubbles and face painting.

Mark your calendars and bring your friends and family for a fun day out or make going to the Fair part of a weekend stay. For the latest Martinborough Fair updates and sneak peeks, follow us on social media.
The Martinborough Fair is owned by South Wairarapa Rotary and all proceeds from the fairs are distributed to charities and organisations especially those in South Wairarapa.

description:
Fabric-a-brac 2025, Fabric-a-brac returns to Wellington for our second event in 2025!

The first Fabric-a-brac was held in Wellington 15 years ago - we're sew excited to still be here, bring the sewing community together and raising money for Mary Potter Hospice.
Fabric-a-brac is a fun fabric and sewing supplies market where you can shop for vintage and modern sewing and upholstery fabric, patterns, haberdashery and more. It brings the sewing community together to share stashes, stories and delight in our mutual love of textiles.
Fabric-a-brac is volunteer-run, and is a fundraiser for hospice - in Wellington this is Mary Potter Hospice. All profits from this event are donated to their work. We've raised $80,000 since our first event in 2009.
Selling fabric
If you have a stash you'd like to sell, you can come and take a stall. You pay a stallholder fee (which goes to Hospice) but anything you make on the day is yours to keep. Stalls are allocated via a ballot, which will take place closer to the time. Go to our website to find the form. Closes mid August.

If you have fabric, haberdashery or patterns you'd like to move on, but don't want to have a table, you can donate items. This will all go onto the hospice table on the day, and all profits will go to their wonderful work.
Fabric donation points
- Made Marion
- Mary Potter Hospice - you can take donations to the Mary Potter Hospice in Mein St, Newtown

Pop-up cafe
The fabulous cafe is back with all of its gorgeous volunteers and fab food. There will be tea, coffee and sweet treats for sale, so you can sit down and chat over your new projects and fabric purchases. All proceeds from the cafe also go to Hospice.
Buying fabric
Bring cash - most stallholders and the cafe will be selling by cash only.
We look forward to seeing our fabulous fabric and sewing community soon!
*Saturday September 20 2025
*St Anne's Parish Hall, Emmett Street, Newtown, Wellington

description:
Adopt a plant: Begonia House fundraiser, Support the Friends of Wellington Botanic Garden and grab yourself a bargain. We’ve kept the plants we need for propagation, but we have many healthy plants left over that we can’t fit into our nursery. That’s where you come in – take home one of our plants and help the Friends fundraise to go beyond the basics with amazing new features like exterior landscaping and internal fixtures for the renewed Begonia House.

These plants are from the display end and thrive in indoor conditions (they are coming from a glasshouse after all). They should do just fine in your house. Have too many house plants? Wrong. You can definitely fit just one more. It’s for charity after all.

We’ll have some available for loose change and others for a minimum donation that won’t break the bank. Payment by cash or bank transfer only (no EFTPOS). All proceeds go to the Friends of Wellington Botanic Garden to put towards the Begonia House enhancements https://givealittle.co.nz/cause/the-begonia-house-the-buildings-and-beyond

Get prepped:
- Share the love! 5 plants max per person.
- Plants come as they are – we practice integrated pest management but you may need to give them a quarantine before joining your other plant pals at home.
- Swap your pot – there will be a station where you’ll swap your plant to a different pot. We need all our A+ pots for our future plants. We’ll be around to help.
- BYOB – bring your own bag or box for transporting your new friend home.
- Early bird gets the plant – no holds.

We’ll post an update in our Wellington Gardens Facebook event page a few days out of what plants will be for sale. Make sure you RSVP to get notified.

A piece of Begonia House is awaiting your adoption; We’ll see you there!
label: Markets & Fairs

description:
Kāpiti Food Fair 2025, All FOODIES and your tribe are invited to an exciting and indulgent day out that leaves a lasting impression on food lovers that will have you returning year after year. We take pride in our offering of GOOD food, GOOD music, and GREAT company for food enthusiasts – the ultimate Food Festival with something for everyone backed with a great vibe and professional entertainment to enjoy all day long!
On the menu for 2025 ……. up to 250 vendor sites including an off licence Liquor Zone, a licenced Tuatara Bar & Thames Pacific Main Stage, ANZ Kāpiti Kitchen Demo Stage, Kāpiti Ice Cream Dining Room, FREE Kid’s Zone, ZEAL Zone buskers, roving street performers and much more!
GETTING THERE: Walk | Bike | Drive | FREE bus from Paraparaumu Railway Station
ALL WELCOME + crowd friendly dogs on lead
LOOK out for the NEW shady picnic zone!
THAMES PACIFIC MAIN STAGE
9:45 AM – 9:50 AM | Official Mayoral Opening
10:00 AM – 10:30 AM | Devon Welch Music
10:45 AM – 11:15 AM | Intercite
11:45 AM – 12:45 PM | Jody & the Stowaways
1:15 PM – 2:15 PM | The Beat Girls
2:45 PM – 3:45 PM | The Relatives
4:15 PM – 5:15 PM | Love Shack
MERCHANDISE: $3 Bags, $5 T-towels, $10 Aprons (not sold online)
NOTE: Humanitix booking fees will be added to the cost of your ticket and paid at check-out.  Booking fees cover Humanitix costs, and everything else goes to their education projects that help to change lives.  Kāpiti Food Fair supports Manaiakalani Education Trust for Māori, Pasifika, and disadvantaged learners in Aotearoa to increase student outcomes and get equal access to digital education.
TOP TIPS for a FANTASTIC DAY at the Kāpiti Food Fair
Bring a gold coin for parking donations.
Pack a hat and sunscreen – complimentary SPF is available if you need it.
Bring a picnic blanket to relax, enjoy the ambiance, and savor your treats with plenty of seating and a NEW shaded picnic spot under the trees.
BYO your own chair if you wish and a chilly bin for purchases!
Plan your foodie route with the Site Map >>
Visit the Tuatara Bar & Thames Pacific Main Stage and relax over a craft brew, wine, local cocktails and delicious cider while enjoying the vibe of the 80's to 90's revival party all day long!  Children are welcome with their parent or guardian.  CHEERS to that!
MOST vendors accept EFTPOS or bank transfer.  BYO cash as there is no ATM available.
THANK YOU SPONSORS
Kāpiti Food Fair is a recipient of the Kāpiti Major Events Fund and is supported by numerous local businesses in-kind and reduced cost of services.  The Fair is a commercial event however we have a social agenda too.  We choose to support Kāpiti Community Patrol, Kāpiti Rotary Charitable Trust, No 49 Squadron Air Cadets, Paekākāriki Surf Lifesavers, Paraparaumu College, and Work Ready Kāpiti to help run key logistics on the day. 
Kāpiti Food Fair Ltd is privately owned and operated by Helene Judge and Jeanine van Kradenburg.  The Fair reserves the right to use any image captured by our official photographer for promotional purposes in any media, in perpetuity without compensation, unless not consented.
#KFF2025 #FoodFestival #KāpitiFoodFair #SomethingForEveryone #VisitKāpiti
Website >> | Facebook >> | Instagram >> | YouTube >> | TikTok >>

description:
Erskine Chapel Market & Open Day, Erskine Chapel Market & Open Day
✨ Erskine Chapel Open Day & Craft Market ✨
📅 Sunday, 2 November 2025 | 10:00am – 3:00pm
📍 Erskine Chapel, 31 Avon Street, Island Bay, Wellington

Nestled high on the hillside in Island Bay, Erskine Chapel is a breathtaking and iconic landmark, with sweeping views across the hills and ocean, and a gothic charm that has captured hearts for generations.

Now in its third year, this much loved annual event returns as part of Heritage Month, celebrating the beauty, history, and cultural significance of one of Wellington’s most treasured buildings.

Foxtail Events and The Wellington Company are once again partnering to open the chapel’s doors for the community, inviting everyone to experience this remarkable space brought back to life.

Join us for a special Community Open Day and Craft Market, where you can:

* Explore a curated market of local artisans and makers
* Discover the chapel’s rich history and stunning architecture
* Enjoy live music, freshly brewed coffee, and delicious baked goods

Whether you're drawn by the history, the creativity, or the peaceful atmosphere, this is a day to celebrate local talent and honour the legacy of a true Wellington landmark.

🗓️ Mark your calendar and don’t miss this enchanting day at Erskine Chapel. A place of stories, memories, and timeless beauty, reawakened for a new generation to enjoy.

description:
Otaki Market - February, Nau Mai Haere Mai, meet you at the Market.
Discover a vibrant outdoor market nestled on State Highway One in Ōtaki, just across from New World. Join us to greet our regular stallholders and special guests from the community.
---
PARKING
There is plenty of free parking available at the Otaki train station.
Main Highway is a perfect spot for customers to the market
60 Minute Parking zone behind Riverstone Cafe.

CASH / ATM
Some vendors have eftpos, and all take cash. There is an ATM located on the main highway.

FOOD VENDORS
And when hunger strikes, our lineup of food trucks and stalls is ready to tantalize your taste buds. From waffles to hangi, seafood chowder to fry bread, sausage sizzles, and a delectable assortment of rice, noodles, and home baking – a feast awaits.

BUSKER
We often have an awesome busker, who'll be playing chilled out middle of the road tunes.

- Art & Craft
- Native trees, flowers and plants
- Candles, soaps and wax melts
- Antiques and collectables
- Produce, including yummy honey!
- Pre-love clothing, shoes and accessories
- Tools for the home and garden
- Toys and books for tamariki

---
STALL HOLDERS
Would you like to be a stall holder?
Join our market family, fill out the enquiry form: www.otakiwomensclub.org/stall-holder
label: Markets & Fairs

description:
Rewa Rewa Station - Mini Mill Tours, From fleece to yarn - We welcome visitors of all ages to a personal tour of our Mill and wool Studio shop.

You will learn the entire process from shorn raw wool to our beautifully finished yarn. Visitors will take a tour on our upper mezzanine looking down on the production floor to learn how wool is washed, dried, combed and spun. Afterwards there is an opportunity to purchase natural quality yarn and other woollen products.

Our Mini Mill is located on our working sheep and beef farm in Tinui. You may have the opportunity to see our Corriedale, Polwarth, Gotland and Romney sheep during your visit. All wool processed in our mill is from our own sheep and we can trace the yarn back to a single sheep ensuring the provenance of all our products.

The tour will take approximately 1.5 hours and a minimum of 4 persons are required. Booking is essential and it costs $25pp.
24 hours minimum booking time required and subject to availability

You can book via email pvieno@hotmail.com or telephone 06- 372-6990

description:
Otaki and Te Horo Producers Market, We’re teaming up with the Otaki and Te Horo Business Association for a special Producers Market
A vibrant local market showcasing fresh, seasonal produce, handmade goods, and artisan treats from Otaki and Te horo. Brings together the community to support small businesses, enjoy live music, and discover unique, locally made products.

Producers (From Otaki and Te Horo)
- Fresh Fruit & Veggies
- Beautiful Blooms, trees and plants
- Free-Range Eggs
- Local Olives & Olive Oil
- Seasonal Produce
- Coffee & Tea
- Small Batch Jams, Chutneys & Honey
- Crafted Wine, Cider, Beer, Spirits & Liqueurs
- Juices & Non-Alcoholic Beverages
- Sauces, Chocolates & Sweets
- Ice Cream, Cream, Milk, Cheese & Dairy Products
- Crackers, Breads, Pies & Pastries

ENQUIRE: www.otakiwomensclub.org/producers-market
- All producers must be based within Otaki & Te Horo boundaries.
- Producers must grow or produce their own products

Craft Stalls & Food Trucks:
Art & Craft, jewellery, earrings, soaps, original art, candles. Plants, flowers, trees, garden art. Collectables (limited to five sites). Handmade craft, health and beautify.
ENQUIRE: www.otakiwomensclub.org/stall-holder

description:
Small Wheels Pride, Come and join Ngā Wīra Iti Collective: the vibrant, diverse, and lively small wheels communities of Te Whanganui a Tara, as we create joyful resistance, and take part in some small wheels activities in conjunction with, and alongside, Out in the City. We'll be holding a Roller Pride Parade, skate sessions, a mini Roller Disco, demonstrations, and more!
Seaside Skates and Richter City Roller Derby will both have stalls, and there'll be an opportunity to give skating a go and get some pointers. Who knows? You could end up signing up for Quad Goals an introduction to Roller Derby, find yourself at the Skatefit lessons at Kilbirnie Recreation Centre, or hanging out with the many other amazing small wheels communities around the region.

description:
Watercolour Paint and Wine Night in Wellington - Bunny Blooms, Turn wine into watercolour! Take a brush in one hand, a drink in the other, and let’s paint a watercolour masterpiece!

Join us for a beginner's watercolour painting event full of fluid colour mixing and delicate brushwork where you’ll craft your own bespoke watercolour artwork. One of our watercolour experts will guide step-by-step through the watercolour process and will encourage you to add your own unique touch.

Drinks: No drinks are included in the ticket price, but drinks are available for purchase.

Food: A delicious menu will be available to purchase throughout the event.

What is Paintvine?
It’s a fun social painting class where our artists will run you through step-by-step instructions on how to create your very own masterpiece in a fun and relaxed bar setting. Perfect for dates, catching up with mates or for meeting new friends!

We provide all the materials you need (high-quality watercolour paper, watercolour paint, brushes). All you need to bring is your creative spirit (or find some at the bottom of a glass). All our events are in licensed venues, so you can purchase drinks to sip as you create! At the end of the night, you will leave with your own watercolour painting!

For tickets, future events, FAQs and more info head to the Paintvine website ⇒ paintvine.co.nz

description:
An Evening of Travel With Wendy Wu Tours, We’re delighted to invite you and friends to a special Travel Information Evening with Wendy Wu Tours – New Zealand’s leading touring specialist.

Known for their fully-inclusive, expertly guided tours, Wendy Wu Tours take the stress out of travel while unlocking some of the world’s most extraordinary destinations. From the cherry blossoms of Japan and the wonders of China, to the treasures of the Middle East, the Four Stans and Africa, a special Women’s only tour in Sept 2026 and the colourful landscapes of South East Asia, Central and South America – there’s a journey for every traveller.

Whether you’re planning your next adventure or simply curious to learn more, this is a relaxed, no-obligation evening designed to inspire.

description:
Family Shiny - Akatarawa Forestt, Registrations open NOW
What to expect
This is a “Family Shiny” trip into the Akatarawa Forest.
Non members are welcome to join us but must register to obtain details of start time and location.
Vehicles must have a low ratio gearbox and must not be a 4wd car, station wagon or van, and if a soft top must have a roll cage.
Visitor Registration- $20.00 per driver
There is no trip fee for this trip however non-members pay a “Day Membership Fee” of $20. This is payable in Cash to the Trip Leader on the day.
The trip should be relatively easy as it needs to cater for people just starting out and all types of 4wd vehicles – but a good way to see if you want to get into 4wding and a chance to meet some of our club members (if you are unsure if your vehicle is suitable just ask).
No Dogs Allowed sorry
Once you have completed 3 Family Shinys you can join the club but can only participate in Family Shiny or Touring Class trips. Once you have also done a Training Day and had your vehicle inspected by one of our approved inspectors you can come along on our other more adventurous trips. To find out more on how to join our club go to https://ccvcwellington.wildapricot.org/page-1725861.
Like our Facebook page to find out about more trips and club nights.

description:
Card Merchant Wellington - Catan Settlement Event #1 2026, Card Merchant is excited to be part of the 2026 Catan Organized Play program here in New Zealand, providing an opportunity for players from all over the country to get involved, learn, and enjoy the game in a structured event setting.
These Catan Settlement Events are designed as an accessible entry point into organized play, giving players a chance to experience tournament-style games ahead of more competitive events later in the year.
Event Details:
Store: Card Merchant Wellington
Dates: 9th May 2026
Time: 11:00 AM
Entry Fee: $20
Event Structure:
Players will engage in multiple rounds to determine overall standings for the event.
Preliminary Round:
Each participant will play three separate games of base set Catan.
Opponents will rotate for each game.
At the conclusion of the preliminary round, players will be ranked based on their overall performance across all games.
Final Rounds:
Depending on player numbers, top-performing players may be split into final tables to determine overall placings.
If player numbers are lower, the event may instead consist solely of multiple Swiss-style rounds, with final standings determined by total points or wins.
This event is designed to balance structure with accessibility, giving players a clear tournament experience while keeping the environment welcoming and easy to engage with.
Prizes:
4x Summer Hex Kits
2x Gamegenic Catan Sleeves
2x Exit: Adventures on Catan
1x Catan Seafarers Duel
Whether you’re new to Catan or a long-time player, this is a great opportunity to get involved, meet other players, and take part in organized play. Join us at Card Merchant for a fun and welcoming day of Catan!
label: Hobbies & Interests

description:
WCC "Tequila and Salsa night!", Hello again bartenders from all walks of life! Brush up on your Tequila knowledge and your Salsa recipes because the next Wellington Cocktail Competition is just around the corner!
As to the theme for this WCC, it is “Tequila and Salsa food pairing” The hero spirit this time around will be a surprise tequila on the night, but I can tell you it will be a reposado. The catch is, you have to pair it with a salsa that you make yourself. There are no limits on the style of cocktail you want this time around, so get creative!
If you would like to participate, get in touch with me or Dan ASAp as we were running low on time to organize this time around.
Minimum 30ml of Tequila must be used in the drink and as always must be the hero spirit of the night.
You will be making two drinks for the judges to try and 5 minutes to make two servings of your cocktail and serve your salsa.
The judges may also be coerced with bribes and favours during your presentation.
The event will be on 28th of September and the competition will start at 6pm.
If you have any questions about the competition, please ask either me or Dan.
The main aim of this cocktail competition is to help those who may not have entered comps before, gain a bit of confidence and experience, or for people who haven’t entered a comp in a while, to shake off those cobwebs.
It also a chance to meet new people and introduce newcomers to the Wellington bar scene. Most of all the idea is to have fun!
Each competitor will have 5 minutes to make and present two servings of their drink.
All Tequila and corn chips for your salsa will be provided, but you will have to source your own ingredients including citrus and sugar syrup. Glassware and ice will be provided, you are more than welcome to bring specialty equipment and ingredients, however you will be marked on how easy your drink is to replicate.
The competition will be judged by a panel of 3 judges. While knowing your stuff is important, brand knowledge is not the key here. This comp is to engage bartenders in a fun comp environment, so drink and personality are just as important.
Competitors will be first come first serve, so message either me or Dan if you want to be entered, there are 10 spots for the night and there will be prizes for the winner.
There will be drink deals on the night, and make sure you bring your friends, workmates, parents ect ect to cheer you on and heckle the other competitors…
See you all there!
label: Community & Culture

description:
Feeding Frenzy Halloween Bar Crawl Wellington 2025, Feeding Frenzy Halloween Bar Crawl Wellington 2025
Packed dance floors, killer beats & spooky drinks. The frenzy hits Wellington Nov 1 — and you’re either in it or missing it.

About this Event


🩸🔥 FEEDING FRENZY HALLOWEEN BAR CRAWL – Wellington 2025 🔥🩸

When Halloween Saturday hits Wellington, the streets turn into a party jungle — and the Feeding Frenzy Halloween Bar Crawl is at the top of the food chain. Hundreds of costumed crawlers will be out in full force on Saturday, November 1st, 2025, bouncing from one high-energy bar to the next, chasing the best music, the best drinks, and the wildest crowds in the city.
This isn’t a night where you settle into one place — it’s a moving, breathing, full-costume swarm of energy. Expect packed dance floors, themed drinks that fit the season, and the kind of Halloween madness that only happens once a year.




WHAT’S IN STORE
🍹 Bars and clubs stocked with exclusive drink specials just for the crawl.
🎶 DJs pushing the energy with Halloween remixes, EDM, hip hop, and throwback anthems.
👻 Crowds dressed in everything from elaborate horror make-up to clever pop culture costumes.
📍 Venues chosen for atmosphere, music, and their ability to keep the party moving.


THE PLAN
The party starts at 7:30 PM at the first venue — grab your wristband and you’re in for the night. The feeding frenzy moves through multiple stops, all within walking distance, with each bar serving its own vibe and drink specials. By 11:30 PM, the entire crowd converges for a final blowout party that carries the night until 2 AM.

PARTICIPATING VENUES We’ll reveal the full lineup soon. Every location will be transformed for Halloween and serving themed drinks you won’t find on the menu any other night of the year.
📍 On event day, click the Crawl Map Link to see every participating bar, drink specials, and live music schedule.


🩸 Whether you’re a vampire, witch, demon, or something completely unexplainable, this is your night to roam the city in full costume and let loose with a crowd just as wild as you are.



🚨 Tickets: Starting at $15 — lock yours in now before they’re gone.

Your Ticket Includes:
🎟️ Entry to all participating bars and clubs in Wellington
🚫 No cover charges
📱 Digital crawl map access
🎵 DJs & entertainment at every venue
🍹 Exclusive Halloween drink specials




⚠️ Important Event Disclaimers
Age Requirement
Entry is strictly 18+. You must show a valid government-issued photo ID to participate. No exceptions will be made.
Wristbands
Your wristband is your pass for entry. Present your ticket or QR code at the check-in location to receive it. Wristbands must be worn throughout the night to access participating venues. If you miss the pickup window listed on the crawl map, you forfeit your access — no wristband means no entry. Lost or unclaimed wristbands cannot be replaced.
Tickets & Refunds
All sales are final. Tickets cannot be refunded, transferred, or exchanged. If the crawl is canceled without being rescheduled, the base ticket price (minus fees) will be automatically refunded. Participating venues, event hours, and drink specials may be adjusted at any time without advance notice.
Venue Entry
Each participating location has the right to refuse service or entry for reasons including intoxication, violation of dress code, inappropriate conduct, or venue capacity. If denied entry at one stop, you may visit another participating venue. No refunds will be issued under these circumstances.
Capacity & Access
Venues may reach capacity at peak times. Admission is not guaranteed once a venue is full. Late arrivals may face lines or delays. Refunds will not be issued for wait times or inability to enter specific venues.
Self-Guided Crawl
This is a self-directed event. There is no host leading the group. Use the DIGITAL CRAWL MAP LINK to navigate between stops.
Venue Awareness
Not all bar staff may know about crawl logistics. If confusion arises, contact the event representative listed on the crawl map. If that venue cannot accommodate you, move on to another open crawl location.
Drink Specials
Discounts and drink deals are set independently by each bar. They may change, sell out, or be discontinued without notice.
Event Timing
Tickets are valid only during official crawl hours listed on the digital map. Showing up early, late, or outside of the crawl schedule does not guarantee entry.
Security
All venues will conduct their own security screening. A wristband does not bypass checks.
Costumes
Costumes must be safe and respectful. Props resembling weapons or offensive attire will result in denial of entry without refund.
Notifications
To stay updated, sign up for text alerts. These will notify you of venue changes or movement times during the crawl.
Conduct
Respect staff, other attendees, and venues. Disorderly behavior, property damage, or harassment will result in immediate removal without refund.
Liability Waiver
By purchasing a ticket, you acknowledge and accept that participation carries risks. You voluntarily assume all dangers, including but not limited to: accidents, personal injury, illness, disability, property damage, theft, alcohol-related incidents, interactions with other participants, and even serious injury or death.
You, and anyone acting on your behalf, hereby release and discharge Events Per Se, its affiliates, sponsors, contractors, employees, volunteers, and participating venues from any liability for losses, claims, or damages — whether due to negligence, gross negligence, breach of duty, or any other cause — that may occur before, during, or after the crawl.
You further agree to indemnify and hold harmless all released parties against any claims, costs, or expenses (including legal fees) that arise as a result of your actions during the event.
By attending, you confirm you have read and understood this waiver, that you are participating voluntarily, and that this agreement is intended to be as broad as allowed by law.
Event Continuity
The crawl will run rain or shine. Venues may change last minute. Always check your email and the digital crawl map for updates.
Acknowledgment
Purchase of a ticket signifies full acceptance of these disclaimers, terms, and conditions. You accept complete responsibility for your participation.

description:
Braai and get together at Battle Hill - 17 January 2026, Braai and get together at Battle Hill - 17 January 2026
How about just a casual get together and braai? The last one we had at Battle Hill was so nice and we met so many new people. Noting that this is weather dependent.

Where:
We have secured "The Dell" area for semi-exclusive use at Battle Hill. (It will be Wellington Anniversary weekend so may be a bit busy.) This means a large section of grass with trees, toilets and the river close by for the kids to play in and see the eels and we can braai in webers, etc. The bigger kids that want to go and swim in the hole can take a walk there. Unfortunately, there are no concrete slabs that we can make fires on so you will need to bring a braai with.

What to bring?
Well, this is not a catered place so you will need to bring the following:
Gazebo
Chairs and/or blankets
Braai and charcoal/wood i.e. weber, etc 😉
There are gas braais as well but you will need to share it with the rest of the people / campers.)
Meat, drinks, salad and water/ drinks (to drink and to K*ll your fire with)
Balls, bats, etc for the kids to play (If someone is keen on arranging games for the day, we would appreciate it!)

There is no cost to us getting semi exclusive use of the area. However, if you want to extend the night and camp, you will need to pay for that.

Please note that this is a working farm area and unfortunately no dogs are allowed AT ALL.

Also note that the gates are locked at 8pm so if you are not camping, please keep this in mind so you can get out in time.

This is an open invitation so no need to RSVP to me but it would be nice to have an indication on how many people will attend by confirming on the event "Going" or "Interested".

I hope to see LOTS of you there!
