description:
Wellington Halloween Bar Crawl 2025 | Saturday, November 1st, Wellington Halloween Bar Crawl 2025 | Saturday, November 1st
Get ready for the biggest Halloween Bar Crawl in Wellington on Saturday, Nov 1st. Hit the city's top bars & clubs!

About this Event


🎃🕸️ WELLINGTON HALLOWEEN BAR CRAWL 2025 🕸️🎃
Get ready for the wildest Halloween celebration in Wellington. Join the spookiest Halloween Bar Crawl on Saturday, November 1st, 2025, and get access to 4+ of Wellington’s hottest bars and nightclubs!
🎶 Music: Live DJs playing the hottest hits and Halloween classics.
📍 Crawl Stops: Lulu Bar & Restaurant, El Horno, The Rogue & Vagabond, and BL3—Wellington's hottest bars and nightclubs for an unforgettable Halloween celebration!
🕙 Kickoff: Doors open at 7:30 PM. Wristbands must be picked up by 10:30 PM!
🎟 Tickets: Starting at just $15. Prices go up soon, so don’t wait!
Throw on your wildest costume and get ready to crawl through the city with hundreds of partygoers. Whether you're spooky, cute, or covered in fake blood—this is the crawl to be at to celebrate Halloween.🧟‍♀️


⭐ Voted Wellington’s #1 Halloween Bar Crawl Experience!
Gather your friends and take over the city with the Halloween Bar Crawl this Halloween, hitting Wellington’s top bars.
🚨 This event is limited to 500 attendees, so grab your tickets early before they sell out! Don’t miss your chance to celebrate this big holiday—secure your spot before it’s too late!





What’s Included in Your Ticket:
🎃 Join Hundreds of Halloween Partygoers in Wellington
🚫 No Cover at All Participating Venues
🎟️ Exclusive Halloween Bar Crawl Wristband for Entry
📱 Bar Crawl Map to Navigate Each Spooky Stop 🗺️
🎵 Live DJs Spinning Halloween Hits and Party Anthems
🕺 End the Night in Style at the Final Haunted Venue
👻 Costume up and crawl your way through Wellington’s hottest bars. Enjoy eerie drink specials, electric energy, and unforgettable Halloween experience!


🚨 Get Your 2025 Halloween Bar Crawl Tickets Before We Sell Out! 🚨
🦇Wellington’s biggest Halloween bash is just around the corner. Costumes, cocktails, and chaos await across the city's top nightlife spots.
It’s time to show off your best look and turn heads all night long. Whether you're spooky, sexy, or just straight-up wild, this year's Halloween Bar Crawl is your chance to shine. Hit the city’s most popular bars, enjoy themed drinks, and party with hundreds of fellow Halloween lovers. Your costume deserves a crowd, and this is where to find it.
🚫🍸🔑 Celebrate Responsibly!
We DO NOT condone drinking and driving. Use Uber, Lyft, get a Taxi, or a designated driver to ensure a safe ride home. Celebrate responsibly and make it a Halloween to remember!
💀 Let the WELLINGTON HALLOWEEN BAR CRAWL begin!


100% CANCELLATION REFUND GUARANTEE
👌 If the event is canceled for any reason, all tickets will be refunded in full! No vouchers, no credits, just your money straight back to the bank!


WRISTBAND PICKUP INSTRUCTIONS & POLICY
Where and When to Pick Up Wristbands
WRISTBAND VENUE:
Lulu Bar & Restaurant
🕒 7:30 PM – 10:30 PM
📍 31 Courtenay Place, Te Aro, Wellington 6011
Details: Please arrive at our first stop to scan your ticket and collect your wristband. You have from 7:30 -10:30 to arrive and pick up your wristband for the night. You can stay at this venue until 10:30PM or explore the other venues.
MIDDLE STOPS:
El Horno
🕒 8:30 PM – 11:30 PM
📍 34 Courtenay Place, Te Aro, Wellington 6011
Details: This is a middle slot—you can visit this venue at your leisure anytime between 8:30 PM and 11:30 PM.
The Rogue & Vagabond
🕒 8:30 PM – 11:30 PM
📍 18 Garrett Street, Te Aro, Wellington 6011
Details: This is a middle slot—you can visit this venue at your leisure anytime between 8:30 PM and 11:30 PM.
FINAL VENUE:
BL3
🕒 11:30 PM – 2:00 AM
📍8 Courtenay Place, Te Aro, Wellington 6011
Details: This is the final stop! Everyone should meet at this final venue at 11:30PM for a massive final Halloween party!


Wristband Policy
This is an open-format crawl. You must start at the first venue to collect your wristband. Once you have your wristband, you can visit any participating venues labeled as “Middle Stops” at your convenience between 8:30 PM and 11:30 PM or stay at the wristband venue until 10:30pm. After 11:30 PM, all participants will meet at the final venue to celebrate at the final party location!
Please remember that you need to collect your wristband at the first stop (wristband venue), as it is required for entry into all venues except the first one. Please also note that wristbands are no longer available after 10:30PM at the first venue. No refunds will be given if you show up late and miss the wristband pick-up window!
This club crawl is a walking bar crawl—all venues are within walking distance of each other. Please plan accordingly for transportation and your walking schedule.
Wristbands for entry will be provided by event staff only. To pick up your wristband, please check in with the event staff at the front reception or host stand.


2025 CRAWL MAP:




🎟️ General Admission – $20
Includes access to all official crawl stops. No frills — just the fun.
───────────────────────────────────────

👑 VIP Ticket – $25
Includes everything in GA plus:
🔥 Access to the VIP-only crawl challenge for free tickets
🚀 First dibs on future crawl drops & pre-sales
───────────────────────────────────────

🤯 Ultra VIP – $35
For the dangerously curious. Includes everything in VIP plus:
🏆 Name on our “Crawl Wall of Fame”
📜 Official “Certified Crawl Legend” digital certificate
🚪 Access to a future exclusive pre-sale drop
❓ A mystery bonus item we legally can’t describe 👀



A SPOOKTACULAR HALLOWEEN PARTY YOU WON'T WANT TO MISS
Skip the overpriced cover charge and long lines at a single bar. With one ticket, you’ll unlock access to multiple Halloween hotspots across Wellington, each with its own theme, music, and party energy. Whether you're rocking a full costume or just out for the night, this is your chance to experience the city’s top nightlife all in one thrilling Halloween themed experience.
THIS IS NOT YOUR AVERAGE HALLOWEEN NIGHT OUT
One bar? One vibe? That’s not how Wellington does Halloween. Grab your crew and bounce between the city’s best bars and clubs, each bringing something different to the table. From spooky cocktails to wild dance floors, the night builds up to one massive end-of-night party you won’t want to miss. 🧛‍♀️
THE BIGGEST HALLOWEEN PARTY NIGHT IN WELLINGTON
This crazy Halloween experience crawl will take you through the city’s top bars and clubs with a line-up built for non-stop fun. Expect packed dance floors, themed drinks, and a crowd ready to party in full costume.
GET YOUR TICKETS BEFORE THEY VANISH!
Spots are filling fast, so now’s the time to lock in your plans. Round up your friends, pick your costume, and get ready to sip, dance, and crawl your way through one of the most exciting nights Wellington has to offer. Halloween only comes once a year... so grab your tickets today! 🎟️



18+ EVENT / GOVERNMENT ISSUED ID REQUIRED


TIPS FOR OUR CRAWL
Pick Up Wristbands On Time: Collect your wristband(s) during the designated check-in time at the scheduled location before 10:30 PM. Late arrivals will not be accommodated — no wristband, no entry.
Assign a Proxy if Needed: Can’t make check-in? Have a friend pick up your wristband for you so they don’t miss out.
Day-Of Updates: Review the Digital Crawl Map on the morning of the event for any last-minute changes or venue updates.
Check Venue Schedule: Each venue’s crawl time is listed in the Digital Crawl Map. Wristbands and drink specials are valid only during these scheduled times.
_______________________________________


IMPORTANT DISCLAIMERS
Age Requirement: You must be 18+ years of age with a valid government-issued ID to participate in this event. No exceptions.
Wristbands: Your wristband is your key to entry. You must present your ticket or QR code at check-in to receive your wristband. Wristbands must be worn at all times to access participating bars and venues. Wristband pickup location and time are listed on the crawl map. If you do not pick up your wristband at the designated location and time, it may not be available, and you will not be able to access crawl venues. No wristband, no entry.
Ticket Policy: All sales are final—no refunds, exchanges, or transfers. In case of event cancellation without a rescheduled date, a refund (excluding fees) will be automatically issued. Event lineup, venues, and specials are subject to change without notice.
Admission & Venue Rights: Participating venues reserve the right to refuse service or entry to any attendee based on intoxication, dress code, inappropriate behaviour, or venue capacity. If refused entry at one venue, you may visit alternative crawl venues available at that time. No refunds will be given if you are denied entry for any of these reasons.
Capacity and Late Entry: Some venues may hit capacity during the event. If a venue reaches capacity or you arrive late, you may have to wait in line to gain entry. There is no guarantee of immediate access or entry to any specific venue once capacity is reached, and no refunds will be provided due to waiting times or access issues.
Self-Guided Crawl: This is a self-guided event with no host leading the group. You must use the digital crawl map to navigate between venues.
Venue Awareness: If bar staff are unaware of the crawl, you must contact the venue representative listed on the crawl map. If you cannot reach them, consider that venue unavailable and proceed to an alternative crawl venue open at that time. Nightlife venues may reach capacity or close early, and not all staff members may be aware of the crawl.
Drink Specials: Drink specials are offered by participating venues and may be modified or discontinued at any time without notice.
Event Time Restrictions: Your ticket is valid only for crawl venues during the scheduled crawl times listed on the crawl map. It is not valid for entry outside these times. If you arrive late or at the wrong venue, refunds will not be issued.
Security Checks: Wristbands do not entitle you to skip venue security checks. All attendees must comply with security screening before entry.
Costume Policy: Do not wear costumes that include items which could be considered or used as weapons. Highly offensive costumes are not tolerated and may result in denial of entry without refund.
Crawl Notifications: Sign up for crawl notification texts to receive updates about when to move to the next venue. There is no host to notify you in person.
Respect & Conduct: Treat others and the event venues with respect. Disorderly conduct, inappropriate behaviour, or damage to the venues will result in removal from the event without a refund. The participating bars reserve the right to refuse entry or remove attendees for inappropriate behaviour at any time.
Liability Waiver: By purchasing a ticket and participating in the crawl, you understand and agree that you are attending entirely at your own risk. You voluntarily assume all risks and dangers incidental to this event, including but not limited to: personal injury, illness, disability, property loss, theft, damage, accidents, death, alcohol-related incidents, and actions of other participants, staff, or third parties. You, on behalf of yourself and your heirs, executors, and assigns, hereby fully release, waive, and discharge Events Per Se, its owners, officers, directors, employees, contractors, volunteers, affiliates, sponsors, participating venues, and their respective representatives from any and all liability, claims, demands, actions, or causes of action whatsoever arising out of or related to any loss, damage, injury, illness, or death — whether caused by negligence, gross negligence, breach of contract, or otherwise — that may occur before, during, or after the event. You agree to indemnify and hold harmless Events Per Se and all released parties from any claims, costs, or expenses (including legal fees) brought by you or on your behalf, or by any third party as a result of your actions during the event. You acknowledge that you have read and understood this waiver, that you are participating voluntarily, and that this waiver is intended to be as broad and inclusive as permitted by applicable law.
Event Changes: Bars and venues may change, but the event will continue rain or shine. Always check the digital crawl map for the latest venue list and updates. Be sure to check your email for any last-minute updates.
*By purchasing a ticket, you confirm that you have read, understood, and agreed to all disclaimers, terms, and conditions stated above, and accept full responsibility for your participation in the event.

description:
Upper Hutt Bike Night - Pack Up & Strap Up!, Upper Hutt Bike Night - Pack Up & Strap Up!
Join us for our first Bike Night for 2026! Summer is in full swing and that means road trips. That also means ferrying your bike if your planning to spend some quality time, wearing down your tyres, on the south island.

We'll be taking you through the must have items you should always take with you on a road trip, aside from the tooth brush and change of under garments.

We'll also go through how to properly strap your bike on the ferry. Remember, if the person next to you seems to be struggling, you better make sure they strap their bike correctly too!

BBQ heats up at 5:30pm, $2 gold coins help us pay for these weekly events. Drinks are available to purchase at the coffee counter.

description:
Saturday coffee ride, Our Saturday morning club ride now offers more options for members.
Starting this week we're trialing a new format to make our rides even more enjoyable for everyone. We'll be splitting into two or three groups based on numbers:
Fast Group

Medium Group

Social Group

No matter your pace, there's a group for you! Each led by a TriWelly committee member. We'll all aim to meet at the same time and place afterward to catch up over coffee.

description:
💃🕺 June Salsa Hutt Dance Party 💃🕺, 💃🕺 June Salsa Hutt Dance Party 💃🕺
May’s Salsa Hutt dance party was great!! 💃🕺😊 For those that missed out last month, and those that can come again this month, place this June party in your calendar:

Come dance the night away with your like-minded friends, and loads of classic Salsa songs from years ago, as well as a “dash” of Bachata and a “sprinkling” of Kizomba…..

• One fantastic wooden dance floor with a mix of Salsa, Bachata, and Kizomba music

• Easy to find venue off a main street in Petone (72 Cuba St)

• Plenty of parking in surrounding streets

• On main bus routes (#83 from Wellington, or #110 from Upper Hutt), or short 8min walk from Ava Train Station

• Water station provided, however bringing your own drink bottle is recommended. Also, BYO soft drinks, fruit juices, NON-alcoholic drinks, etc. (NB/ No alcohol allowed on premises!)

• Please wear high heel/stiletto caps to prevent damage to the venue’s wooden floor – For example, from https://www.beclickless.com/pages/faqs

• Only goes until 10pm so get there early to have a decent "fix" of dancing and socialising 😊

• Only $10.00 at the door (cash), or bank transfer (preferably at least 24 hours in advance):

Account: 06-0507-0092784-05
Account Name: Kasia L Wypych
Particulars/Code/Reference: “Your 1st and last name” - Salsa Hutt – “date of Salsa Hutt party”
******* (If you have prepaid on the night, please take a screenshot and show proof of payment at the door).

• Invite your friends to this event…and see you there! 😊

💃🕺 From 7pm to 10pm

💃🕺 Petone Salvation Army
Corner of Cuba and High Sts, Petone, Lower Hutt (Entrance from High St)

Email Kasia on or ring/text on 021 141 8116 for more details.

description:
Paint and Wine Night in Waikanae Beach - Summer in NZ (first drink included), Paint and Wine Night in Waikanae Beach - Summer in NZ (first drink included)
The concept is simple - take a brush in one hand, a drink in the other and let’s paint a masterpiece!

Join us for a creative night of drinking 🍷 and painting 🎨 in the heart of Waikanae Beach! No previous painting experience necessary. Get your creative juices flowing and your brush strokes going!

Drinks: FIRST DRINK IS INCLUDED IN THE TICKET PRICE

Food: A delicious menu will be available to purchase throughout the event.

What is Paintvine?
It’s a fun social painting class where our artists will run you through step-by-step instructions on how to create your very own masterpiece in a fun and relaxed bar setting. Perfect for dates, catching up with mates or for meeting new friends!

We provide all the materials you need (high-quality canvas, acrylic paint, brushes). All you need to bring is your creative spirit (or find some at the bottom of a glass). At the end of the night you will leave with your own painting ready to hang up on your wall.

For tickets, future events, FAQs and more info head to ➡️ [[ https://paintvine.co.nz/]

description: Wellington Lens Club Fortnightly Meetup, Calling on all photography enthusiasts! Join us this Friday for another fortnightly meetup with the Wellington Lens Club! We gather every second Friday at Heyday Beer Co (264a Cuba Street) 5:30-7pm - if we meet else where it will be announced on the event discussion and in the group. We'll kick off this weeks event with drinks and engaging conversations about camera lenses. Photo walk begins 7pm until 8:30pm, followed by a delicious dinner from 8:30-10pm. This FREE event is open to everyone, especially those interested in vintage lenses and adapting them to modern cameras. Don't miss out!

description:
Let's Sketch Newtown's Hertiage Character, In anticipation of the upcoming Heritage Festival, we will be sketching the heritage character of Newtown. Historic shopfronts on Riddiford Street or character cottages in the side streets - Green Street hosts some of the oldest houses in Newtown, built from 1875.
Meet on the corner of Riddiford Street and Green Street at 10am, sketch for a couple of hours in this area, then meet at New Day Café in Constable Street at 12.30pm for a coffee/chat and to look at everyone's drawings.
Urban Sketchers Wellington will display a collection of Newtown sketches in the SubCulture window (the former substation near McDonald’s) during the Heritage Festival (18 October – 9 November). If you would like contributing work to this display talk to Dave or Annette.
All are welcome. Please bring your own art materials.
label: Hobbies & Interests

description:
Paintvine Uncanvased™- Nude Life Drawing, Undress your creativity in this fun, relaxed and easy to follow beginners nude life drawing workshop.

Over the course of two hours, our expert Paintvine artists will guide you through a series of life drawing exercises designed to introduce you to different methods of drawing, all while you and your friends enjoy a vino or brew. You’ll have access to a wide range of drawing tools and materials to explore your creative side in this experimental workshop.

After a couple of drinks, a few cheeky winks and a good laugh or three you and your friends will bid adieu to your model and head home with a series of unique and fun drawings to remember this creatively curious night out!

This is a popular event, so get your tickets before they sell out!

Drinks & Food

No drinks are included in the ticket price, but drinks and food are available for purchase throughout the event.

What is Paintvine?

It’s a fun social art class where our artists will run you through step-by-step instructions on how to create your very own artwork in a fun and relaxed bar setting. Perfect for dates, catching up with mates or for meeting new friends.

We provide all the materials you need. All you need to bring is your creative spirit (or find some at the bottom of a glass). All our events are in licensed venues, so you can purchase drinks to sip as you create! At the end of the night, you will leave with your own collection of unique drawings.

For tickets, future events, FAQs and more info head to the Paintvine website ⇒ paintvine.co.nz

description:
NZBW After 5 Wellington 15.10.25, Let’s Party !!
BA5 Wednesday 15th October from 5.30pm for a 6pm start
Chamber of Commerce Level 3 / 15 Daly Street Lower Hutt
Come along and receive a complimentary Mocktail and Nibbles on arrival.
The evening includes how to
Make a fabulous Mocktail (the one your drinking)
Make your table and your home stylish for a party OR Christmas Party
Make great your home storage with an amazing range of homewares.
Your lovely hosts are
Andrea from Botanical Homeware
Carol from Foxy Teas
Lyndal from Little Lace Flower Company
 From Andrea
Discover fun and creative ways to use our decorative tins – from stylish storage solutions to thoughtful, unique gifting ideas. I’ll show you how these beautiful tins can be repurposed for every occasion, turning a simple container into a memorable gift or a charming piece for the home. description:
The Feilding Flyer, The Feilding Flyer
Saturday 21 March
Book now: https://fareharbor.com/embeds/book/mainlinesteamheritagetrust/items/?flow=1544292&full-items=yes&back=https://www.mainlinesteam.co.nz/
Spend a relaxed Saturday travelling by heritage steam train to Feilding aboard the Feilding Flyer, enjoying the scenic run through Kāpiti and Horowhenua before arriving around 10:45am.
With a 4:15pm departure home, you’ll have plenty of time to enjoy the day your way.
Station Stops
Plimmerton
Paraparaumu
Ōtaki
Levin
Shannon
Palmerston North
Heading to the Central Districts Field Days?
Field Days is a full day out with plenty to see and do for all ages — and especially fun for kids!
• Interactive displays and hands-on demonstrations
• Machinery of all sizes (big trucks are always a hit!)
• Pet and animal areas to visit
• Kids’ entertainment zones and activities
• Food trucks and tasty treats
• Coffee and beverage vendors
• Outdoor living and lifestyle ideas to explore
Shuttle transfers between Feilding Railway Station and Manfeild Park are included with your train ticket and timed to meet the train, making it easy and stress-free.
Prefer a slower pace?
Feilding itself is well worth exploring.
We recommend a visit to The Coach House Museum for a look at local history and heritage, or enjoy lunch at The Strong Room, a popular local café known for great food and coffee.
You can also browse the boutique shops or simply enjoy the friendly rural town atmosphere.
Onboard the train, you’ll travel in comfortable carriages with large windows, an onboard café, toilets, and an open-air observation carriage — perfect for enjoying the sights, sounds and atmosphere of steam travel
Whether you’re heading to Field Days, exploring Feilding, or simply enjoying a scenic rail journey, the Feilding Flyer offers a classic and relaxed day out for everyone.
Mainline Steam

description:
Bar Crossing Seminar - Manawatū (Foxton bar), Crossing A Bar? We Can Help
Bar crossings can be one of the trickiest, and riskiest parts of boating in Aotearoa. They change fast, they don’t always play nice, and they’ve caught out even the most experienced skippers. Each year, we see serious incidents and tragedies happening on our bars, so it’s super important to know your stuff before you head out.
That’s where we come in. Join us at Manawatu Marine Boating Club for a free and relaxed seminar. You’ll hear straight from local Coastguard skippers and boating legends who’ll share their real-world tips, local knowledge, and a few stories from the water.

Here’s what we’ll cover (and chat about):

Understanding the local features of your bar
Reading and interpreting the conditions when planning a trip
Getting your boat and your crew bar crossing ready
How to handle your boat while crossing
How to do a bar crossing report (and why it’s worth it)
What to do if things go pear-shaped
It’s interactive – so bring your questions, share your own experiences, and have a yarn with others in the boating community.
Bring your mates too – whether they’re brand new to boating or have been around since the days of paper charts and handheld compasses, there’s something in here that could save a trip… or a life.
 Why it matters:
In 2024, 23% of craft-related drownings happened during bar crossings.
5 lives were lost due to capsizing while crossing a bar.
Older blokes are still the most at-risk – but everyone can make safer choices with the right knowledge (and a lifejacket on).

This month, we’re diving into The Story Behind the Brand– because people don’t just connect with what you do, they connect with why you do it. Throughout April, we’ll explore how sharing the human behind your business builds trust, deepens relationships, and turns casual followers into genuine community. Come ready to own your story, speak with confidence, and show up as more than just a business owner– but as the woman behind the brand.
We recommend bringing a notebook and pen- there will be space for reflection, ideas, and those “lightbulb” moments you won’t want to forget.

now and become part of a wonderful community of women in business who are ready to build their dream life and business.
Empower Her is a global female networking group, that offers a welcoming and safe environment for women of all business backgrounds and experiences.
Whether you're just starting out with new business ideas or have years of experience under your belt, all are welcome! Empower Her provides a safe space where you can share your business with others, connect with like-minded women in business, and prioritise collaboration over competition.
Our groups value the power of connection, and we believe that together, we can achieve more than we ever could alone. description:
Optimising value through methodology - A PMO Case Study: Insights from NZ PMO of the Year 2025, Note: This is a Hybrid Event:
In-Person Location: Microsoft, Level 13, 10 Brandon Street, Wellington.
Online: The Zoom link will be sent out the day before the event.
Networking will start at 5.00pm for the in person attendees followed by the presentation starting at 5.30 pm for both in person and online attendees
About this event:
“The New Zealand Traveller Declaration, that international visitors use today, is the product of over 200 dedicated team members working with focus, collaboration, and resilience over two and a half years through much adversity. It is a privilege to present insights from my experience as PMO lead in this remarkable programme team.”

Join Chris Fisher from Nonsuch as he shares personal insights into the award-winning New Zealand Traveller Declaration (NZTD) Programme, recognised as New Zealand PMO of the Year 2025 (and New Zealand Technical Project of the Year 2025)

Chris will reflect on his experience in the NZTD Programme that overcame complex national challenges to deliver a secure, user-centric digital border solution. He’ll unpack how the PMO supported the evolving nature of the programme and tailored methodologies in a hybrid model, blending agile, traditional, and benefits-led approaches to enable rapid delivery, strong governance, and measurable impact.

Chris will also highlight the practical application of frameworks that help power success, including the PMO Value Ring, and share what made the programme stand out in its PMI award application. Expect practical takeaways, lessons learned, and winning strategies that could help your programme, project, or PMO to drive value.

Whether you're leading a transformation programme or evolving your PMO, this session offers valuable insights into how integrated methodology can unlock real-world results.
About the Presenter:
Presenter: Chris Fisher – Head of Consulting, Nonsuch
Join Chris Fisher from Nonsuch as he shares personal insights into the award-winning New Zealand Traveller Declaration (NZTD) Programme, recognised as New Zealand PMO of the Year 2025 (and New Zealand Technical Project of the Year 2025)

description:
Building With AI, Draw the Rest of the Owl
There’s a lot of buzz around AI right now.
Not many honest conversations about what it actually takes to use it in real products.
Most teams can get a demo working.
The hard part is everything that comes after. Reliability, trust, cost, UX, and fitting AI into how teams actually build and ship software.
This meetup is about that gap.
We’re hosting a relaxed, in-person session for people who are past the hype and want to talk about how AI is really being used day to day. Expect practical examples, candid discussion, and time to ask questions rather than polished slides or buzzwords.
Speakers:
Nik Wakelin, Founder of Sterling
Nik will give a genuine look under the hood of Sterling, showing how they’re using AI both in the product and to build it at speed, including the tooling and workflows that are helping them move fast.
Hirtarth Sharma, Founding Engineer at Grw AI
Hirtarth will talk candidly about how they are using AI today, the trade-offs they’ve made, and what’s surprised them along the way.
No buzzwords. No thought leadership. Just builders talking shop.
Details
📍 Talent Army office (with a bar)
🗓 29 January 2026
⏰ Arrive from 4:30pm, 5pm start
🍕 Snacks provided

description:
Leadership And Management Part 1, Learn how to make the transition from buddy to the boss and lead a successful team. Managing staff can be a very rewarding, interesting and fun role, it can also be a tough and demanding job. Between managing your own time and projects, helping your team members solve problems and complete tasks, and helping other managers, your day can fill up before you know it.

This workshop aims to maximise your leadership and management potential and help you become a more confident, effective and efficient leader and manager with information on understanding personality types, effective communication, setting expectations and goals, assigning work, effective delegation, motivating staff, leadership styles, giving feedback and having courageous conversations, understanding what is required to be a good leader and manager and encouraging teamwork.
 
The workshop is designed for those who have been promoted recently into a supervisory management role or for those who have been there for some time but need to improve their leadership and management skills. To book: email - info@biztrainers.co.nz or call 04 974 8790.

Help us celebrate The Wellness Directory’s 16th year anniversary and learn the smarter ways to win clients from your website... without any confusing web jargon and 'tech-aches'!

...If you're struggling to keep your website visitors engaged and generate regular, reliable client bookings then this workshop could be divine timing for your health business. If promoting your services across socials feels like a constant uphill battle and a drain on your time, energy and vibrational then this 16th year workshop is definitely for you!

description:

Are you a small business owner, unsure of where to focus your efforts, possibly feeling overwhelmed by the tasks ahead?
Introducing the 90-Day Business Planning workshop
📅 Wednesday 24/9/25 From 9:00am-1:00pm At 3/15 Daly Street, Lower Hutt
If you’ve been thinking that you need to start working ON your business
(rather than just IN the business), or maybe you are feeling unsure of
where to focus your efforts, or perhaps you are even becoming
overwhelmed by the tasks ahead - You're not alone.
Many entrepreneurs face this struggle.
This lack of direction is not only frustrating - it's a huge roadblock.
Introducing the 90-Day Business Planning Experience
In just half a day, you will be creating and walk out with a ready-to-execute 90-day plan that will give you the clarity and focus you need to move forward with confidence., and then at 30-day and 60-day online follow-up session to keep your progress
What You'll Walk Away With:
A Clear Strategy: Get into the right mindset for success as you connect your passion and goals to a focused 90-day plan that moves your business forward.
An Actionable Plan: Breakdown your big goals into clear, doable weekly steps, making it easy to stay on track and see real progress.
Inspiration & Connection: Learn, share, and grow alongside other business owners, gaining fresh insights, motivation, and a powerful networking opportunity.
Why You Need This Now:
Without a business plan, you are more likely to miss opportunities, waste time, and burn out.
Businesses with formal plans are more likely to succeed and achieve long-term growth.
The time to act is now - this session is your key to getting your business forward successfully.
Stop Going in Circles: 
Invest just half a day, where you’ll be able to create a 90-day business plan that’s ready to execute.
You'll feel empowered to move forward with purpose and clarity as you get to connect with other business owners at the same time.

description:
Putting the 'Where' into decisions: a 40 year journey with the enabling technology named Geographic Information Systems (GIS), During his IT career at Eagle Technology Group in New Zealand, Parker identified a number of software applications to import, sell, and support locally, including the GIS developed by US-based Environmental Research Systems Institute (ESRI).
From the late 1980s, he was involved in many ESRI GIS implementations across central, regional, and local government, DSIR/CRIs, and universities. When Parker retired from Eagle Technology 13 years ago, he founded GIS in Conservation (GiC), a non-profit charitable trust that provides GIS and other IT specialists and software to non-profit conservation and iwi groups at no charge.
In the Wellington region, GiC has supported organisations including Zero Invasive Predators Ltd (ZIP), Zealandia, and Capital Kiwi.
Parker’s talk will present real New Zealand examples showing how GIS has enabled a new way of understanding the world by putting the “where” into data.
This event is supported by voluntary donations. Please consider making a donation when reserving your ticket.
Biography
Parker was raised and worked on his grandparents’ Angus beef and cropping farm in Gloucester, Virginia. In 1976, he graduated from Methodist University, North Carolina, with a Bachelor of Administration, intending to continue towards becoming a Chartered Accountant.
He was instead recruited by Burroughs Corporation to work in IT sales for accounting solutions. Parker emigrated to New Zealand in 1983 and joined Pr1me Computers—now Eagle Technology Ltd—as an Account Manager, where he worked for 29 years until his retirement.
Parker has been a volunteer trapper for 22 years and continues to serve on both the GiC and MIRO committees. At MIRO, he coordinates the protection and study of the endangered Wellington Region banded dotterel population, assists with the little blue penguin project, and manages volunteer trapping at Parangahau Lakes.

description:
90-Day Business Reset - Planning the best way to start the year in your business, 90-Day Business Reset - Planning the best way to start the year in your business
Business isn’t roulette - but sometimes we act like it is

Chasing every “hot opportunity,” hoping for a lucky break,
spinning ideas, feeling the thrill… and the fear.

It’s exciting at first - until it’s exhausting.
The emails, the posts, the pitches…
and still, no guarantee it will work.
Like spinning a roulette wheel,
every outcome feels out of your control.

Well, unlike roulette, your business can be planned, guided, and grown deliberately.

❌ The spin of the wheel? That’s random chance.
✅ The plan? That’s power. Systems, strategy, and focus compound over time. Every choice you make adds up.

You don’t need luck to hit the jackpot -
You need clarity, consistency, and a roadmap that actually works.

The biggest wins come from doing the right things,
in the right order,
over and over again.

👉 Take control of your business before the year ends, and be ready to a highly successful 2026!

This time you have the option – you can join in-person, or online.

The 90-Day Business Planning Workshop is on:

description:
Book Launch - Isaac Featherston 'Petatone': A Colonial Life, Join us next month to launch Isaac Featherston 'Petatone': A Colonial Life by John E Martin. The book will be launched by Charlotte MacDonald.

Tuesday 7 October, 6pm
Unity Books Wellington
All welcome!

'Existing views of Featherston are fragmentary, negative and not very satisfactory. He was the “little Doctor”, a single-minded despot wielding tyrannical power and casting an oppressive shadow. As I began to understand more of his life, I realised that there was little appreciation of a man who had contributed so much.’ —John E.Martin

Isaac Featherston was at the heart of some of the most significant developments in New Zealand from the 1840s colonisation by the New Zealand Company to the abolition of the provinces in 1876. A doctor, newspaper editor and politician, he advocated for the provinces against the domination of central government, while also being primarily responsible for the relocation of the capital from Auckland to Wellington in 1865.

Known amongst Māori as ‘Petatone’ and with a reputation for directness and honesty, he was at the very centre of Pākehā–Māori relations, and in his 1857 official portrait is depicted alongside leading rangatira Te Puni and Wi Tako.

About the author: Dr. John E. Martin has researched and written about New Zealand history since the 1980s, teaching in universities and employed as a historian in the public sector. He was parliamentary historian in the 2000s and 2010s. His publications include rural and labour history, the history of science and engineering, and social and political history. His books include The Forgotten Worker (1990), People, Politics and Power Stations (1991 and 1998), Holding the Balance (1996), The House: New Zealand’s House of Representatives, 1854–2004 (2004), Parliament’s Library (2008), Honouring the Contract (2010), Illuminating Our World (2017), A Colonist’s Gaze (2018) and most recently Empire City: Wellington Becomes the Capital of New Zealand (2022).

Through kōrero and shared storytelling, we’ll unpack how artistic practice can drive systems change and strengthen Lower Hutt's inclusive, culturally rich creative economy, one that reflects indigenous and local voices of communities across Te Awa Kairangi.
Teremoana Rapley MNZM is a pioneering artist, broadcaster, and cultural leader whose work spans music, television, and indigenous creative advocacy. Of Kiribati, Rarotonga, Aitutaki, Mangaia, Cuba, Jamaica, and Suriname descent, she first rose to prominence with Upper Hutt Posse and Moana & the Moahunters. Her practice is rooted in indigenous storytelling, systems change, and mentoring, often acting as a “Matriarchitect” to uplift others. With accolades including the Pacific Music Awards Lifetime Achievement Award and induction into the Aotearoa Music Hall of Fame, Rapley continues to shape Aotearoa’s creative landscape through collaboration, education, and community engagement.
In partnership with Toi Rauwhārangi College of Creative Arts, Massey University.

description:
Navigating the turbulent transition: an international perspective, Navigating the turbulent transition: an international perspective
The world is facing what the head of the IEA has called "the biggest energy security threat in history".

About this Event

The conflict in the Gulf is causing shortages of oil, gas and fertilisers around the world, and the situation could worsen dramatically as countries burn through their reserves. At the same time, we have seen the ending of ever-freer trade, a splintering of international consensus on climate action, and the promotion of geopolitical fragility by the second Trump Administration. Set against this backdrop, however, we are living through explosive growth and plummeting costs in renewable energy, batteries and EVs, and the dawn of a new era of artificial intelligence. What does all this mean for New Zealand? Does the new security and geopolitical environment necessitate a policy U-turn? Or can the needs of climate, economics and energy security be reconciled?
Global energy thought leader Michael Liebreich will share his international insights in Wellington on the morning of Friday 5 June. Michael is the founder of BloombergNEF and Chairman and CEO of Liebreich Associates, as well as being a prolific writer and broadcaster, and host of YouTube channel Cleaning Up. The event is being facilitated by ERGANZ and will be hosted at the Wellington offices of MinterEllisonRuddWatts.
Date and time: Friday 5 June, 7.30am-9.30am. A light breakfast will be served from 7.30am, with Michael's address starting at 8.00am. Please email with any dietary requirements.
There will also be the option to join online if you are unable to make it in person.

description:
UN Women Aotearoa NZ International Women’s Day 2026 Parliamentary Reception, Join UN Women Aotearoa New Zealand at the New Zealand Parliament Buildings for our flagship International Women’s Day 2026 Parliamentary Reception, marking the United Nations’ global observance of International Women’s Day.
This high-impact evening brings together over 220 influential leaders, changemakers, and allies from across business, government, academia, and the community sector to ignite momentum toward a more inclusive and equitable Aotearoa New Zealand.
Held in alignment with the United Nations International Women’s Day theme, this event proudly positions Aotearoa New Zealand at the forefront of global IWD celebrations, contributing to the worldwide call for gender equality and the empowerment of all women and girls.
Why this event matters
In a world facing overlapping crises, from conflict and displacement to economic pressure and the rollback of human rights, gender equality is more urgent than ever. Despite progress, global data shows it may still take up to 123 years to achieve full gender equality without accelerated action.
UN Women is the world’s leading organisation for gender equality and women’s empowerment, working in over 100 countries to advance women’s rights, leadership, safety, and economic participation.
What to expect
This parliamentary reception is designed to both celebrate progress and catalyse action, bringing together leaders and changemakers committed to advancing gender equality.
The evening will feature:
Meaningful connection and conversation over curated refreshments
A keynote address offering insight and inspiration
A moderated panel discussion exploring leadership, lived experience, and pathways for change
Guests will leave inspired, informed, and equipped with perspectives and ideas to help drive meaningful progress within their organisations, communities, and networks.
Your impact
Proceeds from this event support UN Women programmes that accelerate women’s economic empowerment, leadership, and safety, particularly across the Pacific region. By attending, you are standing alongside a global movement working to ensure every woman and girl can live with dignity, opportunity, and choice.
Ticketing
To encourage early commitment, tickets are released in phases:
$85 Super Early Bird tickets are available until 31 January, or until allocation is exhausted
$100 Early Bird pricing applies from 1-16 February
$125 Standard tickets apply from 17 February up to 01 March
We encourage early booking, as capacity is limited.
We warmly invite you to be part of this important United Nations observance and to stand with UN Women in shaping a fairer, safer, and more equal future.

description:
2025 Taiwan Update Lectures, Taiwan’s Multidimensional Energy Security Challenges
Professor Chyungly Lee
A stable and reliable energy supply is essential for a nation’s economic growth and development. In selecting fuel sources, nowadays almost all countries have taken climate change into account, adopted sensible clean energy transition plans, and designed a schedule to meet the goal of Net Zero. While pursuing a 3-E (energy-economic-environmental) balanced energy supply strategy, Taiwan, lacking indigenous resources, faces unique challenges of supply disruptions from over dependence on energy imports and possible blockade, quarantine, or invasion from Beijing. Any disruption of power supply or surge of energy costs could not only threaten Taiwan’s competitive edge in the global high-tech market but also undermine Taiwan’s internal stability and the nation’s survival. In this talk, Dr. Chyungly Lee will first present fact sheets on Taiwan’s energy status, summarize Taiwan’s current energy strategy, and then discuss dilemmas and vulnerabilities that Taiwan might face in the next few years.
What does Taiwan’s two-level politics tell us?
Professor Alex Tan
This presentation examines Taiwan as a critical case study for understanding the evolving challenges to democracy in the 21st century. Arguing that Taiwan serves as a “canary in the coalmine,” the analysis highlights how the island’s early and persistent political polarization—rooted in identity politics and the legacies of authoritarian rule—prefigures trends now visible in established democracies worldwide. The research explores how deep divisions between the Kuomintang (KMT) and Democratic Progressive Party (DPP), reinforced by institutional and social factors, have led to legislative gridlock, declining trust in political institutions, and a “democratic ceiling” that constrains further democratic development. Taiwan’s precarious geopolitical position, facing constant pressure from the People’s Republic of China, intensifies the dilemma of balancing national security with the protection of civil liberties. The presentation analyzes Taiwan’s innovative responses to disinformation, foreign interference, and public health crises, illustrating both the resilience and fragility of democratic systems under external and internal stress.
Gaming Taiwan: Indigenous Epistemologies in Play
Associate Professor Chia-rong Wu
This research explores how Indigenous knowledge systems in Taiwan are being revitalized through video games. Far from mere entertainment, these games draw on tribal traditions, ancestral stories, and deep connections to the land to create meaningful digital experiences. By blending oral storytelling with interactive gameplay, Taiwanese Indigenous game developers are preserving cultural memory while challenging colonial legacies. Titles like Princess Baleng, Tayal, VuVu, and Gadvia show how games can empower communities and pass down wisdom to younger generations in innovative and engaging ways. Gamers not only play a game but also immerse themselves in digital environments shaped by Indigenous perspectives and values. This study encourages all engaged audiences, including educators and students, to consider video games as powerful tools for cultural resurgence and learning. Focusing on Indigenous Taiwan, it highlights how digital games integrate historical consciousness and interactivity to reimagine the past and future of the island.
label: Government & Politics

description:
Gender Attitudes Survey Launch 2025, Kia ora koutou,
The National Council of Women of New Zealand / Te Kaunihera Wāhine o Aotearoa (NCWNZ) warmly invites you to the launch of our Gender Attitudes Survey, sharing the latest perceptions of gender equality in Aotearoa.
Join us on Wednesday, 17 September at the Tiakiwai Conference Centre at the National Library where we will present the key findings from our 5th biannual survey. The Gender Attitudes Survey measures New Zealanders’ views on gender roles in the home, education, workplaces, and in the wider community. We will explore how attitudes have changed since our first baseline survey in 2017, and what these results mean for advancing gender equality in Aotearoa New Zealand.
We will be hosting an amazing group of speakers on the day to talk about the Survey results and what that means for gender equality in New Zealand. More information will be available on this page when speakers are confirmed. Watch the space!
Speakers
Host, Suzanne Manning, President, NCWNZ
Suzanne Manning is the current President of NCWNZ, having been involved for 20 years as a member. She has a background in education and social research, and is passionate about progress towards gender equality.
Keynote speaker: Professor Gail Pacheco
Professor Gail Pacheco is the Equal Employment Opportunities (EEO) Commissioner at the Human Rights Commission. A leading economist, she has previously been the Director of the NZ Policy Research Institute at AUT and a Commissioner at the NZ Productivity Commission. She has an extensive track record of social policy-focused research, often leading large-scale collaborations between academia, community organisations, and the public sector.
Gail has been the recipient of numerous awards. These include: the 2018 NZIER Economics Award for outstanding contributions to the advancement of economics and its applications in NZ; the AUT Medal in 2019 in recognition of her use of integrated data to inform social policy and wellbeing; and most recently, in 2025, she was awarded Life Membership of the New Zealand Association of Economists, an honour reserved for individuals who have made significant contributions to both the association and the economics profession in NZ.
Emanuel Kalafatelis, Research New Zealand
Emanuel has been involved in the social and marketing research industry in Aotearoa New Zealand for over 40 years. For most of those years, he has managed a wholly independent research company, Rangahau Aotearoa Research New Zealand, based in Wellington and mainly servicing clients in the public sector. In addition to the Gender Attitudes Survey, Emanuel has led and been involved in several ‘landmark’ research projects, including projects aimed at measuring New Zealanders’ attitudes to subject topics such as changing the New Zealand flag, New Zealand becoming a republic, the sale of public assets, genetic modification and the impact of COVID-19. Emanuel is a Fellow and current member of the Research Association of New Zealand Inc.
Dellwyn Stewart, CE YWCA Auckland, Board member NCWNZ
Dellwyn Stuart is a leader with a track record of developing and executing transformational strategy, successfully influencing decision makers and stakeholders, and finding solutions in complex, ambiguous environments. Currently as CEO of YWCA, she is passionate about contributing to a future for Aotearoa that is more equitable for all, and finds joy and optimism in her work with young women. Her career spans the commercial and not for profit sectors with a depth of knowledge and experience in philanthropy, fundraising, stakeholder engagement and gender issues.
Youth speaker (name and bio to come)
In-person and virtual attendance
If you’re joining us in person, there will be light refreshments after the launch when you can enjoy conversation with other attendees. Online tickets are also available. Please note that this event will be live streamed from 6pm and recorded for sharing after the launch.
Acknowledgments
The Gender Attitudes Survey and launch are generously supported by the Manatu Wahine Ministry for Women, Rangahau Aotearoa Research New Zealand, YWCA Auckland.

description:
Empowering Aotearoa New Zealand’s Digital Future, You’re invited to the launch of NZTech’s latest landmark report: Empowering Aotearoa New Zealand’s Digital Future – Our National Data Centre Infrastructure.
Data centres are the backbone of our digital lives, powering everything from essential government services and healthcare to banking, e-commerce, and artificial intelligence (AI).  As data centre demand rises, so does the need for a resilient and sustainable energy supply. This sector is playing a pivotal role in New Zealand’s transition to a low-carbon future and helping to future-proof the electricity grid.
Discover what’s really happening with New Zealand’s booming data centre sector and what it means for our economy, communities and our digital future. Meet the data centre operators and electricity providers driving this transformation, and hear directly from Hon Simon Watts, Minister for Energy and Climate Change, and Graeme Muller, CEO, NZTech.
 Join us and find out:
how data centres power our everyday lives and the scale of New Zealand’s data centre sector
how the sector is fuelling economic growth and generating thousands of jobs
why New Zealand’s data centres are global leaders in energy efficiency and driving investment in new wind, solar and geothermal projects
how New Zealand is uniquely positioned to become a regional hub for sustainable digital exports.
the importance of collaboration between government and industry to ensure renewable energy supply can keep pace with digital growth. 
Learn more about how the digital economy and energy sector are working together to unlock new value for all New Zealanders, ensuring our country thrives in a rapidly evolving digital world.

description:
Singapore and the Indo-Pacific in an Age of Geopolitical Competition, Join the Asia New Zealand Foundation and the Centre for Strategic Studies for a discussion with Professor Joseph Liow, Dean and Wang Gungwu Chair Professor of East Asian Studies at the Lee Kuan Yew School of Public Policy. Professor Liow will be in conversation with Professor David Capie, Director of the Centre for Strategic Studies.
As US-China rivalry deepens, Asia’s strategic landscape is shifting rapidly. In this discussion, Professor Liow and Professor Capie will explore what great-power competition means for the region, how Singapore manages its relationships with both Washington and Beijing, and ASEAN’s evolving role in maintaining peace.
Event Details
Date/time: Tuesday, 25 November 2025, 5:30pm - 7:00pm
Venue: RHLT2, Ground Floor, Rutherford House, 33 Bunny Street, Pipitea, Wellington 6011
This is an open event, so feel free to bring a friend! Please RSVP for catering purposes.
About Professor Joseph Liow
Professor Joseph Liow (Joseph Chinyong Liow) is Dean of the Lee Kuan Yew School of Public Policy and Wang Gungwu Professor in East Asian Affairs, National University of Singapore and concurrently, Chairman of the Middle East Institute at the same university. He was previously Dean of College of Humanities, Arts, and Social Sciences, Nanyang Technological University, Singapore, where he was also Tan Kah Kee Chair in Comparative and International Politics, and Professor and Dean at the S. Rajaratnam School of International Studies. He held the inaugural Lee Kuan Yew Chair in Southeast Asia Studies at the Brookings Institution, Washington DC, where he was also a Senior Fellow in the Foreign Policy Program.
 About Professor David Capie
Professor David Capie is Director of the Centre for Strategic Studies and Professor of International Relations. He is also a Trustee of the Asia New Zealand Foundation. David's research interests focus on conflict and security issues, particularly in the Asia–Pacific, and New Zealand's foreign and defence policy. He has authored or co-authored three books and numerous articles and book chapters. His research has been supported by the Rockefeller Foundation, the East-West Center and the Royal Society of New Zealand’s Marsden Fund. David has held visiting positions at the Weatherhead Center for International Affairs at Harvard University and at LUIS Guido Carli in Rome. He was regional co-chair of the Council for Security Cooperation in the Asia-Pacific (CSCAP) from 2020 until 2022 and was a member of the ASEAN Regional Forum Experts and Eminent Persons Group from 2012 to 2019.

description:
Connecting Aotearoa Summit 2026, We’re excited to announce the Connecting Aotearoa Summit 2026—the premier event for shaping New Zealand’s digital future. We launched the expanded event last year to ensure the issues we cover are relevant and pertinent to the nation’s most pressing challenges. In 2026, this is more critical than ever, as the general election looms. We need to make digital connectivity and equity front-and-centre of political debate, economic prosperity, and social wellbeing. The decisions made this year will lock in New Zealand’s digital trajectory for the rest of the decade.

This year, we are proposing that the Summit tackles the crucial question: “How do we get everyone connected to the best broadband service that meets their needs to live and thrive in our digital future by 2030?”

This ambitious national goal demands immediate, coordinated action from policymakers, industry, and community leaders. Join us as we delve into the critical issues surrounding digital connectivity in Aotearoa, directly influencing the next government’s mandate. At this early stage, we are proposing that our sub-themes include:

Policy & Political Mandate: Developing the definitive roadmap for universal connectivity ahead of the 2026 election, securing cross-party commitment to the 2030 target.

Rural Connectivity: Bridging the digital divide and ensuring resilient, future-proof access for all, regardless of location.

Affordability & Digital Equity: Examining the total cost barriers (device, data, skills) to internet access and advancing practical solutions for true inclusion.

Future-Proofing Connectivity: Looking ahead to emerging technologies and innovative, sustainable solutions that will make the 2030 target a reality.

These are all critical to us meeting our vision that by 2030, everyone in Aotearoa is connected to the best broadband service that meets their needs. This prospectus outlines how you can partner with this Summit to influence the national agenda and help us address the question around how we connect all of Aotearoa.
The Minister for Media and Communications, Hon Paul Goldsmith, is confirmed to present the closing Ministerial address.
Platinum Partner for 2026:

description:
Accountability in War: International Justice and Accountability for Ukraine, Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine has posed an unprecedented challenge to the international legal order — raising fundamental questions about accountability, reparations, and the enforcement of international law.
This expert roundtable brings together three visiting Ukrainian legal and civil-society practitioners for a focused discussion on the international justice mechanisms being developed in response to Russia’s aggression, and the role that partners such as New Zealand can play in supporting them.
The discussion will explore recent progress on:
the international compensation mechanism for Ukraine, including the Register of Damage and the newly established International Claims Commission;
ongoing efforts to ensure individual accountability for the crime of aggression, including the establishment of a Special Tribunal;
the broader international response grounded in the rules-based international order.
The roundtable forms part of the Oceania Fora on International Justice and Accountability, and is organised in collaboration with the Embassy of Ukraine in Australia, during the Ukrainian delegation’s visit to Australia and New Zealand in February 2026.
Event details
Date: Tuesday 17 February 2026
Time: 2:00 – 3:30pm
Venue: The Wellington Club
Format: Roundtable discussion
Afternoon tea: 3:30–4:30pm, following the roundtable.
Places are limited. Register if you wish to attend.

description:
Putin’s Russia and Southeast Asia: The Kremlin’s Pivot to Asia and the Impact of the Russia-Ukraine War, Dr Ian Storey will discuss his new book on Russia’s relations with Southeast Asia. The first major study of Russia and Southeast Asia since the end of the Cold War, it traces the dramatic shifts in Moscow’s relations with the region since President Vladimir Putin took power in 2000. Dr Storey assesses the legacy of the Soviet Union, charts the evolution of Putin’s foreign policy and identifies the drivers behind the Kremlin’s ‘Turn to the East’ or Asia pivot. His book also examines Russia’s dialogue partnership with the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) and the development of Moscow’s political, economic and military ties with each of the 11 regional states over the past 25 years. A large part of the book is devoted to Southeast Asia’s responses to Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine in February 2022. At the end of the talk Dr Storey will also provide an update of Russia-Southeast Asia relations in 2025 and how the region views the on-going Russia-Ukraine War.
Dr Ian Storey is a Senior Fellow at the ISEAS-Yusof Ishak Institute, Singapore. He specializes in regional security issues, focusing on Southeast Asia’s relations with the major powers and maritime security, especially the South China Sea dispute. Since joining ISEAS in 2007 he has been the editor of the ISEAS academic journal Contemporary Southeast Asia. Prior to ISEAS, Ian held academic positions at the Asia-Pacific Center for Security Studies, Hawaii and at Deakin University, Australia. Ian received his PhD from the City University of Hong Kong, his Master’s degree from the International University of Japan and his BA (Hons) from the University of Hull, England. He is the author of Putin’s Russia and Southeast Asia: The Kremlin’s Pivot to Asia and the Impact of the Russia-Ukraine War (2025) and Southeast Asia and the Rise of China: The Search for Security (2011)

description:
Gone By Lunchtime: 10th Birthday Party, Three elections, five prime ministers, several hundred emotional junior staffers and 10 years ago, the podcast Gone By Lunchtime was born screaming into the political world.
Toby Manhire, Annabelle Lee-Mather and Ben Thomas have since then been huddling together to make sense and nonsense of political matters in Aotearoa. Join them on an odyssey down memory lane, recalling their own journeys to fronting New Zealand’s Only Podcast™ taking in everything from Jacindamania to suburban pigeons, from crown-Māori relations to the Bad Boys of Brexit, from coalition building to piss-filled Zorbs.
All that, plus a surprise guest, and we'll exclusively reveal precisely how election year is going to pan out.
-
Toby Manhire is an Auckland-based writer, editor and broadcaster. He is editor-at-large at The Spinoff and host of podcasts including Juggernaut 2: The Story of the Fourth National Government and Gone By Lunchtime.
Annabelle Lee-Mather (Ngāi Tahu, Ngāti Kahungunu, Ngāti Māmoe) is a journalist and producer who’s spent the last 25 years working in media, mostly by saying yes to things before thinking them through. She created The Hui, The Casketeers, Hongi To Hāngī and a bunch of other shows her kids refuse to watch.
Annabelle now runs Aotearoa Media Collective with her work wife Mihingarangi Forbes producing the long form current affairs series Mata Reports and the NZ Wars series. She sings the high part in the Gone By Lunchtime trio, slightly off-key, but with conviction.
Ben Thomas is a political commentator and director of Capital Government Relations and Communications, a Wellington and Auckland based public affairs firm. He is a former political editor of the National Business Review and was a press secretary in the previous National government. He writes a regular column for The Post and Stuff.

description:
Wellington Before9: Responsible AI - have we figured out what that means in practice?, Join us for "Responsible AI - have we figured out what that means in practice?," an engaging Before9 (Breakfast event) discussing the latest strategic direction of the technology in Aotearoa New Zealand.
New Zealand's first national AI strategy has arrived, aiming to boost productivity and encourage the uptake of AI "with confidence." But as we move forward, a critical question remains: what does "responsible AI" actually look like in practice for businesses and organisations in Aotearoa? This panel discussion will delve into the nuances of the government's new strategy, exploring its impact on our collective journey. Our panel will unpack the challenges and opportunities presented by a "light-touch" regulatory approach and discuss how we can build a uniquely Kiwi framework for responsible AI in New Zealand.
Our panel will be made up of the following individuals:
Zoe Udy, Deputy Chair at TUANZ
Sasha Vlassoff, Senior Policy Advisor at InternetNZ
Plus one other
Zoe in particular will be able to give an update from our meeting with the Parliamentary cross-party caucus on AI.
We look forward to hosting you at this Before9.  This event is free for members of InternetNZ and TUANZ.  This is a number limited event to 30 individuals so make sure you register early.
Please note the formalities will start at 7:45am but you can arrive any time after 7:30am.

description:
Public Lecture – Amnesty International Secretary General Dr Agnès Callamard, Public Lecture – Amnesty International Secretary General Dr Agnès Callamard
Global human rights leader Dr Agnès Callamard speaks on humanity, justice, and hope in action. Don’t miss this rare event.

Dr Agnès Callamard is recognised globally as a leading authority on governance, accountability, and international affairs. She has been Secretary General of Amnesty International since 2021, previously served as the UN Special Rapporteur on extrajudicial executions, and was Director of Columbia University’s Global Freedom of Expression project. Her career spans decades of leadership in defending human rights worldwide.
In this public lecture, Dr Callamard will reflect on today’s global challenges and opportunities, exploring how collective action can drive change.

Jonathon Boston ONZM, Emeritus Professor of Public Policy at the Wellington School of Business and Government, and Former Director of the Institute of Policy Studies and the Institute for Governance & Policy Studies will host the session.
This event offers a unique opportunity to engage with global human rights discourse in Aotearoa New Zealand at a time when representation, civic freedoms, and the international rules-based order are being widely debated. It also aims to inspire the next generation of change-makers to connect with both local and global struggles for justice.
Event Details
Date & Time: Tuesday 23rd October, 5:00pm doors with lecture starting at 5:30pm
Venue: Rutherford Theatre, Victoria University of Wellington
Who Should Attend? Students, faculty, civil society leaders, activists, and members of the public interested in human rights, justice, and accountability
Accessibility: The venue is wheelchair accessible
Seats Available: 150 seats open to the public (first come, first served)

description:
Peace in Ukraine?, Almost four years since Russia invaded Ukraine in February 2022, the New Zealand Government continues to regard Russia’s action as an "illegal and unprovoked war of aggression", which breaches international law and the UN Charter. As well as joining General Assembly votes condemning Russia, New Zealand has imposed numerous sanctions on Russia and provided various forms of assistance to Ukraine.
In recent weeks, proposals for peace in Ukraine have received much attention. It remains unclear if these will end in a peace with justice for Ukraine, an imposed peace without justice or continuation of a costly war.
This presentation offers the opportunity to hear an up-to-date assessment of the prospects for peace by Ukraine’s Ambassador to New Zealand, His Excellency Vasyl Myroshnychenko.
 Vasyl Myroshnychenko was appointed Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary of Ukraine to Australia and New Zealand in March 2022. Prior to joining the Foreign Ministry, the Ambassador was a founder and partner at CFC Consulting, advising companies on corporate and investor relations as well as business and governmental interaction. He graduated from the Institute of International Relations at the Taras Shevchenko National University, as well as studying at the London School of Economics and Political Science, and at Lehigh University in the United States.

description:
50yrs Misuse of Drugs Act: What impact & what next?, The 1975 Misuse of Drugs Act (MoDA) has failed to deliver. Data from the United Nations Office for Drugs and Crime indicates the demand for drugs is greater than ever, while the supply of drugs knows no bounds. The failure and harm caused by the so called 'War on Drugs' is acknowledged globally and witnessed first hand here in Aotearoa New Zealand.

Back in 2011 a comprehensive review of the MoDA conducted by New Zealand Law Commission recommended the MoDA “should be repealed and replaced by a new Act …administered by the Ministry of Health”. HRCA wants to see that recommendation enacted.

New Zealand has a history for bold world leading policy reform: Woman’s Right to Vote; Decriminalisation of Sex Work; National Needle Exchange, Same Sex Marriage and most recently Drug Checking.

This half day Symposium will provide clear evidence why major law reform is necessary and detail the foundations upon which Aotearoa NZ can once again be innovative and lead the way with new drug laws based on evidence, research and experience -providing a safer environment for future generation.

Harm Reduction Coalition Aotearoa are delighted to have assembled a group of respected independent thinkers with vast experience of drug policy in Aotearoa and beyond, to reflect on the impact of the MoDA and to identify the principles and values needed to underpin new drug laws.

Warren Young: Prof. of Law & Former Deputy President New Zealand Law Commission.
Kirsten Gibson: Chair of Harm Reduction Coalition Aotearoa and Qualitative Researcher.
Fiona Hutton: Associate Prof. Institute of Criminology, Victoria University of Wellington.
Adam Dorsett: 40 yrs Living/Lived Experience of Prohibited Drug Use, 25yrs in Prison.
Julian Buchanan: Former UNODC Advisor and Criminology Professor.

description:
WOMAN LIFE FREEDOM, 
Crows Feet Dance Collective and Iranian Solidarity Group New Zealand present WOMAN, LIFE, FREEDOM a free outdoor dance & music protest event. We come together
to raise awareness about women's and human rights violations in Iran, to stand in solidarity with Iranians resisting authoritarianism, gender apartheid, and state violence. Through public protests, cultural events, exhibitions, and advocacy, the Iranian Solidarity Group New Zealand aims to amplify Iranian, in particular women's voices and support political prisoners.
WOMAN, LIFE, FREEDOM is the second collaboration between Crows Feet and Iranian Solidarity Group, and follows the critically acclaimed performance at the Hannah Playhouse in 2024 where Crows Feet performed WLF, a full length work to Goretsky's Symphony of Sorrowful Songs. In this new 2026 outdoor event we perform with the Iranian Solidarity Group New Zealand to contemporary Iranian protest songs.
Crows Feet director, Jan Bolwell, says "we are thrilled to be presenting this free outdoor event with Iranian Solidarity Group New Zealand. We believe in the power of the arts to expose and comment upon injustices in the world - indeed, we see it as our responsibility, living as we do in Aotearoa New Zealand, in a free democratic society. The bond we have formed with Wellington's Iranian community is a very special one, and the Crows Feet dancers lives have been enriched through this partnership. We hope to attract a good sized audience to our outdoor performances. And may the weather gods be with us!!
label: Government & Politics

description:
Connected Theatres or Fractured World? Emerging Threats and Collective Responses Across the Euro-Atlantic and Indo-Pacific, Since Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine in 2022 it has become clear that threats in the Euro-Atlantic region and the Indo-Pacific are profoundly inter-connected. North Korean troops and weapons have helped Russia’s war effort and Chinese technologies have sustained its military industrial base. Ships registered in the Pacific have been part of Russia’s sanctions-busting ‘shadow fleet’ and implicated in cable-cutting activities in the Baltic Sea. Conversely, Russia has transferred advanced weapons technologies to North Korea, and lessons from the war in Ukraine have implications for Indo-Pacific flashpoints, including across the Taiwan Strait. But if cross-theatre links are clear, forging collective responses has become much more complicated. The recent US National Defence Strategy points to a more limited US role in Europe and expectations that in the Indo-Pacific allies and partners will have to do more. International order is in flux. What does all this mean for New Zealand, for our region, for partners, and for global security? 
Confirmed speakers:
Prof Nick Bisley (LaTrobe University, Melbourne)
Prof David Capie (Centre for Strategic Studies)
Ms Mirna Galic (Center for Strategic and International Studies, Washington)
Prof Beth Greener (Massey University)
Dr Saeme Kim (Asan Institute for Policy Studies, Seoul)
Major General Rose King (Chief of Army, NZDF)
A/Prof Nicholas Khoo (University of Otago)
Dr Julia MacDonald (Centre for Strategic Studies Senior Fellow)
Ms Jennifer Parker (National Security College, Canberra)
Dr Philip Shetler-Jones (RUSI, London)
Prof Alex Tan (University of Canterbury)
Lieutenant General Michael Wright (Commander of the Canadian Army)
A/Prof Jason Young (New Zealand Contemporary China Research Centre)
Registration fee includes lunch, morning/afternoon tea and reception.
Subsidised student tickets are available. Please submit your expression of interest to Mengdi Zhang (mengdi.zhang@vuw.ac.nz) if you would like to be considered.
With the generous support of

Except for the mayoral debate, which will be a facilitated debate dedicated to mayoral candidates, our Meet the Candidate events will involve inviting all candidates running for Mayor, districtwide Councillor, Māori Ward Councillor, and Ward Councillor and Community Board Member positions for that ward to attend.

They will each be provided a dedicated space for them to present their candidacy and have conversations with the community who can attend at any time during the two-hour slots.

Meet the Candidates events help voters:
🔹 meet and hear from candidates
🔹 learn where they stand on issues affecting our District
🔹 make informed decisions when voting in the local elections 2025.

description:
Together in Dialogue: Te Tiriti o Waitangi and Pacific Partnerships, Talanoa Tai Learning is a new collection of learning experiences. They're designed to help you build your cultural responsiveness and improve your ability to engage meaningfully with Pacific communities. Talanoa is a Pacific style of deep dialogue, with a focus on how we connect to each other and strengthen relationships, while still being able to share honest and effective insights. If you want to strengthen your capability in this space, to create transformational change, and to enhance your relationships with people from different communities - this workshop is your chance to grow.
What will I gain from Together in Dialogue?
In Together in Dialogue, you will share meaningful conversations around Te Tiriti o Waitangi and the role and responsibilities of Pasifika as allies. By engaging in deep talanoa, you will explore cultural, historical, and contemporary implications of Te Tiriti o Waitangi and how Pasifika people can strengthen this unique relationship and promote cultural awareness.
This is a 3.5 hour in-person learning experience. The day will begin with a Pōwhiri, to welcome you into the space.
Who is this workshop for?
These learning experiences are for both Paciﬁc professionals and those from other communities. You don’t need to have prior knowledge or experience in this area - we’ll meet you where you are and provide a welcoming, supportive environment for you to learn.
Cultural responsiveness is for everyone. Whether you’re in social work, education, government, community sectors or beyond, these learning experiences have been designed to help everyone build their skills. If you think this professional development might be right for you, it is!
Cost
When work is busy and budgets are tight, cultural responsiveness can often take a backseat. But this is an aﬀordable investment that will help you address the challenges that so many of us share, like how to build engagement and trust, and avoid relationship missteps. It’s not a buzzword; it’s a vital skill if you want to better meet the needs of the people you serve. Price: $170 (inc GST) per person, including morning tea.
Facilitator
Our team’s extensive research and experience feed into these sessions, with a focus on interactive activities and practical, real-life examples. Our team has decades of experience across a range of ﬁelds, in particular social development, education, and research. We have worked with organisations and businesses across the Paciﬁc, and our engagement with diﬀerent Paciﬁc languages, cultures, and communities means we oﬀer robust, informed perspectives. Fa’aeafale’upolu (Polu) Samuelu is the facilitator of this Talanoa Tai Learning experience.

description:
It's in the Ballot - CDC – Carterton District Mayoral Debate -In-studio, Contestants of 'It's in the Bag' become political candidates in 'It's in the Ballot' Meet the Candidates.
Candidates choose a numbered box which has a question that has been submitted online by voters before the show.
It’s in the Ballot” is a community-led awareness and participation initiative. We aim to increase voter interest in the general and council elections nationally.
By using broadcasting and web-based technologies to deliver in-studio; online on Facebook, YouTube and Twitch; and edited as YouTube videos we aim to enable voters to make informed voting decisions.
This means you can send the weblink of the YouTube Video of your electorate show to your friends and whanau to encourage them to watch the show before they cast their votes.
Voting papers are sent to voters on Tuesday 9th September 2025 and must be delivered into a voting dropbox by 12pm Saturday 11th October 2025

description:
Your City. Your Voice. – Ethnic Communities Forum 2025, Join us for Your City. Your Voice. – a community-led forum designed by and for Wellington’s ethnic communities.
Saturday 23 August
Wellington Indian Association, 48 Kemp Street, Kilbirnie.
2pm to 4pm
Doors open from 1.30pm
This special event brings people together to kōrero about the issues that matter most in our neighbourhoods — and how we can shape the future of our city, together.
What’s on?
Housing – Updates and support
City Safety – Learn about new initiatives and share your ideas for safer neighbourhoods
Small Business – Hear about tips, tools and opportunities
City Revitalisation – Updates on upcoming major city projects and what they mean for our communities
Family-friendly
Delicious kai provided to share and enjoy together.
All voices welcome
The capacity for this venue is 150 people, and the doors will close for entry once that capacity is reached.
Whether you’re new to Wellington or a long-time local, we’d love to see you there.
This is a free event - No need to register — just show up, bring your whānau, and take part.
Be heard. Be seen. Help shape Pōneke
Contact: Engagement@wcc.govt.nz for any questions
